I have a different perspective to Jan about losing weight. From mine, I say give it time. The more screwed up your metabolism when you start, the longer it takes to start. When I understood the principles of Jack's teaching at the start of my journey I knew that from my starting point, and at my age, that I wasn't going to heal immediately. I don't believe losing weight _healthily_ can start until the circadian rhythm is working well on an ongoing basis, reading both the signals from the body at that moment in time, and from the seasonal signals, predicting future abundance or lack of it. A mammal's reproductive cycle is geared to the seasonal light cycle.

Anyone can lose weight by not eating at all, or IFing, or following a religious fasting pattern originating from a particular latitude, or however they do it. But the question is - should they? If the circadian cycle is healthy, leptin receptors are operating properly, and its done in line with the seasonal food cycles, the results might be good, particularly for a male. But does that make it right for a female? Done when the brain's leptin receptors can't get the signal, that same regime is the signalling equivalent of a famine, and - like any mammal - your hormonal system will respond accordingly. Females can't outsmart their fat cells ....Nature has built female fat cells differently because females are what carry the next generation....They are operating on the environment they have experienced in the past season, on the information they are getting in the moment, and from the light cycle in their particular latitude, can forecast future abundance that will enable them to put on any weight necessary to carry them - and any offspring - through a winter. Wherever mammals live on the planet, they need the signals from their environment to create the right physiology for reproduction. In a high quantum yield (say nearer the equator), an animal will not need to store fat in the same way as one nearer the poles.

There is a maximum number of births a female mammal can have during their reproductive lifetime, assuming the environmental conditions are right to reproduce every season. For males its different, which is why I think men's fat cells operate differently. In evolutionary terms in a long term famine, male mammals don't have a female's fat system, so are more likely to die first. But of those that survive, one male can father offspring with a lot of females.....

My advice is make friends with your fat cells ..... and give them the environmental signals available at your latitude. As your circadian rhythm and leptin sensitivity improves, I think you will find that body composition improves gradually over time, even if the scale doesn't move. (More muscle? Less fat?) Your leptin receptors will get the message that actually you are holding too much fat for this time of year at your latitude, and your fat cells will respond accordingly. Just a different perspective ....

Chlorophyll a and b work together because they have an overlapped absorption spectra. The same thing is true for the respiratory proteins. People do not see nature's design because they are ignorant of light's effects on living systems. The picture below shows you that chlorophyll a absorbs most in the purple and red light regions of the visible spectrum. This is UV and IR light. Chlorophyll b is shifted to the right in the UVA and blue range and into green and yellow.

This resonance is what makes energy transfer possible. So when the 100 Hz vibration is absent electron transfer suffers. This lowers the amount of ATP that can be made from light and cells seek other sources of electrons from foods. This is the quantum basis of obesity from a quantum perspective. > I paid the consultation fee and still she did not agree to order the test .
> I am really not sure how do I do it.

Hi Nija,
The best tool in my fat-burning toolbox has been intermittent fasting with x2 big meals per day. I start my day with about 40g+ of protein...usually something like 4 pastured eggs with some seafood. Then I have a very late lunch with another large meal... I typically go from 2pm to 7am the next morning for a fast. Some women that have tried this have bad results for whatever individual reasons they have found. Some have required more acclimation to a longer fast.

I'll generally get 5 days of intermittent fasting in a week. At my age of 47, if I don't do at least 4 days of week of fasting, it's very hard for me to shed any fat I want gone. Like many of us, I was once 280 pounds, and I'm 180 today, but I have been doing some biohacks to increase my bodyfat at the moment... I should be at 160 pound optimally and was that way last year. I always include an avocado in my last meal of the day to keep my potassium levels optimal during the 18 hour fasting time. The avocado helps to promote a bit more of a fat-burn about 12+ hours into the fast. I also try to minimize sugars at the last meal of the day so I don't get hungry later in the day and it also helps to promote fat-burning after insulin baselines.

I also incorporate a bit of lighter, anaerobic exercise with leg lifts, dumbbells, squats, etc. I like to do it in the evening around 4 to 7pm a few hours after that last meal at 2pm... this helps insulin get baselined enough in case the last meal pushed it up (like too much protein). I've found even 20 grams of protein at that last meal can trigger my hunger depending on the added carbs (not enough fiber to slow down the GI response) at the meal. Plus in the evening before bed, the lack of fuel in the stomach really helps me to get a solid sleep with blue-blocking. The lack of energy promotes some CT in the evening naturally because there's less thermogenesis in the body from the missing food, and often do face-plunges or splashes if I don't' take a cold shower...

Intermittent fasting is my best tool over several weeks to really target fat from the body from what I've found personally. I've also weaved in ketosis for about 7 years now. The synergy also works extremely well, but often too good if I push the blood ketones too high. So I've found that in the spring/summer/fall, I like to do more intermittent fasting with some carbs as they come into season, then push keto all the way in the winter and limit the fasting. However, if I'm looking to lose more bodyfat, I know I have both tools in my toolbox I can do at the same time for a powerful 1+2 combo...

### caroline — 2017-05-07T05:59:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-213572>

Thanks Scompy for telling us how you manage to keep the extra pounds away. It seems so much harder for women to figure this out ....at least it is for me.

So many women here are struggling with getting their weight down and then keeping it down. Maybe we want the extra pounds gone now?
Men generally seem to shed extra weight pretty fast on the leptin RX ......women - not so much altho our bulk does go down.

Scompy - what are your thoughts on a BPC?

I find if I have a BPC in the morning and then one at 12:30 and then lunch at 3:30 - 4:00pm I do pretty well. I do think that I have to watch the fat intake with the BPC - I think that messes me up.

It is such a fine line ......I don't want too much fat, I don't want too much protein, I do better with no fruit \[I love fruit\] and some veg don't agree with me - like broccoli etc.

and then ..... throw in for good measure .....all the yummy cooking I do for my partner! He just naturally uses food for fuel. He is very active and his body works so well so he needs a really good meal for fuel ....then he digests it - works hard ...and then needs more fuel!

He cut out sugar and starting eating way better meals .... and has lost 10K. He is eating a lot more good fats and often his weight drops by a couple of K. - and then I worry he is losing too much. Tony can even have the odd cookie ....he does spend a lot of the day outside and does stretches on the wet grass. Tony is a walking advert for Jack and the three legged stool!

I hope other women here will share their experiences ......... the good, the bad and the ugly so we can all learn from each other.

I've watched a lot of Stephanie "The Business" Person on youtube for many years. She's 49 years old and has a body of a 20 year old from the ketosis she's been doing for the past 10 years. She's also had terrible injuries in the past with her knees so she does mostly upperbody work. Stephanie also has a lot of advice for women.

She's more of a year-long advocate of ketosis, but she also gets outside a great deal and knows exactly what to eat and what to avoid to spike insulin. She has her circadian rhythm DOWN, and has advocated (in many past videos) that people will stall trying to lose fat and get ripped if they miss that element. She puts Chronobiology first in her routine or it bumps her out of being ketotic. I may have to weave in some ketosis now that my hack is done to get back down to 160 by summer, but then I'll still eat lower sugar, lower GI foods when they come into season.

I was looking back at the original leptin RX blogs this morning, and one thing that struck me was that since they were originally written the nnemf and blue light problem has escalated much further .... and women are naturally more environmentally sensitive. I think women tying in eliminating as much as they can and mitigating more could make the difference between success or failure, both in terms of weight control, and prior to menopause, getting the environment right to be able to conceive and have healthy children if they choose to. I think the quiz in Reality 5 can be a game changer for helping women using the leptin RX . But from one of the blogs: "IFing is worthless in LR states". Add in a bad environment and the result is .......? Already stressed cells and a starvation signal? Worse for women because they are already more environmentally sensitive than men?

Also if only 1/3 of the electrons come from food, then if I can't get the 2/3 from the natural environment for any reason, missing or reducing the 1/3 doesn't do me any favours. For instance if I had to work all day in a nnemf hell and IF'd, for me as a woman it would be a recipe for a disaster. Yes I could IF and lose weight faster, but at what cost? What if I was also leptin resistant? Being post menopausal makes it more difficult to lose weight, but whatever the short term gain in terms of weight loss, what would 10 years of permanent ketosis do to my mitochondria in the long term when I live in an area with definite seasons? I'm nearly 60, and I'm not interested in aiming for a body of a woman 30 years younger, that's façade stuff ... My brain and heart mitochondria are more important, if weight loss takes longer so be it.

### JanSz — 2017-05-09T00:34:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-213697>

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=213684)
>
> yesterday I have spent 4 hours walking in sea beach .I have felt amazing and with lot of energy .
> It was very warm and felt so much rejuvenated . I would like to know -- what is the best time to be in when UV index is low or high .
> I try to in sun morning sunrise when UV index is less than 3 . I am california , San francisco(CA 94404) where UV index is 6 already by 9 -10 am .
> Please suggest the timing for light for better effetive

If your time on the sun is limited,
best time is two hours before and two hours after solar noon.

The thing is about solar noon.
I summer, when we have Daylight Saving Time (DST)
clocks are moved one hour ahead (of solar noon)
So, when we are under DST the best time is 11AM-3PM
other times 10AM-2PM

...........................
Important.
Skin must be hardened to accept prolonged sun exposure.
When having skin that have not seen sun in ages,
one must take one whole month of incremental increases to build skin resistance to sun.

.

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-09T09:56:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-213725>

> [nija said:](/goto/post?id=213684)
>
> yesterday I have spent 4 hours walking in sea beach .I have felt amazing and with lot of energy .
> It was very warm and felt so much rejuvenated . I would like to know -- what is the best time to be in when UV index is low or high .
> I try to in sun morning sunrise when UV index is less than 3 . I am california , San francisco(CA 94404) where UV index is 6 already by 9 -10 am .
> Please suggest the timing for light for better effetive

I think of the sun both as a clock, and a battery recharger .... For the clock function to keep my circadian rhythm on time it helps me to get out in the light at regular times of the day, even if its only for a few minutes, and dressed. My eyes need the changing signals. I want my brain to go through its sunrise, its midmorning, its noon (all crescendoing frequencies), then the decrescending frequencies of mid afternoon, sunset ... and then its darkness night time signalling. If I occasionally miss the sunrise because I've slept in, where I am the UV doesn't come in until later, and I'm still going through the rest of the day cycle, but the ideal for me is regularly resetting my clock during the day, preferably starting at sunrise, because that has the best chance of switching the right hormones on and off at the right time. My brain isn't just looking for frequency, its _change_ of frequency. Even though your UV comes in early, at sunrise it still gets the VIS+IR signal to help optimise hormones. What I'm aiming to avoid is later UV turning hormones off that haven't been turned on properly by the VIS+IR signal ....  So if I was working indoors all day, having seen the sunrise, I would also try and have a few minutes outside at regular intervals (coffee break, lunch etc) for the sun clock to give me a circadian update, telling me what's _changed_ .... There's a good visual here

I also think the pre UV signalling and exposure is important for preparing the body to efficiently handle the coming UV battery charging (I think my brain registers the crescendoing frequencies - get ready for some serious energy gathering gang, UV is on the way .....).

### drezy — 2017-05-09T21:31:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nijas-journal-depression-fatigue-obesity.19189/post-213765>

As a mea culpa for hijacking your journal earlier, I'll go ahead and act appropriately now.

I liked this forum post from @[Mystic Rose60](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/14425/) concerning her mom's BP:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/)

If an elderly lady is able to reverse this than the odds for a young woman like you look good as long as you keep an eye out on your environment.

I had issues with BP that traced back to methylation & high homocysteine. I thought I had corrected it and that going from high to not-so high was all I was going to get because it was in my SNPs. A little over 2 months into regularly getting UV (using [dminder](http://dminder.ontometrics.com) to help keep me methodical) I started getting this "weird" feeling more and more. It wasn't painful or annoying, but just felt different. After a week or so I realized that my hands were warm now all the time. I tested and my BP was 120/70. That's something I had given up on earlier. The "weird" feeling is how I'm supposed to feel.

I can't separate the causation to either just UV or just CT since I started them both at about the same time.

Notes based on your journal:

1-3 minutes(I do at least 10 to be safe) of morning sun is enough to kick off your SCN

If you can restructure your day a little to maximize UV exposure that'll help. FWIW today UV kinda starts at you location a little before 11AM, is maximum at 3:06, and ends at 7:25.

Your skin type requires more UV than mine(dminder can help you a bit with timing). My son's skin is essentially imported from China via his mom's genes. He can spend hours in the sun no problem. I used to bo the white guy that would turn red lobster color after 35 minutes. I've fixed that too. Just keep this in mind. My son jokes about the sun being racist, but when my wife and I do our naked-as-the-day-we-were-born sunbathing and take skin types into account my Polish & Swiss butt usually tops up at about 35 minutes these days and she needs about 1 hour.

The too much information section:

Using empirical knowledge from @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) ( [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/page-2#post-213003](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/page-2#post-213003) ) and a good suggestion from @[SatoriHeart](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19303/) we put lugol's infused coconut oil on our respective bits (so to speak). If you have the privacy it's worth trying.

There were many benefits I can detail, but the big one is that my wife's bad sleep that she's been struggling with before we met is gone. She sleeps like a baby 90% of the time now. As a husband, having a well rested wife is a big deal!

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) @[Sue-UK](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/9658/) @[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) please give some light

### nija — 2017-09-22T23:16:51+0300

My weight is 174 with 5 feet 3 inch . Diagnesed very low level of HPV . I live in fremont california where I can catch 4 strong wifi signal of my neighbor .
Even if I switch off my WIFI , what I can do better for my environment .

1\. Sunlight--->rise and go outside and look at the sun \*\*\*45 minutes of sun every morning from 7 am until 7:45 flat lie in ground After
30 . minutes of noon sun--After lunch, around 12 :00 pm, I would go out and get some UV sun for about 30 minutes
2\. Spring water/RO water
3\. CT protocol 3 times week
4\. seafood-Oyster and fish EPI-paleo
5\. BAB withing 30 min of wakeup(Leptin reset )
6\. 3 liters of water
7\. HIIT 20 min everyday
8.15 min of mindfulness

# Thread: I think I'm turning into a mitochondriac
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-turning-into-a-mitochondriac.19191/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-03-09T18:36:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-think-im-turning-into-a-mitochondriac.19191/post-209960>

Hi all. I've been lurking in these forums and reading and viewing all things Doc Kruse has to say. Yep, with all that reading I think I've gone and finished the whole internet! \[\*spoilers\*\]The middle was a little slow but it has a great ending and I'm hoping maybe for a sequel!

Joking aside, I've been lucky enough to just be in "health optimizing mode" after some 40 year old weight gain. I've flowed from a Paleo diet into a more ketogenic diet over the last 10 years and I think that has helped me maintain my health.

Full disclosure: my Christmas into New Years season got a bit boozy and I packed on same unwanted pounds. I reengaged intermittent fasting, which helped, but one thing blew me away. I started walking 5-10 miles a day outside. My extra weight flew off. It didn't make sense to me since it was zero exertion and I was walking at a speed that even grandmas would point and laugh at.

The number of calories I was burning should have been nowhere near the weight loss I was experiencing. I hit the internet and that's why I'm here now. The only factor that changed for me (as a software engineer) was the light. I stumbled across Doc Kruse and this community backwards by digging through the internet with the question "why would light make a difference?"

An Epi-Paleo head explosion ensued!

I'm glad that I did that search. I've now added 3x / week CT , blue blockers, way more shellfish and other changes into my life with more changes pending and I'm feeling great.

Thanks Doc Kruse and all who are in this forum. It's a life changer.

Weight loss pic below(Don't freak at the "BMI" I'm 6'4" with a fit build and ~ 15% bodyfat now):

# Thread: Brain disease/chronic fatigue/ocd diet hack
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-disease-chronic-fatigue-ocd-diet-hack.19193/>
**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Chaostheory — 2017-03-10T03:49:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-disease-chronic-fatigue-ocd-diet-hack.19193/post-210003>

So I've recently come to the realization that my disease is probably mitochondrial in nature. I have testing that shows it, mito hacks make me feel stronger mentally...but I'm stuck on what to do to get more resilient so I can continue to heal versus fall back into my old habits.

The other day and yesterday I went and looked at the sunrise, and went on a walk. I stood barefoot outside and waded through a local lake. I also spent $5 to be in an infared tanning bed for 30 mins (this felt really good).

Most of my lab tests are fairly normal, but they were done after receiving fecal transplants, which I think may have normalized a lot of things in my body. However, I do still have gut inflammation, adrenal burnout, and mitochondrial issues.

I have a lot of signs of choline deficiency. I'm going to try duck eggs (intolerant to regular eggs) as well as a phosphatidylcholine supplement or maybe MSM (I think I'll do MSM). Phosphatidylserine from soy has helped a little in the past. I'll also supplement uridine. I've also been taking resistant starch for the butyrate (from potato starch). Before going to a doctor, I was trying some crazy things like nsi-189 which didn't work as well as I'd hoped.

I've been occasionally taking acetyl-l-carnitine and alpha-lipoic-acid (is also in one of my blood sugar supplements).

Some hacks I've been thinking of trying: restore4life to help arrest the gut issue, creatine for overall body energy/strength, and I really need something to help with my food cravings/addiction and I was thinking forskolin might be able to help me with that as well as help with thyroid (feel free to tell me other ways to do this).

The infared made me feel better and more resilient to the cold lake, but is being in a tanning bed healthy? I'm not sure. I'll probably just buy an infared reptile lamp anyway so I don't have to go to the tanning salon except maybe for their uva bed.

One question: the only big things I've changed recently are pqq versus coq10 for the last week (ran out of coq10 & had some pqq lying around), infrared bed for 30 mins one day plus some outside sun last few days, and wading through a local lake the last few days. I'm starting to feel a little more alive, or aware, really. I'm not entirely sure, but I think the lake is what did the most for me. What do you think?

The three things I'm adding into my RX: reconnecting with busy friends I've been avoiding, tai chi or qui gong to move things through my body, and maybe babysitting or some kind of social work so I can feel good helping others. Also I printed out the Change RX and skimmed over that.

Oh, another thing I've been doing based off of what I watched on a youtube video: Primal Play. It's specific physical exercises that are good for your brain, I found it through the terry wahl's website.

Would just a regular infrared reptile lamp from a pet store be good enough as far as infrared goes?

I also have been taking magcalm at night and doing some epsom salt baths, and think I may be low on potassium since i’m getting muscle twitches. How much potassium should i take and what brand? Should I also be supplementing boron? I’m working up to 37.5 mg iodine (was low in it since i live in michigan), and some selenium.

My final idea for my brain is to put LLLT on my thyroid and brain to help for myelination purposes. Would that be better than the infrared?

### matthewsb77 — 2017-03-15T20:03:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-disease-chronic-fatigue-ocd-diet-hack.19193/post-210424>

Hey Chaostheory! Thanks for the post. Sounds like we're in a similar position, though I'm a little worse off than you. I'm learning a ton and would love help if I can.

First, a question for you: what tests are showing that your mitochondria aren't working properly? I mean, what was measured? Also, what kind of UV bed were you using? Did it have UVA, -B, and -C?

Also curious about the lake -- what is the benefit there? I'm genuinely curious, as I don't think I've gotten that far in Kruse's discussions yet.

As far as NIR light therapy goes, look at what Dr. Lawrence Wilson suggests on this for a potential design. He also has a whole book on the subject of NIR therapy, but you've gotta ignore the Nutritional Balancing system he advocates. (I've used it, it's good, but a couple decades behind all the EMF and other modern problems.) [http://drlwilson.com/SAUNAS/SAUNA PLANS.htm](http://drlwilson.com/SAUNAS/SAUNA%20PLANS.htm) If you don't want to or can't build a whole unit, you can use one Sylvania 250W red heat lamp. I highly recommend doing a full four-bulb set-up, though (not three as might be shown in Dr. L's plans). It makes a huge difference to get your whole body exposed. And do it naked. More fun, and more exposure. I felt a huge difference between one lamp and four, seriously. And if you can do the full set-up, get this light bulb as well for the UV: [http://www.blacklight.com/items/ftbpa19blb](http://www.blacklight.com/items/ftbpa19blb)

And if you can spend the money, I recommend the Magnetico that Kruse recommends. First INSTANT on it I felt different. The next morning I woke up with a smile on my face for the first time in years. I was so sick when I started it that I sometimes couldn't bathe myself.

I don't know enough about the cholines or LLLT to help you there. I DO know what a biohacker friend of mine says, which is that the laser therapies aren't necessary if you have red bulbs and UV. But that's just one person telling you what one other person has said. . . don't take my word on that.

For iodine, I recommend Nature's Way Kelp. Cheap and effective, though some might disagree with me on it. If you're trying to get boron, Endomet Laboratories has a Calcium/Magnesium/Boron supplement that is relatively inexpensive and works fine. Others may have a negative opinion about Ca/Mg, but it's where I've gotten mine. Also make sure that if there's a thyroid issue, that you're also taking selenium. I can't remember exactly why this is necessary, but you need that and iodine together.

And though it might sound like obsession, people on this forum aren't kidding when they say to manage your light environment. If you aren't already doing it, make sure you take AT LEAST the step of doing morning sunlight right away to set your clock, and blue-blockers, covered skin, and minimal light at night. Some on here say total darkness or red only after sunset. I find that a little extreme because then I would have no social interaction whatsoever (I'm coming out of a phase of being unable to even do staircases, so my interaction comes from the two people I live with, in the evenings). And again, can't say enough for the NIR sauna units. If you or anyone you know has decent hands-on skills for building, you can build the whole thing for $100 or less, including the bulbs. I have a timer added to mine, too, which is nice.

Gut-wise: don't overlook the simple stuff. Sauerkraut, kombucha (sparingly, as it contains a lot of aldehydes), probiotics.

I'm also finding CBD is helping a lot.

EMFs: what's your environment like? Are you grounded? Around computers and cell phone a lot? I'm sure you already know this, but if you're not at least minimizing your EMFs, that can affect your whole system, including your thyroid. I believe I remember reading something about radiation destroying the thyroid? And Kruse talks about how the thyroid can get destroyed by the wrong light, over time. . . so again, refer back to the bit about light environment.

For improving mito capacity and health, you can also look into Magnesium bicarbonate. It's the form of magnesium best suited for improving energy in the mitochondria. My friend Matt Blackburn makes the stuff still, I believe, or you can get it from PristineHydro.

This one's a little "out there" and some would say pseudo-science, so don't do it on its own, but worth looking into: German New Medicine, and German New Medicine thyroid. Google those. You can get a look at some potential emotional conflicts and how they can cause actual rings on the brain (shown in CT scans) and how that affects certain functions of organs in the body. It didn't do anything, but I know some people whose lives totally changed overnight with awareness of a traumatic trigger and how it related to their symptoms.

Muscle twitching can also be about Mg deficiency, I believe. Instead of supplementing potassium long-term and potentially imbalancing something you're not aware of, I'd recommend trying a megadose of K for a couple days (like, too much if you were to take it regularly) and see what changes.

I do Epsom salt baths every few days. I don't know how much they help me.

Hope this helps. I tried to be as detailed and exhaustive as possible, addressing all the points I could, as I really appreciate it when people do the same for me. Much Love.

Looking at your blacklight bulb, I'm thinking maybe one at 365nm, like the one below could be better, since yours goes up to 410nm, which is into the blue, and we want to avoid blue.

[https://www.blacklight.com/items/BL7WLEDBULB](https://www.blacklight.com/items/BL7WLEDBULB)

Yes, as Dr. Wunsch points out below that all shortwave light does damage to the retina, and that naturally sunlight balances ample red and NIR with UV, so it can be prudent to always use red and NIR as well anytime one uses UV indoors, like you do in your setup.

Also a couple of questions re. your blacklight, about how long per day do you use it, distance from eyes, and if you let light from it enter the eyes directly?

### Jack Kruse — 2018-02-21T05:29:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-disease-chronic-fatigue-ocd-diet-hack.19193/post-235259>

5G waveforms: The findings of Dr. Kurt Oughstun and Dr. Richard Albanese on radar technologies which show that mmW (from 5G) might penetrate deep into the body because they create re-radiated waves called Brillouin precursors.

Brillouin precursors show up in Debye media. In thermodynamics and solid state physics, the Debye method is a method developed by Peter Debye in 1912 for estimating the phonon contribution to the specific heat (heat capacity) in a solid. Debye methods now have been extended to liquid crystals like water. Water in the body acts as an anisotropic polarizer. Liquid crystals are examples of anisotropic liquids. Water can be ordered to be a liquid crystal when it is bound to proteins in hydration shells. Anisotropy is the physical property of being directionally dependent, as opposed to isotropy, which implies identical properties in all directions. Water controls the flow of currents using this physical property it contains when it is in a liquid crystalline form. Hydrogen and oxygen alone do not have this ability, so anisotropy is an “emergent” property of water molecule when it is free of deuterium. Life takes massive advantage of this. We also know in disease this breaks down. This occurs in our brain as tau proteins lose electrons and become oxidized, they become denser because they have more protons than electrons within them. This is an unstable state in nature. Things must have a balance of charges and when they don't matter must change. Anytime matter undergoes a phase transition, the matter loses energy and information. This is why 5G is going to spike brain degeneration, mood disorders, mental illness, and prion diseases. Since charges must be balanced, this immediately changes their shape and length of proteins in Debye media (human tissue) to form beta sheets and lose their alpha conformation. That is really what Alzheimer's diseases is. We also know that when water loses its normal dielectric constant (think D, Br– or F–), alpha sheets transform to beta sheets because of the massive shifts in the magnetic moment of the PrPc protein. It also increases the amount of deuterium in cell water which also increases the magnetic moment of water because of the quantum spin of deuterium. Maybe now you can see why I call 5G a coming buffet of deuterium for anything with a human brain. When electrons are lost for any reason at all, the dielectric constant drops and the magnetic moment of the tissue or prion protein in question changes dramatically. This is the quantum thermodynamic change induced by 5G waves I expect to explode soon. When a magnetic moment or field is changed in a tissue, protein, planet, energy drops and the quantum system loses information because entropy rises. This drop, when it occurs in tissues, also induces alpha sheets in prions to transform to beta sheets. This is where neuro-degeneration and autoimmunity begin in all human cases. This is why neurodegeneration is spiking as we move from 1G to a 5G world. The 5G world is the tipping point for our bio-physics based upon my Debye math or probability. All Debye media all have serious dielectric properties as a consequence. The study of dielectric properties concerns storage and dissipation of electric and magnetic energy in materials and tissues. Water is biology's most important dielectric. When the sun hits water its dielectric constant goes from 78-160. 5G waves will interfere with this operation because of the topology of the waves and the power density involved. Dielectrics are important for explaining various phenomena in electronics, optics, solid-state physics, and cell biophysics. Brillouin precursors are visible only at propagation distances such that the impulse response of the medium is essentially determined by the frequency dependence of its absorption and is practically Gaussian. Water has a normal magnetic dipole moment of 1.85 Debye. Polypeptide chains in the α-helical configuration normally have enormous dipole moments. They are upwards of 500 Debye because the individual moments of the peptide bonds are all aligned properly. Beta-sheet amyloids do not have a large dipole moment because they are smaller in size and the shape and their bonds are not aligned properly. This improper bond alignment is what changes the protein length, shape, and mass. Deuterium causes many of these conformational changes. This is why energy and information are lost on the neocortex. This is how cognitive haze or bad memory begins from a quantum perspective. This is why cognition suffers and disease ensues when the beta sheet predominates. This will explode in a 5G world.

ADD like symptoms my whole life never formally diagnosed.

Low mood
Candida
Poor digestion
Poor sleep I work nights in sleep lab
5'9.5 height. 200 lbs approx

Surgeries total hysterectomy 2011 fibroids and anemia
Cholecystectomy 1987
UPPP2011 tonsils very enlarged
Eye surgery 1965 age 3
Ankle ligament repair 1986
Deviated septum repair1985?
2 miscarriages
2 live births

Pyroluria test positive 2 yrs ago. By
Dr in Chicago was on lots of his supplements Did not feel any better
Stopped them.

All regular labs w Primary are within normal range as of jan 2016. W recent physical for work.

. I avoid traditional medicine. Except for acute infections trauma etc I had natural unmedicated births breastfed both my kids and did not vaccinate them.

taking supplements magnesium, digestive enzymes ,betaine hcl fish oil iodine, Sublingual b12. Thorne methyl guard and a few others I can't recall I'm at work at this writing

Diet recently stopped all grains and most dairy still crave sugar
Eating more veggies Leafy greens organic eggs Kerry gold butter
Spring water. Coffee at sleep lab
Organic chicken.( gotta try seafood I know! Only like lobster)

Cold showers and 2 ice baths so far

Meditate daily

Her mom, my maternal grand mother died when my mom was 13 from supposedly Colon cancer this was 1950's.

# Thread: Hi all! Greetings from Holland.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-greetings-from-holland.19203/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Remo — 2017-03-11T17:20:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-greetings-from-holland.19203/post-210125>

It is very nice to meet you all

I have been following Dr. Jack Kruse for a while now and greatly enjoyed listening to his podcasts so I decided it about time to sign up for the forum!

My health (especially mental) has been a big problem for me since I was a child. Chronic fatigue, extreme sensory input issues, panick attacks, light and EMF sensitivity, IBS, hyperventilation you name it. I have been diagnosed with a form of high functioning autism since I was a kid but I never accepted this myself as I could not indentify with the dozens of other people with the same diagnosis.

When I started looking into the meaning of Autism and brain disorders it became pretty clear that many of the complaints associated with these disorders were caused or severely worsened by the environment these people were in. I noticed this heavily in myself as I felt like a completely different person when I was on vacation compared who I was at home! When I got back my IQ and brain functioning would drop fluctuate to the extreme negative ( I honestly believe I was much smarter as a 10yo then I was at 17 !! )

Over the years I discovered some relief by: Grounding, Raw Fish (Sushi, herring!), walking, being outside, drinking mineral water..
This was waaaay before I ever heard of Jack Kruse/Paleo/Bluelight etc. Most of these positive effects I just "noticed" while not knowing why they make me feel better. I am VERY thankful to all of YOU that I do understand now (still need to learn SO much though).

Seriously, I have felt like I have been on the brink of death a few years ago... I could barely get out of my bed, my oxygen levels were extremely low as well (hypoxic). Looking back I suspect it was severe adrenal fatigue/thyroid disfunction. I am still far from optimal functioning but still , the difference is very positive!

Again it is very nice to meet you all. I have allot of questions which I hope some of you can help me with. If any of you want to know anything from me just let me know

Sincerely,

Remo

### JanSz — 2017-03-11T17:24:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-greetings-from-holland.19203/post-210126>

diagnosed with a form of high functioning autism
\=================================================
Get tested.
Check
EPA
DHA
AA

Good likelyhood that you have
high EPA
low AA

If you do, get more detailed fatty acids analysis.
Make corrections to anything that is over (plus-minus 25% of laboratory range, counting from mid range).

/////

///////////////

### Remo — 2017-03-11T17:46:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-all-greetings-from-holland.19203/post-210132>

hey Jan, thanks for the reply! i tested these in the past and my ratio wasnt optimal (results are from some years ago, will find them).

Moreso, I think it says enough that seriously the moment I eat a fresh Herring my brain just feels some kind of "relief". ..my brain fog usually goes away but only for a small while.

I tried doing ketogenic several times but it made my adrenal fatigue worse (not keto flue!). However, during these past trials I did not keep in mind the seasons (did keto all year).

Was told pyroluria was my issue and given a bunch of supplements

I'm sure I have hormonal problems etc

Here reading and just started cold showers and eating seafood

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-12T02:56:46+0200

### Remo — 2017-03-17T22:07:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-w-me-your-how-depression-was-eliminated.19205/post-210546>

what Jack said.

+
I have been heavily bi-polar since I was a little kid (n=1) and after trying out everything the only things that consistenly worked for me where: DHA (from raw fish only! supplements do nothing for me), Cold Showers, Walks (lots of them!), Sunlight (of course), being self forgiving (work on this everyday), but most of all..... EXPRESSION! you have to find a way to creatively express your sorrow. I cannot state how important this is! for me this is music composition and improvisational techniques but maybe it is gardening for you... or maybe a certain sport or game ? i do not know, i do not know you.. do you ? explore, challenge your brain constantly. You can do it! Love.

ps. listening to classically oriented music can be very therapeutic.

I like to chant and listening to music -deva premal and snatam

Linda

### Penny — 2017-03-20T18:13:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-w-me-your-how-depression-was-eliminated.19205/post-210756>

When I was a kid I figured out 2 tried and true ways to happiness:
1) Do something nice for someone
2) Make someone laugh

Having said that, I have heard chromium is great for depression - chromium is great for blood sugar:
[https://www.amazon.com/Lifting-Your-Depression-Psychiatrist-Discovered/product-reviews/1591201640](https://www.amazon.com/Lifting-Your-Depression-Psychiatrist-Discovered/product-reviews/1591201640)

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11343609](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/11343609)
**Chromium treatment of depression.**
[McLeod MN](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=McLeod%20MN%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=11343609), [Golden RN](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/?term=Golden%20RN%5BAuthor%5D&cauthor=true&cauthor_uid=11343609).
**Abstract**
Eight patients with refractory mood disorders received chromium supplements and described dramatic improvements in their symptoms and functioning. In several instances, single-blind trials confirmed specificity of response to chromium. Side-effects were rare and mild, and most commonly included enhanced dreaming and mild psychomotor activation. To our knowledge, this is the first case series describing the response to chromium monotherapy. The putative antidepressant effects of chromium could be accounted for by enhancement of insulin utilization and related increases in tryptophan availability in the central nervous system, and/or by chromium's effects on norepinephrine release.

Cold showers make me totally high too

But really, I don't know how you people stand living in these cold/rainy places - I just implode without the sun - maybe a sun lamp and/or UV lightbulb? I use a reptile light sometimes when it's cloudy - 18 degrees? I would die...

### FrankMathis — 2020-06-26T19:44:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-w-me-your-how-depression-was-eliminated.19205/post-290540>

Kratom isn’t technically an opioid, but its effects [are similar](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0361923016300582) to those of [opioids](https://www.healthline.com/health-news/should-we-care-that-kratom-is-classified-as-an-opioid), such as morphine or codeine.

The active ingredient in kratom is called mitragynine. Mitragynine binds to opioid receptors in the brain, relieving pain.

This action might be behind the antidepressant and anti-anxiety effects reported by some kratom users.

There’s currently very little research on kratom’s effects on mood.

One [2017 review](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.drugalcdep.2017.10.012) confirmed that among some users, kratom enhances mood and relieves anxiety.

The researchers also highlighted that kratom can have sedative effects. Researchers have yet to examine whether side effects such as sedation can interfere with its purported benefits.

# Thread: Greetings from Sydney, Australia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sydney-australia.19224/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 12

### StuartG — 2017-03-14T07:14:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sydney-australia.19224/post-210301>

50 years of age. (feeling like a teenager)
Parents both living - (Dad) x2 bypass ops, pacemaker/defib, bowel reconstruct, due for heart valve next month. (Mum) Breast Cancer surgery/removal post 6 months.

Self employed. Went hard for years (indoors though physical job) Sport fell by the wayside.
Snapped Meniscus in 2008/surgery /took 4 yrs to come good.

Increasing chronic systemic inflammation - multiple crippling heal spurs, plantar fasciitis.
Add in a unfortunate and undiagnosed or investigated thoracic hyper-extension resulting in fracture.

Five years of gradually increasing discomfort/ curvature and degeneration lead to bone on nerve December 2012. (why are we hero's and don't get things looked at!)
Three months of every scan possible, I went on 12 painkillers daily and a back brace. Back spasms were insane.

Add in depression, pre-diabetes, high blood pressure, unfavourable cholesterol ratios, triglycerides and a few other markers all gone. SNP for MTHFR.
Stomach ulcers looming, April 2013 I went cold turkey on all processed foods and beverages AND I was 50kgs obese.

Intensive Chiro got me going, decompressing daily at home. Spent 3/4 of most days for 3 months wrapped up in heat gel packs to find relief. Eventually began swimming to open up spine.

Long story short, no meds/ nothing since May 2013.

4 years along, down 50kg,. Feb 2017 ramped up Blue Light mitigation and introduced CT (just cold baths twice daily)

Excited to report 3 years of lingering thoracic daily (crippling some weeks) has completely gone! Last week I completed 18 days straight mini walking Challenge for Charity walking 215 km. This amount of gravity’s compression usually would have made this impossible seeking many modalities for relief. I feel like a Ferrari. Like I said, thoracic pain is gone!! Amazing. Light, Water, Magnetism.

### JanSz — 2017-03-14T16:08:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-sydney-australia.19224/post-210332>

plantar fascitis

At night, it heals in position you are in.
When you get off the bed and step on foot, it stretches, pain because all night healing just went to hell.
Keep your foot stretched, day and night, long enough so it can heal enough to carry your weight.
Use this type of boot:

[https://www.amazon.com/Plantar-Fasciitis-Posterior-Night-Splint/dp/B00PX5HGPE/ref=sr\_1\_10\_s\_it?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1489500393&sr=1-10&keywords=plantar fasciitis](https://www.amazon.com/Plantar-Fasciitis-Posterior-Night-Splint/dp/B00PX5HGPE/ref=sr_1_10_s_it?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1489500393&sr=1-10&keywords=plantar+fasciitis)

next time try a month or two.

\===================================
///////////////////////////////////////////////
Stomach ulcers looming

You may have HPylori
check that, if you have, no questions asked, use antibiotics until it is gone, rebuild gut flors

You may have low gastric acid,
you may check level by testing
you may start taking Betaine HCL tabs, see if that would givel you relief.

\=============================================
////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////
unfavourable cholesterol ratios, triglycerides

there is a test
Spectracell Micronutrient Analysis
if you are ever in USA, EU or where they are available
it is must have
[https://www.spectracell.com/patients/get-tested/](https://www.spectracell.com/patients/get-tested/)
[https://www.spectracell.com/order/](https://www.spectracell.com/order/)
\=============================
but
also important is to check fatty acids
best tests is this (well, second best)
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

watch them all, but start with Omega6 and Omega3
on that test you want (ideally) all at the level equal to average level of laboratory range.
but acceptable is
plus minus 25% off laboratory range.
Adjust your diet aiming at that type results.
Usually
it means
no fish or krill oils
but many other.

Omega6 and omega 3 are in the news this days.
People are being sold bill of goods.
If you cannot get above test, then get this three:
EPA
DHA
AA
the test must be such that it will give you
value for each of them
and
upper and lover acceptable levels.
Treat them same way as i described above.

You do not want a lot of what is advertised today.

### Assadi — 2017-03-14T09:35:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.19225/post-210308>

Hey i have a few questions would appreciate an answer thx

1-Is it ok to be in front of blue light computer mostly as long as you have blue blocking glasses and there are a lot of people who are in front of blue light but still seem healthy

2-Is keto ok for teens and isn t it bad if you train instensly for the adrenals

3-How can i skyrocket energy levels other things then water , sleep and micronutrient

4- what are the top 5 supllements and are hot bath bad

### JanSz — 2017-03-14T15:53:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.19225/post-210331>

\[qu ote="Assadi, post: 210308, member: 19909"\]Hey i have a few questions would appreciate an answer thx

1-Is it ok to be in front of blue light computer mostly as long as you have blue blocking glasses and there are a lot of people who are in front of blue light but still seem healthy
Every human is unique. Some are strong other are weak. Some are tall other are short.
Think of situation, when being outside, when would you see the blue light and when not.
Answer, ---->when you can see daylight additional light from computer or cellphone screen is of lesser concern.
So, your major problem (mismatch) with light is from the screen is when sun is out.
Other considerations are less than major.
Major action of blue light is to tell your pineal gland if there is a day or night.
At night pineal gland is allowed to make melatonin.
Melatonin stops adrenals from making cortisol.

2-Is keto ok for teens and isn t it bad if you train instensly for the adrenals

3-How can i skyrocket energy levels other things then water , sleep and micronutrient
Support your mitochondria.
What you call micronutrient support is likely different than what I call it.
I do testing every 6 months to figure out my status and then adjust my diet and list of supplements.
Following tests:
At LabCorp:
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
Homocysteine, Plasma

At Spectracell
[https://www.spectracell.com/patients/get-tested/](https://www.spectracell.com/patients/get-tested/)
[https://www.spectracell.com/patients/products/](https://www.spectracell.com/patients/products/)
Micronutrient Test (MNT)
**$** 390.00

I also use selenium and Lugols.

4- what are the top 5 supllements and are hot bath bad

See above, do test, figure out your individual needs.
each person is separate individual with different needs.
\[/quote\]

### Lahelada — 2017-03-14T19:25:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.19225/post-210349>

Assadi, no blue light of artificial origin can be considered good at any time of day. Jan SZ was essentially saying that light blue light mitigated by natural light streaming in through an open window may take longer to show damage . A lot of variables come into play. Some people react quickly others take longer . You will need to do a good deal of reading before you can properly evaluate your situation. Same goes for your question about energy. Light ,water magnetism is what we discuss here. No such thing as five top supplements. Hot bath depends on context. Intensive training under blue light is a NO for anyone. Training outside is better but still not recommended for everyone. Read the Epi Paleo RX for a starting point.
Make your questions more case specific ,explaining who you are asking for and why . Otherwise any answer will be misleading.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-04T00:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.19225/post-211697>

Since they are making health claims they will have to deal with the FDA soon.

As the sun sets, blue visible light is preferentially scattered (removed) from sunlight, leaving an emission appearing orange-red \[600 - 700 nm\]. At night, there is darkness with limited visible light emitted from the stars, with the exception of when there is a full moon. During the full moon, there is five times the amount of visible light emitted from the sky, and significant light emitted in the blue visible range (Ugolini, 2007).

Humans evolved being exposed to different spectra of light in the morning, the late afternoon and evening. So it should not be surprising that human physiology is profoundly affected by the daily and seasonal changes in the visible light spectrum. Exposure to the appropriate spectrum of light during the day and evening enhances human health and well being, immune response and productivity (Roberts, 2000; Vetch et al., 2004; Cutolo M et al., 2005; Heschong and Roberts, 2009). However, exposure to light sources that do not match the natural solar spectrum to the time of day or evening, is hazardous to human health (Wilson, 1972; Stevens et al., 2007; Rea et al., 2008; Erren and Reiter, 2008; Arendt, 2010). The reason visible light has such a powerful effect on human health is that light exposure through the eye modifies circadian rhythm (Gaddy et al., 1993; Roberts, 1995; Czeisler et al., 1995).

### WalterNL — 2017-04-04T12:12:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions.19225/post-211710>

> [Assadi said:](/goto/post?id=211016)
>
> Hey i was wearing blue light blocking glasses during the day and i felt super tierd is it beacuse my body started producing melatonin during the day or is it something else

Yes that could be. During the day you need full spectrum sunlight, which includes blue light.

# Thread: Baby toys
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2017-03-14T21:08:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210355>

Hallo,

do you guys have some suggestions which toys you can buy for a child 6 months+?
Some relatives want to make some gifts.

### Penny — 2017-03-14T23:10:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210362>

> [Butters said:](/goto/post?id=210355)
>
> Hallo,
>
> do you guys have some suggestions which toys you can buy for a child 6 months+?
> Some relatives want to make some gifts.

Anything that helps their sensory perception - I love the whole Montessori method:
[http://www.montessori-on-a-budget.com/2013/04/top-10-things-for-montessori-baby.html](http://www.montessori-on-a-budget.com/2013/04/top-10-things-for-montessori-baby.html)
[http://www.dailymontessori.com/montessori-materials/montessori-toys-gifts/](http://www.dailymontessori.com/montessori-materials/montessori-toys-gifts/)

You can't go wrong with wooden blocks and anything soft -
Maria Montessori said don't give a child anything to do unless you are 90% sure he can successfully do it - it was great advice really

We loved the bouncy chair - just don't get one of those rolling chairs -

### drezy — 2017-03-19T00:39:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210625>

I'm with @[Penny](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/446/) .

To back up what was said. Think textures that hands and a mouth could appreciate: parallel lines, wavy, spiky(within reason), smooth, abrupt depth changes, gradual depth changes, stepped depth changes. Don't discount warm and cold as well as still and vibrating. My child took to cold object so much that we threw random toys in the freezer. Cold items kinda makes sense based on this whole site/science.

Might as well see if your baby can explore textures as well as mini-ct at the same time.

### Inger — 2017-03-19T11:11:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210649>

Check out it does not have any chemicals in it... many toys have  the baby will put it in the mouth etc. Babies want to taste everything!

### Penny — 2017-03-20T00:04:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210701>

One of those ride on fire trucks was a great toy - and then there is the veritable wagon -

### drezy — 2017-03-20T03:40:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-210722>

\*Warning\* I'm ahead of 6 months a bit here, but it's worth mentioning while I've got it loaded in memory

My boy is 12 now, but I loved being a dad to a young child. I'm a little ahead of the 6 month age, but one of our favorite activities was "identify the spice". I do quite bit of cooking and have so many different spices.

Name 5-15 spices and then have the child smell them. Then, later, have the child smell the spice and name it. Sensory + lingual skills are involved.

Even though my boy is 12 now, he walked into my dad's place when my dad was making "maple" bacon and informed my dad that "That's fenugreek, not maple." My dad was taken aback a bit until he read the ingredient list t find out that, sure enough, fenugreek (since it smells like maple but is cheaper) was used.

### JuicerMoz — 2017-07-31T12:41:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-220500>

A toy is an item that is used in play, especially one designed for such use. Playing with toy scan be an enjoyable means of training young children for life in society. Different materials like wood, clay, paper, and plastic are used to make toys.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-07-31T16:58:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-220527>

Make sure none are from China.

### Butters — 2017-10-21T22:34:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-toys.19228/post-226347>

Thanks. Time showed she loves more running around and climbing on things

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-i-go.19236/post-214910>

Grandmother: Appendix removed & hysterectomy 3years later developed Dementia
Mother: fibrocystic ovary, Appendix removed, High Cholesterol, hysterectomy & undiagnosed intestinal flare up in the last 3 year.
Me: Diagnosed with dyslexia as a child, Lethargic my whole life, brain fog, Migraines since childhood, Tension headaches as adult, Appendix removed at 10, PMS at youth & PMDD after childbearing.

Grew up in a suburban neighborhood until 18 when I moved to Hayward Ca. Moved back to my hometown after a year. At 19 I developed a chronic cough that sound like a tick. I was on many different types of medications in an attempt to calm or stop the cough. I also had many tests done. After 5 years I was referred to UCSF where I started receiving botox injections into my vocal cords. I still coughed just could not talk for 2 months until it wore off. Right before the doctors cut my Vagus nerve as the next course of treatment I got pregnant. After reading Dr. Kruse's work I look back and am thankful they didn't cut it. I coughed until my 3rd trimester where my cough calmed down on its own. My hormones after I had my first child got very bad. After my second even worse. I would spend 2 weeks after my period started ok. Right before I ovulated I would panic because I knew the cascade was coming. After I ovulated I became someone different. 1.5 years ago after my first child was diagnosed with ADHD I started my journey to health and wellness for myself and my family.
**First years health wins:**
\*Removed cleaning chemicals replaced with Essential oils and safe options
\*Stopped using all medication (Tylenol, Aleve, Ibuprofen and allergy medications) for myself and family switched to Essential oils
\*Switched to only Organically grown produce.
\*Switched to free range chicken,grass fed beef and wild caught seafood

At the end of last year I read Dr. Kruse's book. Dots started connecting and so my real to optimal began.
**Current**:
**Morning**: 1-2hours outside 75% skin exposed while grounded.
**Noon**: 1-3hours outside 100% skin exposed while grounded.
**Evening**: 1-2 hours outside before sunset grounded.
**Blue blockers on at sunset**.
Wifi is shut off in the morning
Doing face dunks 3 times a week.
Taking colder showers and jumping in the pool every day.
Started Win Hoff breathing technique this week.
Eating Epi-Paleo
**Supplements:**
_GI Repair containing:_ L-Glutamine, Aloe Vera, Methylsulfonylmethane, Slippery Elm Bark, Marshmallow root, D-Glucosamine HCI, Deglycyrrhizinated & Proprietary Enzyme blend (Amylase,Lactase,Cellulase,Invertase&Maltase).
CoQ10

Although I know that I can not get health in the environment I got sick in this is where my family needs to be until we are ready financially to move. My husband works in IT in the bay area which breaks my heart everyday he leaves for work. #needsaquantlet ( yes,I just hash tagged for no reason)
**Goals:**
\*be more disciplined! parenting can have you running in circles
\*increase seafood
\*increase CT
\*hack my environment to protect from EMF
\* keep reading and studying

The test was taken at 1 pm. My doctor recommended Adren-All supplements. Should I take the supplements first or the medication?
Is this me?....
**Adrenal Fatigue Rx: Chronically depressed cortisol: The more common clinical situation we see in AF:**
When the body is under chronic stress, pregnenolone, the precursor to all other steroidal hormones, is diverted to produce cortisol (known as pregnenolone steal syndrome). When this occurs, it is to the detriment of all other steroidal hormones, like DHEA and its metabolites, including progesterone, testosterone, and the estrogens. Vitamin D production will also be down regulated. As pregnenolone is diverted to cortisol, DHEA depletion begins quickly. The result is an elevated cortisol to DHEA ratio. A normal ratio is approximately 4:1 to 6:1 in most ASI labs. This upside down ratio is able to inhibit the PVN of the brain quickly. Why? Cortisol is needed to make new nerve cells in the hippocampus. This is the key element to the Adrenal Fatigue Rx.

**The Key Problem for Healing:** Re-establish the progesterone level in the brain immediately. I also do this for brain surgery cases. General anesthesia also depletes our brain of progesterone and can lead to cognitive dysfunction for a few days post op.

A. We must re establish the Progesterone level quickly to increase BDNF signaling to make new neurons to replace the damaged nerve tracts that allow for altered signaling.

B. Once the new neurons are made, they need to be connected together. This is done with optimal diurnal cortisol AM and PM levels.

C. The new neural circuits created must wire together to become “hardwired” via Hebbian learning. This is done by optimal diurnal melatonin levels at night when we are chemically reduced. This is when the brain trims bad neural circuits and strengthens good ones to regain control of signaling. If melatonin is off you can not re wire a bad brain no matter what anyone tells you. This is why sleep is so critical to human biology.

D. Neurons that fire together wire together. This means that you should consider things that strengthen your newly built circuits when you use this Adrenal Fatigue Rx. Cold Thermogenesis, short explosive sprinting, and meditation are the best safest ways to increase chronic secretion of brain derived nerve growth factor (BDNF) and nerve growth factor (NGF) in the brain to get it to heal quickly.

E. NT building blocks and co factor supports to power the synaptic clefts. DHA and iodine are hugely important to synaptic cleft signaling. It provides the bio-energic power and antioxidant protection to explosively powerful signaling found in all neurons.

F. Strict control of all inflammatory cytokines and acute phase proteins to keep the system humming and immune system working in unison with the PVN.

G. Consider the rest of the things in my initial [Adrenal Fatigue Protocol](https://www.jackkruse.com/what-might-casey-anthony-and-oj-have-in-common/) at this point. I only use this in minor cases. Most cases need a rewire as I am laying out here in detail in this blog.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-19T15:38:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-devein-or-not-to-devein-that-is-the-question.19237/post-210670>

Eating the exoskeleton provides you with massive amounts of chitin, iodine and carotenoids that help with solar callus and light assimilation. **Carotenoids** are [organic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organic_compound) [pigments](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pigment) that are found in the [chloroplasts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chloroplast) and [chromoplasts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chromoplast) of plants and some other [photosynthetic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photosynthesis) [organisms](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organism), including some [bacteria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bacteria) and some [fungi](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fungi). Carotenoids can be produced from fats and other basic organic metabolic building blocks by all these organisms. The only animals known to produce carotenoids are [aphids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aphid) and [spider mites](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spider_mite), which acquired the ability and genes from fungi. **Carotenoids from the diet are stored in the fatty tissues of animals, and exclusively _carnivorous animals obtain the compounds from animal fat.
Carotenoids are photoinactivators for our eye clock. When excessive blue is present more Vitamin A (retinol) is made initially, before it becomes extinct in the macula. Why?

Ever heard of Carotenoid protection? Aphids use it and so do we to protect us from nnEMF like RF radar..............

Most photosensitizers are organic dyes, but we use carotenoids and parts of proteins as photosensitizers and inactivators.
Carotenoid protection takes singlet oxygen and extincts it. Singlet O2 + 1CAR = triplet O2 + 3CAR
The deactivation 3CAR >1CAR + liberation of heat or IR light.

Photosensitizers have rules......quantum optical rules they must follow.

1\. Excitation
2\. Decays
3\. Type 1 or Type 2 reactions (which are O2 dependent)
4\. Carotenoid protection

After absorption of light photons, the photosensitizer is first transferred to an excited singlet state by the quantized energy in the photon. Then 3 possible decay channels are possible. This is why quantum physics works on probabilities and not absolutes. The 3 cases are non radiative, radiative singlet state decay to the ground state, and intersystem crossing to an excited triplet state.

Anyone who avoids seafood/crustaceans for ANY reason is not part of my tribe._ SIMPLE QUANTUM RULE.**

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-19T15:46:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-devein-or-not-to-devein-that-is-the-question.19237/post-210672>

Mankind has been made "surfers" by governments, militaries, and by telecom corporations dollars. For "oceanic surfers" all over the world, the perfect wave in the sea is a dream, a myth that will probably never materialize. But many "surfers" know that to tame and control waves in the sea is a matter of calculable strength, physics, and power. Likewise, dealing with all the possible implications concerning electromagnetic field exposure is an exquisite matter of equilibrium between public health and social benefits. New insights on the current knowledge about radiofrequency (RF) effects on a biological system are being published every week now, yet this won't slow the roll-out of 5 G that uses massive amounts of RF radiation to operate. RF radiation is used on RADAR systems that are shown in the video below. There is now a mounting literature regarding the consequences of extremely low frequency-electromagnetic field (ELF-EMF) and RF exposure on cancer and the possible exploitation of RF in cancer treatment has been thoroughly reviewed in the literature. Sadly too few people or their oncologists know the data. They continue to be fooled by their beliefs. Early in vitro studies of RF-EMF were mostly designed to study the toxic effect of RF on a cell by looking at the nuclear genome and DNA damage. Some of them aimed at clarifying the intracellular pathways that would sustain induced DNA damage and cellular fate. Not one of these studies every considered the other genome in the cell, namely, the mitochondrial genome that creates all of the energy needs of the cell. The more energy that the mitochondria can produce at any one time keeps the nuclear genome quiescent. That means that when energy generation is lowered from the mitochondrial genome this will cause an INCREASE of nuclear genomic changes by increasing ubiquitin rates via epigenetic expression. This is controlled by calcium second messenger system. The published research largely ignores these links this is why every scientist believes that RF radiation is safe for humans when this is not true. Watch the aphids on this flower bounce every time they are shot with RF radiation from an airport radar 14 miles away........If this affects them at this distance what do you think is happening to your mitochondrial with every spin of ANY RADAR you cannot see in your local environment?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-19T15:50:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/to-devein-or-not-to-devein-that-is-the-question.19237/post-210674>

Radars usually operate at radio frequencies (RF) between 300 MHz and 15 GHz. They generate EMFs that are called RF fields. RF fields within this part of the electromagnetic spectrum are known to interact differently with human body.

RF fields below 10 GHz (to 1 MHz) penetrate exposed tissues and produce heating due to energy absorption. The depth of penetration depends on the frequency of the field and is greater for lower frequencies. Absorption of RF fields in tissues is measured as a Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) within a given tissue mass. The unit of SAR is watts per kilogram (W/kg). SAR is the quantity used to measure the "dose" of RF fields between about 1 MHz and 10 GHz.

-   An SAR of at least 4 W/kg is needed to produce known adverse health effects in people exposed to RF fields in this frequency range.

RF fields above 10 GHz are absorbed at the skin surface, with very little of the energy penetrating into the underlying tissues. The basic dosimetric quantity for RF fields above 10 GHz is the intensityof the field measured as power density in watts per square metre (W/m2) or for weak fields in milliwatts per square metre (mW/m2) or microwatts per square metre (µW/m2).

-   Exposure to RF fields above 10 GHz at power densities over 1000 W/m2 are known to produce adverse health effects, such as eye cataracts and skin burns.

Cataracts are now produced at an explosive incidence in today's microwaved blue lit world...........coincidence or not? The wise will realize the connections.

Carotenoids can be produced from fats and other basic organic metabolic building blocks by all these organisms. The only animals known to produce carotenoids are [aphids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aphid) and [spider mites](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spider_mite), which acquired the ability and genes from fungi. **Carotenoids from the diet are stored in the fatty tissues of animals, and exclusively _carnivorous animals obtain the compounds from animal fat.
> Carotenoids are photoinactivators for our eye clock. When excessive blue is present more Vitamin A (retinol) is made initially, before it becomes extinct in the macula. Why?
>
> Ever heard of Carotenoid protection? Aphids use it and so do we to protect us from nnEMF like RF radar..............
>
> Most photosensitizers are organic dyes, but we use carotenoids and parts of proteins as photosensitizers and inactivators.
> Carotenoid protection takes singlet oxygen and extincts it. Singlet O2 + 1CAR = triplet O2 + 3CAR
> The deactivation 3CAR >1CAR + liberation of heat or IR light.
>
> Photosensitizers have rules......quantum optical rules they must follow.
>
> 1\. Excitation
> 2\. Decays
> 3\. Type 1 or Type 2 reactions (which are O2 dependent)
> 4\. Carotenoid protection
>
> After absorption of light photons, the photosensitizer is first transferred to an excited singlet state by the quantized energy in the photon. Then 3 possible decay channels are possible. This is why quantum physics works on probabilities and not absolutes. The 3 cases are non radiative, radiative singlet state decay to the ground state, and intersystem crossing to an excited triplet state.
>
> Anyone who avoids seafood/crustaceans for ANY reason is not part of my tribe._ SIMPLE QUANTUM RULE.**

wow. Great stuff. makes total sense too. And yet another reason not to need to take a pill (like astaxanthin)!

RF radiation is used on RADAR systems that are shown in the video below. Early in vitro studies of RF-EMF were mostly designed to study the toxic effect of RF on a cell by looking at the nuclear genome and DNA damage. Some of them aimed at clarifying the intracellular pathways that would sustain induced DNA damage and cellular fate. Not one of these studies every considered the other genome in the cell, namely, the mitochondrial genome that creates all of the energy needs of the cell. The more energy that the mitochondria can produce at any one time keeps the nuclear genome quiescent. That means that when energy generation is lowered from the mitochondrial genome this will cause an INCREASE of nuclear genomic changes by increasing ubiquitin rates via epigenetic expression. This is controlled by calcium second messenger system. The published research largely ignores these links this is why every scientist believes that RF radiation is safe for humans when this is not true. **TIRED CHILDREN FROM radiation SCREEN TIME?** Could this be why kids brains soften into mental illness and developmental delay and autism so that they could conceivably kill themselves and one another in the future??? Are school shooters built the same way? I think so.

### Scott Hardin — 2018-11-21T18:50:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256209>

"I don't know much about the guy, but someone once told me the heart crushing story behind Wim Hoff's usage of the cold. Holy shit! If you ever read or listen to that account \*Warning\* only do so in the sun. It's as crushing as it is powerful."

Wim's story is VERY, VERY powerful.

I did the Wim Hoff Method about 4 years ago and it felt like a neat trick - I could walk barefoot in the snow for example. However, I was living somewhere with beautiful views of nature, but ZERO direct sunlight. I was always sick, always depressed. The breathing exercises were torture with a permanent sinus infection. I'm about to get back into it now.

I've been 8 months without antibiotics for a sinus infection which is the longest I have ever been in my 42 years. I think my higher vitamin D status helps AND I suspect that UV irradiates the blood and Red and NIR are helping my body regenerate damaged tissues.

Can't wait to try WHM again when deep breathing doesn't feel like razor blades cutting through my sinuses.

Also - I now realize that Wim does so much of his actual work outside that he likely doesn't even realize the importance of the sun.

### Sheddie — 2018-11-22T00:18:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256254>

> [Scott Hardin said:](/goto/post?id=256216)
>
> I live in Atlanta, pollen capital of the USA; However, I think my issues were 1) lack of sunlight and 2) mold and mildew. Most homes here have basements and nearly all are damp. 1.5 years ago I moved to a condo and Im on the 3rd floor… terrible for EMF but the first place I've lived in 20 years that is 100% dry.
>
> I've been offered sinus surgery numerous times, but I've never met anybody it helped.

For some reason since moving here years ago, I never developed the very common sinus problems. People kept saying, just wait 7 years and you'll suffer, too. BUT, just last August I got my first sinus infection ever -- after plane travel. It was very unpleasant (I finally learned empathy!) and I tried a number of ineffective remedies for weeks. Finally, the thing that worked was rinsing my sinuses religiously, three times a day, then twice, now once a day (maintenance) with a baking soda and sea salt mixture (1 tsp each in a quart of distilled water). The immovable 'goop' in those inside cavities flanking my nose was gone the third day and stayed gone after about 2 weeks. I use the linked apparatus but there are many that work similarly. [https://smile.amazon.com/Neilmed-Sinus-Rinse-Starter-packets/dp/B000RJGB5C/ref=sr\_1\_6\_a\_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1542837492&sr=8-6&keywords=sinus+bottle+rinse&th=1](https://smile.amazon.com/Neilmed-Sinus-Rinse-Starter-packets/dp/B000RJGB5C/ref=sr_1_6_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1542837492&sr=8-6&keywords=sinus%2Bbottle%2Brinse&th=1) (Unfortunately I don't know if a dev. septum would contraindicate this method?) Last week I took another plane trip, used the _Humidiflyer_ while traveling (thanx to this site) and the sinus rinse later -- NO sinus issues this time! Hopefully, never again.

>
> This reminds me of perhaps an uninformed question, but I’m asking anyway. Why were isolated populations near the equator (like Mayan, etc.) so devastated by the diseases brought by invaders? Why wasn’t their redox strong enough? Lack of DHA?

Well, what I have read is, the invaders brought alcohol with them and probably foods that were not natural, and traded these for other things with the native indians.
That would be one reason how their redox lowered.

The other reason I guess is, invaders brought viruses and bacteria that the native indians had had no chance to adapt to, as it was from another continent... so they just did not have immunity to it. That is often an issue when foreigners enter tribes that live far away, without any contact to the modern world.

Otherwise, their redox was indeed really strong. Their wounds healed super fast, they were really strong and healthy.

### Scott Hardin — 2018-11-23T22:50:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256370>

> [Phosphene said:](/goto/post?id=256280)
>
> This reminds me of perhaps an uninformed question, but I’m asking anyway. Why were isolated populations near the equator (like Mayan, etc.) so devastated by the diseases brought by invaders? Why wasn’t their redox strong enough? Lack of DHA?

While Redox is a HUGE part of the story, let’s not ever be so dogmatic as to think it’s the entire story. Infectious disease still is a major evolutionary driver.

### drezy — 2018-11-23T23:34:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-do-i-still-feel-cold.19238/post-256375>
