> Nature requires you to sift through bullshit to find the prize..........If some comments or ideas or critic move you off your target then this site is not for that person. I think the key is curiosity and mito-hacking. No one's results will be identical to another because no environment is identical and neither is their mitochondria or their distance between their respiratory proteins. So when somebody bitches and moans about critiques.........words, grammar spelling..........you name........they announce their own limitations to optimal.

I agree with you in the sense that every person's environment is different and it is an N=1. But that being said, you could have one person who smokes, but everything else about their environment is optimal, and they'll probably be fine. It sounds like Sean has had some success with dealing with his crohn's, so obviously he must be doing something right, and is looking at improving performance/gaining muscle mass.

My N=1 is that no matter what the season, I do best on a mostly carb and fat diet with protein on the lower end (40/40/20). Keto this winter destroyed my testosterone, and I suffered from terrible eczema. Since adding carbs back to the mix, and sticking to saturated fats like dairy, butter, coconut oil and milk chocolate while cutting out PUFAs, and the improvement was nearly instantaneous.

I personally think keto is probably not such a bad idea for an overweight individual needing to train their body to burn fat by purposely inducing a state of insulin resistance so they can essentially simulate hibernation and burn fat. But at the same time, they might do well on a high carb/low fat diet so that the glucose can fuel the metabolism, and the lack of fats in the diet forces bodyfat metabolism. Bears reverse their insulin resistance coming out of hibernation by eating honey. Maybe humans need a little sugar too, and cut out the PUFAs: [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18171691

Haha, also, forgot to mention that maple syrup is in season here in Canada in the late winter/early spring and it's pure sugar. Probably a good idea to eat lots of it](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18171691)

### Sean Waters — 2017-05-18T14:49:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/post-214249>

Time for an update............

1\. I've started Doug McGuff's Body By Science protocol and noticed very immediate improvements in strength, solidness of muscles and size of muscles as well as body fat. I also feel the endorphins post work-out - which I never did with regular Linear Strength Protocols.

2\. I've found that resting for 7 days is good, but resting for 9-10 days is even better and muscles start to pop up at this time.

3\. I have finally got a Keyboard and Mouse at work to keep away from my Laptop an extra few feet, and the eczema on my wrists and hands has reduced by about 90%. This is one of my LAST problems that I really wanted to address. I've still not used my smartphone, its been off for 2 months now, my 2G phone remains on airplane mode except during workhours.

4\. I have realised that "Bulking" with the goal of gaining "Mass" instead of "Lean Muscle Mass" is actually ridiculous, especially when it's so difficult to maintain Ketosis on such a high calorie diet.

With my pyruvate kinase deficiency (which seems to be attributed to non-native EMF) I really need to stay in Ketosis to remain of sound MIND and BODY - If not, then my energy levels drop and my mitochondria lose electrons rapidly. Sugar really is the difference in this non-native EMF environment I live in. Until I can change that, I must adhere to Ketosis to be optimal.

I think my dopamine levels were low this winter when I wanted to pile on 30 pounds of mass. I would now rather be Leaner and Slimmer with a better Mass Equivalence.

5\. Actually gaining LEAN MUSCLE MASS at the rate in which my impatient brain wanted to, was absurd, and impossible considering my environment. I realise now that a steady gain of 10 pounds for this year is probably the target I should aim for - any more will be a bonus but should not be expected.

6\. Following on from the above comment, the rate of growth often achieved by people is due to Steroids and/ or rare Genetics. I think CT could act as a natural steroid however, and I am CT'ing every day under Cold Showers. I am very close, if not definitely, Cold Adapted now... I never feel the cold and don't even flinch under the shower anymore or feel the Pre-CT "fear" that I suffered with before.

7\. I have noticed that anytime I get symptoms such as Twinges, Mouth ulcers, Headaches, Aches/ Pains, Sickness or Nausea, Eczema, Bad moods - paranoia or anxiety, Aggression and Bad tempers.......... It's ALWAYS because I am in the vicinity of some form Non-Native EMF.

8\. I want to go camping. Real bad.

Thanks for reading, Peace.

Post:

The last few weeks I've been feeling really tired some days, especially if my sleep has been affected or disrupted, I was thinking this was due to:

1.  not being in ketosis after eating too many "accidental" carbs in my foods/ gluconeogenesis
2.  EMF
3.  disrupted sleep
4.  **a combination of the above**

So I started doing the following:

1.  Fasting longer (24 hours to increase ketones)
2.  Avoiding EMF even more
3.  Focusing on not eating post 8pm and blocking light so sleep is better

Results:

1.  Still had no energy. Felt like I had weights strapped to me.
2.  Felt defeated, unmotivated, very down and frustrated.
3.  Words, memories etc. not coming to me as quick

I was in Ketosis between 1.8 and 3.0 mm/mol , showering in cold water daily. But I had these problems. It didn't make any sense.
This was all on Saturday morning.
So I realised my problem and solution is either one of two things:

1.  **The nnEMF** I get at work and in my city could potentially be ramped up, beyond my perception, or just winning the battle against me. And **I need to MOVE to another country to get better** and be optimal.
2.  **I need to start eating more carbohydrates**, as it's summertime, and **Ketosis is mis-matching with my dark brown skin tan** that I have developed over the last few weeks through exposure to UV index of 1 every morning, and UV index of 5 or 6 all day on weekends and at lunchtime during the week.

I've started planning the move.
I've also started eating more carbs
Strangely, I do feel better. Which is weird for me, as when I tested in 2016 I had a Pyruvate Deficiency that blocks glucose metabolism - leading to low energy, cognitive issues, mood etc.
I was discussing this exact thing on a thread in Optimal Fitness: [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/help-for-a-keto-leptin-rx-bulk.19254/)
This means my Pyruvate Kinase Genetic Deficiency - could quite possibly be "turned off" from the Sunlight, epigenetically... (Think I could be quite high on the Gerald Pollack "OUT ON A LIMB" meter with this one) OR I have just become Cold Adapted through CT'ing for over a year now, and developed Metabolic Flexibility.
So I'm not sure where to go from here...

-   Could I have a low testerone problem in Ketosis like you Kris? (_Caveat: I seem to have lots of energy during winter, in Ketosis, with low sun and very cold CT's)_
-   Is the nnEMF causing a perception that I need Carbs?
-   Is it natural for my body to want Carbs, as I've caught so much sun, and my circadian biology is mis-matching in Ketosis with all this sun?
-   If it is the nnEMF causing a false perception that I need carbs , do I stick with the Carbs anyway until Winter time?...
-   If I should eat more carbs, then how much more?

Insulin61.6 uU/mL0.0 - 20.0 uU/mL

**I am not a doctor.**
Have you got any diagnosis? Post them.
\------------------------------------------------------------
From Mayo Clinic I got:
[http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-.../expert-answers/hyperinsulinemia/FAQ-20058488](http://www.mayoclinic.org/diseases-conditions/type-2-diabetes/expert-answers/hyperinsulinemia/FAQ-20058488)

Hyperinsulinemia is most often caused by insulin resistance — a condition in which your body is resistant to the effects of insulin. Your pancreas tries to compensate by making more insulin. Insulin resistance may eventually lead to the development of type 2 diabetes.

This is most likely reason.
There are two other that are rare:

A rare tumor of the insulin-producing cells of the pancreas (insulinoma)
Excessive numbers or growth of insulin-producing cells in the pancreas (nesidioblastosis)

\-------------

Congenital disorder is a condition existing at or before birth regardless of cause.
\-----------
Congenital hyperinsulinism
These conditions are present at birth and most become apparent in early infancy. Mild cases can be treated by frequent feedings, more severe cases can be controlled by medications that reduce insulin secretion or effects.
\-----------

nesidioblastosis looks like another name for Congenital hyperinsulinism
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congenital\_hyperinsulinism](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Congenital_hyperinsulinism)
Diagnostic
Inappropriately low levels of free fatty acids and ketones provide additional evidence of insulin excess.

There other ways are described.
\-------------

You have lots of inflammation going on.
You have unwanted organism identified.
It would be good idea to get closer look there.

......

If you had the same ones drawn in 6 months time after completing the leptin reset, doing CT, blue blocking, grounding and harnessing the sun for seasonal signalling and harvesting of photons etc, it should show an improvement in trend.

The leptin reset will help reset your circadian rhythm. When the circadian rhythm is working better, the liver handles food differently after an overnight fast - it sends energy to the peripheral tissues so that we have the energy to move around our environment, think better, make better decisions. In the meantime its also an abundance signal, telling your _female_ brain that you aren't in the middle of the famine it thinks you are in (i.e. overcoming leptin resistance). Just improving circadian rhythm, getting better sleep and getting natural sunlight can improve insulin levels. Insulin is a solar hormone. Apart from Vitamin D the UV portion of sunlight is also antibacterial, antiviral, it can improve thyroid function etc etc. CT helps reduce inflammation, with reduced inflammation comes better leptin sensitivity. That may take time but women often notice shape changes from lower inflammation.

I'd get the basics of light water magnetism under my belt before spending another dime on a follow up test. The public podcasts Jack mentions are free. Then I'd weigh up spending $1000 on more tests that could overwhelm me or even if I did understand them would be out of date very quickly.... versus 10 months gold membership with access to the whole back catalogue of webinars and QandA's from _a man who fully understands that a female's bioenergetics and biochemistry is different to that of a male....._ (I'd have been long gone if that wasn't the case). A soon to be out of date test versus getting a meter to hack the NNemf in my environment.... Work (and spend) smarter not harder ....

The keeping a gap after eating of about 4-5 hours before bed, and then trying to get 8+ hours of sleep, surpasses my 12 hour window in eating. I have to eat every 12 hours since I do not have a gallbladder (removed 2009). I have learned since then (painfully), if I do not eat a decent meal within 12 hours of eating previously, I get extremely sick. My intestines feel like they are on fire and I get episodes of extreme diarrhea and then a state of extreme fatigue. My doctors and I feel like this is because since I have no place to store the bile anymore, if there is noting for it to munch on, as soon as something comes in, there is too much bile in my system, and it just suddenly evacuates my system. This has been happening since just after my surgery and since the.

How can I get around this for the Leptin RX?

Any exercises, specially energetic exercises, can only hurt you.
Body is very efficient engine, fat lost via exercise is nil.
But,
you can think of at least not increasing your body fat.
Try to eat minimal amounts of food.

we would have some window of information and what could possibly may slow down your pancrea.

nesidioblastosis ---->Inappropriately low levels of free fatty acids

Ask your doc to figure out what (free fatty acids) you are missing, and where you are high,
not in general, but in detail.
(I mean find each separate fat name, your current level of it and what it should be)
and when you are eating
eat what you are missing, avoid all else, specially those that are high.

Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
You would figure where you are low and concentrate on foods that contain that.
But that is about what is in your blood (that is separate problem).

Fats that we eat are processed in number of ways.
To implement those processes you need:
hormones, vitamins, minerals and other micronutrients.

Start with reading [https://www.jackkruse.com/easy-start-guide/](https://www.jackkruse.com/easy-start-guide/), there are lots of links to other pages about the leptin RX too, which could answer lots of questions and help you be more relaxed about trying it. Once you have the idea, and if you make an informed choice to do it, follow the instructions, continue with 3 meals as long as is necessary, and if your last meal is a bit later than ideal, to give you your current needed 12 hour window, then at this point don't stress about it. Do the best you can. My experience is that as circadian cycle improves, it gets easier. The liver is a solar organ, once it is running to its proper circadian cycle, your problem may resolve itself. Your longer term aim is to get your circadian cycle right, and inflammation low enough, for the leptin released from your fat stores to reach the leptin receptors in the brain whilst you sleep, to encourage fat burning whilst you sleep. It should signal a lowering of appetite, and help restore digestive and hormonal circadian cycles. I would not deliberately intermittently fast while you are leptin resistant, it is not part of the protocol, and could make things worse.

I'd also suggest starting a journal in the Optimal journal section, particularly if there's more of a history (such as the gall bladder issue). People who follow the optimal journal section but don't follow the labs forum are more likely to jump in offering their insight and experience.

I was just talking with a friend who previously competed internationally, and her husband still does compete internationally, about the difference in the power grids between the US and Europe. She said it is difficult for American athletes to fly to Europe and compete, but it is not as difficult for European athletes to compete in the US, and she thought the power grids may play into this.

Does anybody have any thoughts on this?

Thanks in advance

### JanSz — 2017-03-22T00:34:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/power-grids-and-international-competition.19261/post-210851>

Mitochondria frequency --100hz
USA power grid frequency--50Hz
Europe power grid frequency--60Hz

IIRC @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) discussed that.
But that would put USA residents at advantage when in Europe. (2x50=100)
\--------------------------
The other consideration would be a jet lag.
Assuming that USA residents would fly eastward, that is disadvantageous when comparing to westward flights.
[http://www.cnn.com/2016/07/13/health/eastward-jet-lag-worse/index.html](http://www.cnn.com/2016/07/13/health/eastward-jet-lag-worse/index.html)

My main issues: fatigue, balance problems, obesity, sleep disturbance, leg pain, back-flank pain, breast pain.

    -   Started tracking my meals and my water intake, focusing on Paleo eating

### Sue-UK — 2017-03-25T11:29:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-new-start-here-is-my-journal.19267/post-211045>

> [CFAN said:](/goto/post?id=211020)
>
> Why? What's wrong with Columbus? I am in a suburb of it.

Hi CFAN

One of the teachings is that location matters. It could be because someone lives at a latitude that does not get good sunlight or what sunlight they get is affected by smog, or it could be because they live in a population dense area where, even if they limit their own use, their cells are being bombarded with damaging wifi signals from the people around them. Or it could be that they live in a city that is on the early rollout list for 5G. There are lots of potential reasons, and where someone lives could be exposing them to several factors, that could affect how well someone does following the other principles of the teaching. If someone knows their risks, they have the opportunity to avoid those they have control over, mitigate those they can't by things like extra CT, taking a holiday in a warmer climate etc etc. Or ultimately some may, at a later date - if its possible - decide to move, if they don't see a chance of healing, or staying healthy, in the environment they currently live in. Its about the issues in context with someone's whole life, family issues, job, whatever .....

To give you a flavour ..... try taking the quiz in Reality 5. [https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/](https://www.jackkruse.com/reality-5-scompys-quantum-quiz-mito-hacking-life/). Just like your lab test, what you score, is your starting point. However low it is, don't panic. Once you've done it, you can look at the ones you didn't score on, and pick the low hanging fruit, ones that you have control of, and can do something about straight away. Some I can't change (I'm over 50), one I wouldn't change yet if I was leptin resistant (fasting), so think of it as a general guide, not a straitjacket. Your own context matters more than getting a top score on the quiz.

# Thread: Peeing as grounding?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peeing-as-grounding.19272/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-03-24T23:24:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peeing-as-grounding.19272/post-211030>

Does my bladder & possibly kidneys want to be regularly grounded?

I live on 3 acres between two other properties that don't seem to have wifi. That may be a reason why the basics of this site (light hygiene, CT, intermittent fasting, grounding...) seem to have worked very quickly for me and my wife.

After some reading, I'm regularly rethinking the most basic human daily actions. Since my acreage has significant privacy, I encourage my boy to pee outside. Urine is highly conductive. It seems like would be like a grounding rod into the ground and up into the bladder, which would normally be a fairly painful process otherwise.

Most people pee into a potty or urinal with a a plastic (non-conducting) P-trap.
So...
Does my bladder & possibly kidneys want to be regularly grounded?

I personally couldn't think of a better way to ground an internal organ (at least momentarily).

The incidence of prostate and kidney issues sure does seem to be rising...

I wear my Carbonshades and make sure that my skin is covered in light cotton and if I get on the computer to do my research then, I put my light hoodie on too and tie it around my face. Also, light cotton gloves that have the fingertips cut off, so that I can type. I don't do any kind of light therapy, using my UFO, etc., until later in the day. I wait for the sun to rise to start my hormone panel for the day. I go to bed early and after 7 or 8 hours of quality rest, I have to rise and shine  I do have a black uv light in the bedroom , just in case my husband needs help, which he did when he first came home from rehab. I had to see to help him turn with his brace on his arm, etc. I reserve that for emergencies only.

# Thread: Is there a concensus regarding the use of MMS ? (Miracle Mineral Solution)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 40
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Remo — 2017-03-25T21:05:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-211098>

> MMS is the general acronym term used throughout theworld to indicate many of the different uses of a solution of sodium chlorite in water. _Sodium chlorite is a mineral/chemical, that when mixed with a food grade acid generates_ **chlorine dioxide**. Chlorine dioxide kills nearly all diseases known inside and outside of the human body, and, it is chlorine dioxide which is the active ingredient used in most of our protocols.

Does anybody have experience using this substance ?

Someone adviced me to try this but I am still hesitating.

[http://www.mms-supplement.com/](http://www.mms-supplement.com/).

### Remo — 2017-03-30T00:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-211346>

nobody ?

I have never seen such a big divide in an alternative health protocol as in MMS. Forgive me for my own ignorance but I need your input.

Andreas Kalcker (physicist) seems like a smart and honest man:

Quote from a very informative site: [http://mmswiki.is/index.php/Basic\_Science\_of\_MMS](http://mmswiki.is/index.php/Basic_Science_of_MMS)

> The chemical ClO2 is a weak oxidizer with an oxidation potential of .95 volts. The oxidation potential determines the strength of the ability of an oxidizer, to oxidize other compounds. Chlorine dioxide is the weakest of all the oxidizers that are ever used in the human body. For your reference, oxygen has an oxidation potential of 1.28 volts, hydrogen peroxide has an oxidation potential of 1.80 volts, and the strongest oxidizer ever used in the body, ozone, has an oxidation potential of 2.07 volts.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-30T00:13:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-211347>

**I'll just put this here: Hope is the strongest tool a supplement marketer has. Stupidity of his customers is number two.**

### Remo — 2017-03-30T00:47:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-211352>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=211347)
>
> **I'll just put this here: Hope is the strongest tool a supplement marketer has. Stupidity of his customers is number two.**

Jack! thanks for responding. I agree with your sentence and vision regarding supplements. In general I do not consider myself stupid. Sometimes I just act like it

Im the first to admit that my chemistry/physics is downright awful. I come to you because I want to know _why_ you think Chlorine dioxide cannot be used as a tool to help kill of bacteria and funghi.

Chlorine dioxide is the active ingredient in MMS after it is activated by acid. The Chemical formula of chlorine dioxide is ClO2. That formula shows that there is one atom of chlorine (Cl) and 2 atoms of oxygen (O2) in a molecule of chlorine dioxide. These 3 atoms are held together by electrons to form the molecule of chlorine dioxide. Chlorine dioxide is a gas, and MMS are in most cases used as a gas dissolved in water. The ClO2 gas can be used at times directly on the skin, or even in the mouth. Chlorine dioxide is one of the most effective killers of pathogens such as bacteria, molds, fungus, viruses, bio-film and other disease-causing microorganisms, which includes the vast majority diseases of mankind.

Chlorine dioxide is generated from sodium chlorite, which is NaClO2. Sodium chlorite has a pH of 13 which means it is highly alkaline. When citric acid or most any other acid is added, they bring that pH down towards the acid condition, causing the sodium chlorite to become unstable and thus begin to release chlorine dioxide (ClO2) from the sodium chlorite (NaClO2). The more acid that is added, the more chlorine dioxide is released, and at a faster rate. That is until all of the chlorine dioxide is released. You can see that sodium chlorite is an unusual chemical that is made largely of chlorine dioxide, but the chlorine dioxide does not become available until the acid is added. As it was stated above, chlorine dioxide is a gas, but it dissolves in any water that is present or that it is mixed with

(Industrial strength Chlorine dioxide solutions has absolutely nothing to do with MMS.) MMS is only a few drops of Chlorine dioxide (or even just Sodium chlorite) diluted in plenty of water – it simply does not have the potential to bleach anything at all. That is ridiculous - to push the idea that MMS is a bleach, as no one has ever used MMS for a bleach.

Chlorine dioxide is unique in the chemical world because the chlorine dioxide molecule has a chemical characteristic that changes and makes it selective for pathogens. The change happens when the chlorine dioxide molecule pulls one electron from a pathogen, which normally is not enough to damage the pathogen, but that electron then comes over to the chlorine dioxide molecule and changes it. When this happens a thousand times or a hundred thousand times, the pathogen is destroyed, mainly because this process blows a hole in the side of the pathogen. There is another oxidation process which is called "chlorination", which involves the chlorine coming over and combining with the item to be oxidized, thus making new compounds. When this happens with chlorination, many times, one of the new compounds because of the chlorine involved, is carcinogenic (cancer causing). This information is just added for reference. Chlorination has nothing to do with MMS, but it might confuse some of the critics who happen to use Clorox bleach in their laundry. But again, chlorination has nothing to do with MMS.

Chlorine dioxide has the weakest oxidation potential (.95 volts) of all the oxidizers. Oxygen has the next highest oxidation potentials of 1.28 volts. Those oxidation potentials of .95 and 1.28 volts can do very little damage to the human body. _This is because these voltages cannot overcome the opposing oxidation potentials in the human cells and tissues. And as you can see, if oxygen does very little or no damage, then the lesser oxidation voltage of chlorine dioxide is not likely to do any damage at all._ Chlorine Dioxide is highly selective and does not harm stomach and intestine useful microorganisms: Chlorine dioxide is highly selective for pathogens that cause diseases in the body, and does not kill the various useful flora of the stomach and intestines. This is due to the unique properties of the chlorine dioxide molecule. Please see several explanations given in the references.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-30T02:32:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-211360>

Mitochonrial function is critical in running the detox programs built by 4.5 billion years of nature in our liver, skin, and GU systems. I'd rather you use what nature provided that a oxidation number based upon a chemical found outside of a cell.......you dont live outside your own cells so the oxidation number of this chemical is immaterial to the premise.

### Remo — 2017-04-09T22:46:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-212072>

> [Tammy Rogers said:](/goto/post?id=211863)
>
> I tried MMS many times and all it did was leave me with a constant ringing in my ears. Never again.

interesting Tammy.. Can I ask how long you used MMS ? Did you follow the official protocol ? Did you notice any other side effects ?

Keep in mind (I believe) that MMS, despite it's name, is not a miracle cure like its name suggest . It does not negate an unhealthy lifestyle or an abundance of EMF exposure/Blue light directly.

However, I do believe that MMS can be a very potent and powerful **tool** in overcoming infections and chronic yeasts/fungus which are increasingely linked to many of the neolithic diseases we speak of.

So far I have had mixed but interesting results:
I had a "keloid" scar tissue bump on my knee which was the result of a knee injury when I was a child. It was there for many years and this bump, how little it may be, was actually very sensitive to touch. Now, these things almost never go away from themselves but after using MMS it has started to decay ! really remarkable and interesting actually. It is slowly drying out and it seems to just fall of in the right time.

Also. I had a "calcified toenail" which just "scattered away" a few days after I started MMS. It definitely has an effect on fungus/calcium deposits (?).

After several days I became very nautious and had much diarhea. I persevered and this problem went away. My backpain cleared up a bit but most of my problems remained.

I do believe I need to follow the strict protocol for a while before I will see results. Enemas and detoxification are important steps in this.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-15T19:02:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-there-a-concensus-regarding-the-use-of-mms-miracle-mineral-solution.19276/post-348672>

When you add dopants to a semiconductor that aren’t part of the intended recipe, you’re essentially altering its electrical properties in ways that might not align with its original design. Dopants are impurities added deliberately to tune a semiconductor’s conductivity—n-type dopants (like phosphorus) donate extra electrons, while p-type dopants (like boron) create "holes" by accepting electrons.

If you introduce an unplanned dopant (supplement/peptide/drug/jab), a few things can happen depending on what you add, how much, and the base material (say, silicon or gallium arsenide).

First, the carrier concentration changes. If you accidentally dope an intrinsic (pure) semiconductor with an n-type dopant when it wasn’t meant to be, you’ll flood it with free electrons, making it conductive in a way it wasn’t supposed to be. If it’s already doped (say, p-type) and you add an opposite-type dopant (n-type), you could compensate the existing carriers—holes and electrons cancel each other out—reducing conductivity or even flipping the material’s type entirely if the new dopant overpowers the old one.

Second, the bandgap and electronic structure can shift. Dopants don’t just add carriers; they can mess with the lattice and energy levels. An unintended heavy dopant (like a rare earth element in silicon) might introduce deep-level traps—states in the bandgap that snag carriers and kill efficiency in devices like transistors or LEDs.

Third, crystal defects can pile up. **Semiconductors are finicky; their performance hinges on a pristine lattice.** Random dopants, especially at high concentrations or if they don’t fit well (wrong atomic size), can strain the crystal, creating dislocations or clusters. This tanks carrier mobility and reliability—think slower transistors or leaky diodes. Details matter in quantum biology and the marketers know you are all dumbasses.

What does the world of physics tell us about semiconductor contamination?

Real-world example: in silicon solar cells, iron contamination (an unintentional "dopant" from processing) acts as a recombination center, slashing efficiency by trapping electrons before they contribute to current.

Another case: if you’re making a p-n junction and sneak in an n-type dopant on the p-side, you’ll blur the junction, wrecking its ability to separate charge.

If you cannot charge them even going into the sun does not matter...........GOT IT? It is a big freaking deal.

# Thread: RF Survey of JK Members
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoeT — 2017-03-27T02:25:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/post-211178>

I thought it would be interesting to take a survey of what everyone's average RF exposure is in the workplace. We talk a lot about "EMF Hellholes," but has anyone really defined what constitutes good, bad, and "hellhole"? For the purposes of this thread, lets use Cornet's standard unit of measurement of MW/M2. The "safe" level for the general public (non emf-sensitive), as per Michael Neuert is .1.

My Environemnt
My workplace averages about .3
My home is actually really good in most spots, at about .005ish.

This got me thinking....I live 20 miles from NYC. Shouldn't I be seeing "hellhole" type levels? As an example, when I physically go to NYC, the streets of midtown register between 3 and 8, with intermittent spikes. That is pretty hellhole like i would say!

### JoeT — 2017-03-27T18:24:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/post-211224>

I'm a little surprised here...no takers on this??....I thought it would provide some interesting insight into our RF environments! ?

### JoeBranca — 2017-03-27T20:25:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/post-211233>

My workplace, specifically where I stand most the time, the Cornet hits between .15 and .55
Home seems to be an RF safe haven, it remains stable everywhere at .0005

However if it is the case that the Cornet does not truly pick up everything in the range it claims, I'll figure out another RF meter to get which picks up any gaps.

### JoeT — 2017-03-27T21:04:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rf-survey-of-jk-members.19282/post-211237>

Shame I didn't get any feedback on how it went, but it was very thought provoking setting up the programme for the volunteer.

### Rubicon — 2017-04-06T00:37:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-effect.19286/post-211843>

This LinkedIn post reminded me of something I saw on flicker a few weeks ago.

Creator of Iris, Daniel Georgiev, on LCD/LED monitor brightness and why your monitor flickers: [http://iristech.co/brightness/](http://iristech.co/brightness/)

**Summary**
Monitors that use Pulse Width Modulation (PWM) to reduce brightness introduce breaks in the display where the LED's turn off for a very short time. Those breaks create imperceptible flicker
Another method to dim the monitor called DC Dimming reduces brightness without causing flicker
It's possible to use DC dimming on your monitor with the right software on your computer and avoid flicker
Iris uses DC dimming

**How to eliminate flicker on any monitor**

1.  Using the buttons on the front of your monitor, adjust the brightness to maximum
2.  Use software like Iris to reduce the brightness to an acceptable level, as low as you'd like

That's it, your brightness is adjusted without introducing flicker.

Another option is to buy a monitor that does not use PWM to dim the screen so you don't get flicker. Daniel has a list of some monitors that might work on the Iris site: [http://iristech.co/flicker-free-monitors/](http://iristech.co/flicker-free-monitors/)

### Jason36 — 2017-05-06T17:56:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-effect.19286/post-213506>

> [Rubicon said:](/goto/post?id=213488)
>
> Thanks for the info @[Jason36](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19820/)
>
> In your testing, for a monitor that is flicker free were you able to confirm that Iris will lower the brightness and change the color temperature without introducing flicker?
>
> What method did you use to test flicker?
>
> I would like to test my own monitors.

I guess you could say my test isn't accurate. I am highly sensitive to flicker to the point where I can't even use a monitor or any monitor for that matter. I have struggled for 9 years using a computer with mostly having my computers hooked up to a Plasma TV because CCFL and LED kill me. I can barely look at a flickering screen for more then a few minutes without dying of eye pain. I can even feel the pain if a monitor is in my general vision but I am not looking at it. I have talked with a few people on the Internet with the exact same problem as me and I feel that a lot of people don't understand this issue correctly. I also understand it's hard to know about it when you can't feel the flicker. I have tested two CCFL monitors with IRIS plus one LED monitor that is supposed to be flicker free( I know the BENQ monitor I own is a dud because I have the same model at work and it is flicker free) and none of them worked. I even emailed the IRIS guy and he told me it doesn't work with all monitors. The reason it doesn't work is because lots of monitors use PWM even at 100% brightness. His program he is saying turn the brightness of your monitor to 100% then use the software to turn it down. That will not help if your monitor is flickering at 100% brightness. Alexander Wunsch says using an old cell phone camera is a simple way to test for flicker. Aim the camera at the screen and film it and you will see the flicker. Most new phones have a filter on them so you can't see the flicker anymore. I don't need to do this because I can feel the flicker immediately when I sit in front of a terrible monitor. My recommendation is just buy a flicker free monitor to begin with. I can confirm at work I have used Iris on my monitor at work and it doesn't flicker when I use the software to lower the brightness. If you buy a flicker free monitor you can just dim the brightness without it flickering anyways. I would use IRIS to turn the color temperature down to 3000 at night though not to disrupt your day/night cycles.

I believe more information needs to be done on screen usage and what is the best screen you can use. Hardly any of us can avoid it with our jobs and the poor kids in school these days. I am of the opinion that flicker free is not good enough. All these monitor companies saying their monitors our eye safe and good for the eyes because they don't flicker is ridiculous. All LED monitors our mostly the same in that the spectrum is garbage and it's all blue and no red. If you will pull up a typical spectrum of a Plasma TV you will see a giant spike in red and green. That is why I have never had any issues with these TV's. LED can't produce red or green. I believe that Samsung may have solved that issue with their quantum dot monitors. If you look at their spectrum chart it is almost identical to a plasma TV. Not to mention their quantum dot monitors our flicker free. The red color cancels out blue. OLED is also probably next solution if you can find it in flicker free. If you pull a spectrum chart of OLED you will see a little lower blue and a higher green and red. Alexander Wunsch also mentioned that when using OLED when the device is displaying black you are getting true black and not a closed shutter blocking light which is used in normal LED. Then you are not being directly exposed to radiation of the LED.

This is the color spectrum of a typical Samsung quantum dot TV. Now look at the color spectrum of a Plasma TV.

I believe the ultimate solution would be for a quantum dot monitor to be made with a 3000 color temperature instead of the typical 6500 that is used. I forget the name of the company but someone at made an E-Ink computer monitor. That would be the ultimate for most of your computer needs and just turn on a second display when you need color. I think the E-Ink was going for around 1300 dollars which is way too much money. My daughter is very educated when it comes different types of artificial light and I always hear about all the nonsense going on in the school. They make the kids all use these cheap netbooks with terrible flicker and high blue light. I bought her clear blue blocking glasses that filter up to 460NM the same ones I wear. She even showed the class when she put the lens of her glasses in the LED light of the projector how it deflects a bunch of the blue. She even tells me she notices when she goes to different peoples houses who use CFL bulbs and LED TV's how much harsher it is then our plasma TV's and Incandescent's. I own 3 plasma TV's because I despise LED

### JanSz — 2017-05-06T18:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-effect.19286/post-213508>

@[Jason36](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19820/)
It looks like Quantum Dot is the way to go with TV, possibly even using it as computer monitor.
Any help in finding spectrum chart similar to what you posted for Samsung quantum dot TV
but for Vizio M60-D1
I just talked with them, they do not have it.

.

How does that impact flicker effect considerations?

**Is OLED better or worst from Quantum Dot when considering flicker effect?**

### Rubicon — 2017-05-06T20:07:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-effect.19286/post-213517>

Thanks Jason! Awesome info.

Sorry to hear that you're a "flicker canary". I have an old friend that I believe likely has the same issue you do. A few years ago when LED monitors first became available he couldn't use them due to visual issues and was quite distressed. At the time I had no idea what he was talking about, but I think I'll try to contact him and confirm if he's got a visual flicker issue.

I did some flicker testing today and found:

\- a short video that shows what flicker looks like through an old phone: [Measuring Light Bulb Flicker with Nothing More than a Cell Phone](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Qym5-126BDY)
\- using the camera on an iPhone 4 I could see what looks like the flicker pattern on my lights, and most of my monitors
\- the iPhone app [Flicker Tester](http://www.visosystems.com/products/flicker-tester/) (worked to test an incandescent flood light on a dimmer)
\- the android app [Oulight](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=fi.aunio.oulight&hl=en) (Looks good but a paid app, not compatible with my devices, didn't try)

When measuring flicker with Viso from an incandescent flood light on a dimmer, it registered more flicker at the highest power setting than at the lowest:

### Jason36 — 2017-05-06T23:31:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-effect.19286/post-213536>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=213508)
>
> @[Jason36](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19820/)
> It looks like Quantum Dot is the way to go with TV, possibly even using it as computer monitor.
> Any help in finding spectrum chart similar to what you posted for Samsung quantum dot TV
> but for Vizio M60-D1
> I just talked with them, they do not have it.
> \------
>
> .

I think with TV I would go with plasma. If you can't do that then I might consider OLED. The problem with all these new TV's is they have wireless in them. That Vizio TV you are looking at looks harsh in my opinion.

IF you type spectrum then go to google images you can find quite a bit of info out. This was what I got when I typed your TV in. You want that red just as high as the blue or higher. Damn these new TV's Also you want to make sure they are flicker free. LG has OLED TV's that are flicker free. The problem with computer monitors is that we don't have OLED yet. I believe Dell has just released an OLED monitor for 5000 dollars but I bet It's not flicker free. So that really leaves us with quantum dot because all the other ones are just blue light hell.

### Jason36 — 2017-05-06T23:43:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/flicker-effect.19286/post-213539>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=213509)
>
> And there is OLED TV.
> OLED stands for Organic Light Emitting Diode.

>

>
> **Is OLED better or worst from Quantum Dot when considering flicker effect?**
>
> //
>
> ......

My opinion at the moment is OLED is worst for flicker at the moment. I believe this is something that needs to be researched because they could make the technology better. Samsung S2 is OLED flicker free and the Yotaphone 2 is OLED flicker free as long as the brightness is above 25%. As I am incredibly sensitive to blue light and flicker I can tell you there is a major difference in how I feel when exposed to regular led vs OLED. Also there isn't very many computer monitors that use quantum dot at the moment. We really only have Samsung and Phillips has a cheap one but it flickers as soon as you drop the brightness below 100%. Asus I think is dropping a line of quantum dot monitors soon.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-composition-tests-which-is-best.19291/post-211339>

Hi,
In the book, it mentions a DEXA scan. My doctor wouldn't order it since he believes it is only for osteoporosis. So I have to go outside him. I have found via another site's blog about private testing that a local medical university would be a good way to get it, though I would have to pay out of pocket. Luckily I live near Ohio State, so I can go to their world famous med school for it.

However, when I went to their site for pricing. They had some other options that intrigued me. I do not know their benefit vs JUST an DEXA scan. Whether I should try for all of them, or just some.

Can anyone advise? The other options are: skinfolds, hydrostatic weighing, and BOD POD

[http://eslab.ehe.osu.edu/body-composition/](http://eslab.ehe.osu.edu/body-composition/)

# Thread: Questions from the mind of a 7 year old.....
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 31
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stardust — 2017-03-30T01:39:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211355>

I try to explain the things I do and learn to my children. I have a 5-year-old and a 7-year-old. I was explaining to my 7 -year-old what cold thermogenesis is and why I will be doing it. She asked me if she could try it. I did not have an answer for her. I told her I would post here and ask. Is it safe for a 7-year-old to cold adapt? If so, would we use the cold thermo protocol?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-30T02:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211357>

She can. We have many kids who do better with it than their parents.

### drezy — 2017-03-30T02:32:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211359>

Given that the point of much of this is that us mammals can become cold adapted again, I went ahead and let my 12 yo son do it once a week. If we were in cold caves way back then our kids were too, right?

No problems 4 weeks in.

My boy is large for his size and is really broad shouldered. If he were smaller and really lean I'd decrease his time and/or temperature,but his latest CT was 56˚ for 40 min

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-30T02:33:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211361>

Every kid.......wants to go out with no clothes on or hardly any clothes when they young and its snowing. What and who stops them? Why? Conventional belief. Think about that. Does the other mother mammals on this planet do as the human primate mother with their young with respect to temperature?

### drezy — 2017-03-30T02:49:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211364>

Just won't look as cool without, The North Face, Patagonia, Arc'Teryx, or Marmot.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-03-30T17:00:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211399>

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

These are my two grandsons, and they are both cold adapted little guys. Like their Grandma, they love the cold. They know the routine of CT and warm up by the IR fire before heading outdoors to run around and boy do they laugh! They are 26 and 13 months in these photos that were taken in January of this year. It was 27 degrees with a slight windchill and the snow was just starting to fall and dust the ground. They also take cold dips in lake Michigan. Other than each having just a few slight sniffles that disappeared within a day or so, they have not been sick. Nature is their drug and they have thrived as a result of it.

### Mystic Rose60 — 2017-03-30T17:46:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211405>

Whenever I see the children in Russia indulging in CT, it makes me smile. The Russians know how to do it right.

### drezy — 2017-03-30T20:14:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211409>

@[Mystic Rose60](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/14425/)

Cool post and traditions with those grandsons.

It's been 9 or so years since a baby butt graced my house. I always smile and chuckle when a baby butt goes strolling by. It's a reminder to me that all these rules and concerns in my head are mostly of my own making. Baby butts don't care. Baby butts are like the honey badger.

### Dia — 2017-03-31T17:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211473>

As a little girl in JA, I remembered my grandmother doing some strange things that only now are making sense to me. One thing that stood out for me... I had a cousin that at three years old was still not walking. Every morning my grandmother would hold him down in a tub of cold water up to his waist, for about ten minutes or so, then she would put him waist deep into a hole that she had dug, and kept him buried there for about four, five hours. Fed him from the hole and everything. He would cry to exhaustion, then fell asleep standing up, while buried in this hole. After about 4 weeks of this torture, he started walking! It was crazy!

I think she would have been charge for child abuse if she had lived in North America, and did that.

### JanSz — 2017-03-31T22:17:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211487>

> [Dia said:](/goto/post?id=211485)
>
> Yea, you know what was strange as well... even though we lived in the equatorial region. Every single morning my grandmother would pour cold water on our heads for a few second, as soon as we wake up.

When one stays in one place for at least few generations, old folks wisdom is generated.
Your grandma learned her wisdom somewhere, locally.
Poland is not settled place for the last few centuries.
There are families that have multi generational roots, not that many.
My family was uprooted number of times and number of ways.
Grandfather spend his youth in New York State being there always temporarily so he could feed his wife and children that lived in Poland. He was ready to retire, then WWII happened. On my mother's site was even worst.
Things like that adds value to the family I was able to create.

..

### drezy — 2017-03-31T22:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/questions-from-the-mind-of-a-7-year-old.19293/post-211488>

> [Stardust said:](/goto/post?id=211355)
>
> I try to explain the things I do and learn to my children. I have a 5-year-old and a 7-year-old. I was explaining to my 7 -year-old what cold thermogenesis is and why I will be doing it. She asked me if she could try it. I did not have an answer for her. I told her I would post here and ask. Is it safe for a 7-year-old to cold adapt? If so, would we use the cold thermo protocol?

@[Stardust](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19767/)

You made me really think about your kid's approach. It's kind of a "What is it and and when can I try it?" approach.

Adults sometimes need 1 television doctor, 2 surgeon generals, and a few industrialized nations to acknowledge something before we maybe start considering it. This is often 40+years(half a generation) after first proof of a concept. Although I don't undergo massive regular testing I've come to understand my main issues and "check engine lights" in between testing. Not to pimp my own post but I place a high value in understanding how you personally breakdown and asked in this forum if others do [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...r-bodys-check-engine-light.19241/#post-210689](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/what-is-your-bodys-check-engine-light.19241/#post-210689) . If you have a good sense of these immediate feedback factors, you'll know in short order if anything you try here has basic benefit in between any tests.

You and I were taught science but your 7 year old is basically saying "Where's the data and let's see if we can replicate it." There's some natural/purer science! Encourage curiosity. TedEd has some interesting info. Even just today Jack mentioned Lynn Marguiles. Here is a kid friendly-ish video that kids might like on cell evolution that is a baby on-ramp for a portion what is unfolding here:

Kids might not get all the science, but they learn and absorb way faster than we even give them credit for.

# Thread: Hello From Canada!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-canada.19294/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason36 — 2017-03-30T03:35:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-canada.19294/post-211368>

I am so excited to have discovered Jack Kruse and the amazing knowledge he has regarding blue light and nnEMF and the damage it causes. I have been all over the Internet for 9 years looking for answers to my issues and finally I found someone besides Alexander Wunsch who is talking about how bad LED and CFL lights are for your health.

My 3 issues that I am trying to cure are flicker/blue light sensitivity and IBS. I think the flicker issue is my worst I am so frustrated with it. I have been trying for 9 years to cure it with expensive organic useless food that has done nothing except drain my bank account. When I discovered Alexander Wunsch I started going outside a lot through out the whole winter. Then about 7 weeks ago I discovered Jack Kruse on YouTube and I watched probably 30 of his videos while taking notes. I read Jack's book and am currently reading a book by Nick Lane called the Vital Question. Next I am going to read Oxygen by Nick Lane. I have half read the 4th phase of water by Gerald Pollack. I am going to read a bunch more books that Jack has listed in his podcasts and blogs.

So after the first night of watching videos from Jack I immediately went to findaspring.com and found a spring and have never missed a day of drinking only spring water. The interesting thing about where I live in Canada a bunch of the cities around here don't use fluoride in their water. I do understand why spring water is better though and will never go back to tap water. One of the things I guess I have always been doing right is eating fish. I almost ate sardines every day for the last 6 years. I also liked Sockeye Salmon a lot. I have increased my quantities on fish and am eating oysters a lot now. I'm guessing I didn't eat enough fish because I still have my flickering sensitivity to lights

I used to ground a lot and meditate outside but then the food gurus would have me convinced that food was the cause of my health issues. So I would waste my time and focus on food again and stop doing what was important to make myself better like get the heck outside! and CT! I have spent the last 7 weeks since I discovered Jack being outside in the cold as much as possible. Some weekends I would spend the entire day outside. I can tell you today was the first day UV was back because I could feel it in my eyes. It may have started a few days earlier but we have had quite a few cloudy days. This was a good thing because I feel great.

I have never had an issue going outside in the sun without sun glasses because I have not been wearing them for 5 years already. I figured this out on my own and never believed anyone who told me differently because I always used to say to my wife that I only feel good in natural light. I always feel terrible in fake light. I use only clear Incandescent light blubs in my house. I still haven't found the DC 12V lamp that Alexander Wunsch has recommended. I have used Plasma TV's for 5 years because of the giant spike of red light in them and because I can't tolerate anything related to LED and CCFL.

I have barely ever owned a cell phone because I can't use them because of the terrible screens and flicker. I used to own a Samsung S2 with an OLED screen that didn't flicker but couldn't tolerate it anymore when they changed all my lights at my work to LED. At the very least they got flicker free LED but even with the SCT orange goggles I find them hard to deal with still because blue light can leak in a few spots. I recently just got a Yotaphone 2 because it has an E-Ink screen. It barely ever gets used though and is never on my body. The wifi goes off every night in my house and everything is wired up. I want to get rid of the wireless router. It's just my wife who likes to use her cell phone on our wifi that is preventing that.

After all this studying with Jack I can pretty much pin point where everything went wrong in my life in regards to my health. It was back in 2008 when I was 28 years old I lived on the 17th floor of an apartment working night shift and had just bought a laptop and you guessed it would set it on my lap to use it. I don't think at that time I had any CFL(swirly bulbs I can't stand them!!!) lights in my house but my work was terrible with the fluorescent tubes. They had just switched them over to the newer fluorescent lights that flicker way more and that was the end of me. I also used to own a portable gaming system that had a CFL light and of course used PWM and that ended quickly because I had to stop using it.

The frustrating part was at that time nobody on the Internet had much to say about this issue. No doctor has ever helped me with this issue. I have pretty much just tolerated the pain of flickering for so many years. The only luck I had was finding forums of people with the same problem but nobody really having a solution to it. I am so happy to have found Jack and finally feel like someone knows what the heck is going on. I believe I need to recycle my Mitocondria so that they have an aquapora 4 gate. That is my goal. Jack thanks so much for providing all of us with this great information. You are changing the world!!!

### DLO — 2017-04-04T23:11:42+0300

Many NFL teams have now put their play books on iPads and the iPad screen has massive flicker effects when the screen brightness is altered

### StuartG — 2017-03-30T05:33:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct.19295/post-211373>

We’ve had a hot & sticky Summer in Australia and one of our ceiling fan remotes is always on the blink. Replaced the battery. Light’s ON but no action. Lucky the fridge is next to the room as it works perfectly now. Not sure if going to fridge more frequently is a good idea but everything runs better, Cooler. Lol

[](https://ibb.co/dxDCJv)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-30T15:30:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct.19295/post-211391>

So what is the success in this story?

### StuartG — 2017-03-31T06:27:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct.19295/post-211446>

A drop in temperature increases conductivity and DC flow. Faulty remote like low Redox and poor electron transfer.

### Dean6789 — 2017-03-30T06:31:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-california.19296/post-211375>

Hi Everyone,

I'm new to the Forum and have some fundamentals to get clear on as I begin some basic bio-hacking.

My first question is: I'm living in an apartment that was built about 60 years ago and has standard lighting fixtures with white painted covering/domes over all of the fixtures...

At the moment, I'm stuck inside a fair amount during the day --

FOR STARTERS, wouldn't it be best to remove all of the white domes and just expose the light bulbs underneath (they are all incandescent bulbs)?

Much appreciated!

### drezy — 2017-03-30T06:56:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-california.19296/post-211376>

Nothing is going beat the sun.

The domes will likely be small beans compared to the rest of your neolithic life.

If truly kept from the sun it might be worth checking out this [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-01T21:39:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-california.19296/post-211552>

welcome and drezy is correct.........redox is the key

### Dean6789 — 2017-04-08T05:54:14+0300

### nonchalant — 2017-03-31T19:16:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cosmosapiens.19300/post-211474>

Nervous systems
On page 399 of John Hind's book, he says flatworms were among the first invertebrates to exhibit bilateral symmetry (they have two parallel nerve cords running the length of the body, with eyespots at one end.) It is easy for us here to see that the eyespots would provide energy down both nerve cords. Hind says that the question of why bilateral symmetry is important hasn't been satisfactorily answered. But looking at the picture on the next page, wouldn't electricity traveling down the parallel cords make a nice magnetic induction field inside to benefit the worm?

And looking at the picture of the hydra, the nerve network surrounding the tubular body would create a magnetic induction coil. When it is firmly attached to the earth, it has plenty of energy for that mouth at the top and all the tentacles. Aren't tube-shaped organisms some of the first fossils, whether they had a nerve network or a bilateral system?

Speaking of eyespots... Wouldn't the eyespots create more energy for the invertebrate, to allow it to move more often, or even swim freely? Which came first, the eye, or the ability to swim (rather than drift)?

What is HAARP?

The High-frequency Active Auroral Research Program, or HAARP, is a scientific endeavor aimed at studying the properties and behavior of the ionosphere. Operation of the research facility was transferred from the United States Air Force to the University of Alaska Fairbanks on Aug. 11, 2015, allowing HAARP to continue with exploration of ionospheric phenomenology via a land-use cooperative research and development agreement.

HAARP is the world's most capable high-power, high-frequency transmitter for study of the ionosphere. The HAARP program is committed to developing a world-class ionospheric research facility consisting of:

The Ionospheric Research Instrument, a high power transmitter facility operating in the High Frequency range. The IRI can be used to temporarily excite a limited area of the ionosphere for scientific study.
A sophisticated suite of scientific or diagnostic instruments that can be used to observe the physical processes that occur in the excited region.

Observation of the processes resulting from the use of the IRI in a controlled manner will allow scientists to better understand processes that occur continuously under the natural stimulation of the sun.

Scientific instruments installed at the HAARP Observatory can also be used for a variety of continuing research efforts which do not involve the use of the IRI but are strictly passive. These include ionospheric characterization using satellite beacons, telescopic observation of the fine structure in the aurora and documentation of long-term variations in the ozone layer.

"Light-responsive agents offer the promise of targeted therapy, whose benefits include (i) prolonged action at the target site, (ii) overall reduced systemic dosage, (iii) reduced adverse effects, and (iv) localized delivery of multiple agents. Although photoactivated prodrugs have been reported, these species generally require short wavelengths (<450 nm) for activation. However, maximal tissue penetrance by light occurs within the “optical window of tissue” (600–900 nm), well beyond the wavelength range of most existing photocleavable functional groups. Furthermore, since multidrug therapy holds promise for the treatment of complex diseases, from cancer to neurological disorders, controlling the action of multiple drugs via wavelength modulation would take advantage of a property that is unique to light. However, discrimination between existing photoresponsive moieties has thus far proven to be limited." Protein kinase C and Zn work with mitochondria to get that done in neurons. Hydrogen fuel cells and batteries are both electrochemical cells. They each have two electrodes in contact with a material that can conduct ions, called an electrolyte. One electrode is the anode and the other is the cathode. **In a hydrogen fuel cell electrons are released from the hydrogen that is supplied to the anode whereas in a battery the electrons are released from the material in the anode itself.**

Because battery electrodes actively participate in the conversion of chemical energy to electrical energy, over time this can have a damaging effect on the electrodes and therefore on the effectiveness of the battery. Unlike batteries, the electrodes in hydrogen fuel cells are relatively stable since they act as catalysts in the release or acceptance of electrons and are not chemically changed during this process.
When pure hydrogen is used as the fuel, the only by-products generated from the fuel cell are pure water and heat. This makes fuel cells potentially very efficient devices with minimal environmental impact. DHA does this by switching them from M1 to M2 phenotypes in this immune cell line. This cell is very commonly found in the human GI tract. If you got a gut problem........you need the Epi paleo Rx. You need oysters. If you do not like them........then enjoy your illness.

This is a critical spot in autoimmune activation. We encounter inflammation almost daily in our species, but our body has ways of turning it off naturally. DHA is that switch in CMI in macrophages. This is an important step toward understanding exactly this happens. You're likely to be hearing a lot more about maresins when medicine realizes that DHA is an electron story.

In the July 2013 issue of The FASEB Journal, researchers found that macrophages use DHA to produce the chaperone like "maresins," which serve as the "switch" that turns inflammation off and switches on resolution in cell mediated reactions.

CITES:

Jesmond Dalli, Min Zhu, Nikita A. Vlasenko, Bin Deng, Jesper Z. Haeggström, Nicos A. Petasis, and Charles N. Serhan. The novel 13S,14S-epoxy-maresin is converted by human macrophages to maresin 1 (MaR1), inhibits leukotriene A4 hydrolase (LTA4H), and shifts macrophage phenotype. FASEB J July 2013 27:2573-2583; doi:10.1096/fj.13-227728 ;

[http://www.fasebj.org/content/27/7/2573.abstract](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.fasebj.org%2Fcontent%2F27%2F7%2F2573.abstract&h=ATPFOGh_9AU9-Tx01LHAzta7tbF3TXR0uqYLGGBl_1IM27abVX8GbBxScofqDl3qeYYGw8TkmgNFyCYI3V_EaTa8L7EJFbmibKjlJsKabRn4d1d8O-k5_BJKm53OSGTrGb7sVePdyA&enc=AZOqkkQNBUBr98yt2Me7OuNJpMDcHGZi5y41937VEjvDo5KsMLnYeGZJFHq2V8fMJY8ORna-U9NQFIalWZn_zkEg1nGZ8bzvV44BF2ua_fWUk0V5n6f815UL-UAoWA4-4r4PT-m4-Cle_jnAzxozryL3T2bCwyYzqyTs-0tyUt9XnGWrFwMQmEdLM7yAN3Ze5OK490Pqev4pETbuengTsI-y&s=1)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-01T05:59:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kinetic-alfven-waves-link-to-plasma-creation-in-mitochondria-h.19303/post-211519>

If we have a mitochondria that uses H+ hydrogen plasma as a fuel cell does that mean that maybe the sun is a electric star?

Because hydrogen gas does not occur naturally in the environment of Earth, hydrogen fuel must be derived from other substances that contain hydrogen such as methanol, gasoline, natural gas, and **water**. Nature chose water for cells, but man chose fossil fuels to get its hydorgen. Most hydrogen that is produced today comes from natural gas on Earth in manufactured hydrogen fuel cells.

If hydrogen is made from water the only byproduct is pure water. Now stop and think about what I told you a mitochondria makes.........CO2 and water.

When heteroplasmy rate is high human mitochondria produce more CO2 and less water because they are burning more carbon sources from food and not using H+ in the mitochondrial matrix. As a result these people will have more CO2 in their blood and gut. They will tend to burp more and fart more. They also will be dehydrated chronically because they cannot generate enough water from beta oxidation of fats. The more glucose they burn the more CO2 their mitochondria will make. The more fat they burn the water their mitochondria will make.

When hydrogen is produced from water, the electricity is used to split the water molecule. In chloroplasts sunlight does the job, but in mitochondria the electric plasma in the mitochondrial matirx where the H+ lies does the job.

**How does a hydrogen fuel cell work in a manufactured cell?**
Hydrogen gas is supplied to the anode of the fuel cell. The anode is coated with platinum, which acts as a catalyst to break down the hydrogen into protons and electrons. In cells they use phosphorus or phosphorus containing enzyme catalysts to do the same job.

If a circuit is connected between the anode and cathode then the electrons can travel through the circuit and provide power to any load that is connected as part of the circuit.

Reaction at the anode:

**2H2 → 4H+ + 4e−**

This is an example of an oxidation reaction.

The flow of electrons through the load is the electric current that is generated by the fuel cell.

The hydrogen ions (protons) that are produced from the hydrogen at the anode travel through the electrolyte in the fuel cell to the cathode. Oxygen supplied to the cathode reacts with these hydrogen ions and electrons arriving via the external circuit to produce water and heat, both of which are removed from the fuel cell.

Reaction at the cathode:

**O2 + 4H+ + 4e− → 2H2O**

This is an example of a reduction reaction.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
When you recommend seafood in the diet, I always think that there must be more to the story. And eventually it will be addressed in latter blogs.

For myself I figured that I should not go much wrong when I get my fatty acids analysis done and adjust my diet accordingly, being mindful to have good amount of DHA.
Is this acceptable approach?

It make sense to me,
but not completely, since it looks like people who are getting sick have very high DHA.
\----------------------------------------------------

In The BodyBio Detoxx Book by Patricia Kane
there is a number of case studies.
Most of those cases present with very high EPA and DHA on beginning of treatment and much lower latter on when the patient is in improved condition.

Case #18 is about Parkinson's (page 212)
It starts with:
EPA =578 %status
DHA=228 %status

Year latter it ends with:
EPA=191 % status
DHA=146 %status
\----------------

%status is calculated relatively to laboratory range.
At the middle of laboratory range %Status=0 (zero)
At the low or high laboratory range % status is (minus 50%) or (plus 50%) respectively.
So at the beginning there was
228/50=4.56 times excess of DHA
and
patient seen relief when her DHA dropped to
146/50=~3 times excess of DHA
so patient still had high excess of DHA, but likely it was somewhat tolerable at that level.

,,

### JanSz — 2017-04-05T16:08:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kinetic-alfven-waves-link-to-plasma-creation-in-mitochondria-h.19303/post-211802>

There are many different types of triglycerides,
with the main division is between saturated and unsaturated types

Triglyceride is an ester derived from glycerol and three fatty acids
membranes contain phospholipids
phospholipids=modified triglycerides
phosphate group replaces one of the fatty acids chains-->that happens on sn3 position
Sn1 position is taken by saturated fatty acids (most of the time)

Sn2 position, position of glycerol where unsaturated FA is added most of time. Exception—surfactant (dipalmityoyl phosphatidylcholine) has saturated FA at both sn1 and sn2 spots.

[https://quizlet.com/1604789/phospholipids-flash-cards/](https://quizlet.com/1604789/phospholipids-flash-cards/)
\-------------------------------------------------------------------------

DHA is unsaturated fatty acids
DHA must go to sn2 position

**Why there is so much emphasis about DHA, that it works properly only when it is in sn2 position?**

\=======================================================================

# Thread: Andrea's journal: Alive
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andreas-journal-alive.19311/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Andrea — 2017-04-04T13:10:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andreas-journal-alive.19311/post-211713>

I can as well start a new journal...

First, a glance back at where I'm coming from:

In the beginning of the previous century, my great grandfather was an electrician who took part in electrifying the city where he lived. When he asked my great grandmother to marry him, she replied "Yes, mr. J., I will marry you - if you promise that our home will be as bright during night as in daytime."
Unfortunately, she got what she asked for.
My grandmother was conceived in this chaos. She was a "difficult" kid with ADHD like behaviour, and all her life she suffered from lung problems (severe asthma/allergy) and continuous skin problems (eczemas and other).

Fast forward to me:
I was a smart kid with high IQ, and I was athletic and strong. In my 20's I was ridiculously fit. However, all my life, every spring and summer I suffered from photodermatosis (skin rashes anywhere on my body when exposed to intense sunlight) and my eyes were sensitive to the sun. I was also allergic to grass, nickel, pollen and some other things.

Not knowing any better, I consumed loads of aluminium and fluoride for years.

At age 29 I moved to a larger city with cell towers on every other rooftop and more wifis than I can count, my lifestyle became more "modern", and within a few years I became fat and fatter, more and more tired, more and more brain dead. Whenever I did something physical I broke: tore a muscle, hurt a joint, got a bruise - always some new injury. I couldn’t even bring home a grocery bag without hurting myself. The docs that I asked for help all told me to eat less and excise more, which is what I did - until I barely ate at all and still kept getting fatter.

In 2011 I was diagnosed with hypothyroidism and was put on synthetic T4 and T3 and cortisone. The docs were shocked at my extremely high LDL and I barely managed to escape statins. Also at that time, they found several anomalies on my ECG and ultrasound, and my cardiologist was seriously worried.

A couple years later I found dr. K.'s site.
I changed a few simple things in my lifestyle. It made a difference, but not good enough. Then I made some less comfortable decisions and changed more...

And now:
\* I haven't taken any meds for more than 28 months.
\* My ECG looks normal.
\* My LDL has dropped to a more normal level.
\* I can wear my old clothes again.
\* My allergies are gone.
\* My red-green colour blindness is gone.
\* I am more at ease with myself and life than ever.
\* Some of my old sports injuries are causing less trouble and less pain.
\* My balance - which was impaired after a concussion in my early 20's - is more or less restored.
\* Last year I didn't have any signs of photodermatosis whatsoever and this winter - after two weeks at 16th or so latitude - I had the best, deepest tan ever.
\* I feel alive!
...
...

So what's next?
I want to keep living. And I want to keep healing my body and brain, my mitochondria; I still have a long way to go - and I am curious about how far I can go... I want to learn to hear my intuition, to listen to it and to follow it. Explore love. Love...

I hope that one day I will get a chance to look you in the eyes and say it to you in person. And I do have a question for you...

### Andrea — 2017-04-19T07:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/andreas-journal-alive.19311/post-212554>

I got a surprise recently, when I had my eye sight checked.
When reading letters of smaller and smaller size, they say that 1.0 is full good vision, and the number goes down with age and reduced vision.

When I did my first check, that I remember, at age 17, I had 1.25 on each eye, which they said was exceptional.
In my early 30's, after way too much time in front of computer monitors and out of the sun, I was down at "normal" 1.0/0.9.

Now I'm 40+ and... 1.6. The optician couldn't believe it. They had never encountered anyone who could read those tiny letters during their 35 years in the business - and they didn't have smaller letters for me to read.

I had previously noticed that my vision in the dark has improved remarkably the last few years, but this was very unexpected.

So, I'm older than ever and I can see much better and sharper than ever? Go figure...
