# Thread: What it takes to be a leader
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-it-takes-to-be-a-leader.19318/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### drezy — 2017-04-05T22:40:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-it-takes-to-be-a-leader.19318/post-211835>

"What it takes to be a leader" often sounds daunting, important, and complex.

I'd love to be a leader of my own mother's exploration of the mitochondrial approach here. I'd personally lop off a few fingers if that could be the case. I'm also trying to be the leader of my son who is experiencing the awkward 12 yo world. I'd lop off more appendages if I could just get him to understand that lots of people just don't matter and that you have to stay focused on your goal. If I got these two wishes I'd be pretty appendage-less.

I've worked into leadership positions and fallen into them. All of us who have experienced an improvement of condition are in a leadership position, like it or not. Once you "arrived" there is no solution, salve, or quick fix. There is no fan-base, no cheering crowds, and no blatantly clear victories.

I don't have the answers to how you lead the changes you want, but I found historical context to be useful.

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) has to sometimes tell people to not look at [the finger that's pointing at the moon](https://essenceofbuddhism.wordpress.com/2016/04/19/what-the-finger-pointing-to-the-moon-analogy-really-means-from-zen-buddhism-the-buddha-in-the-shurangama-sutra/). I think leading any kid of mentality change,which we all seem to want, involves that. There is a kitsch and sometimes shallow messaging in the west around images or statues of Buddha. I guess it's supposed to evoke some idea or another.

The Sekhiyas in buddhist monastery text illuminate what may have to be involved in leadership. These were "rules of conduct" established by Buddha himself for his monastery. From the most enlightened being and his devout followers sprung this divine rule: [don't chew like a cow](http://en.dhammadana.org/sangha/vinaya/227/75sk2.htm#ch----50). Straight from the Buddha there. I had to pass up the "Don't shake food particles on fellow diners" and "don't dribble stuff out of your lips". My point is here is this enlightened being arriving to a monastery after a journey that might kill you or me and establishing extremely basic eating etiquette end up being a task the "enlightened one" just has to tackle up front. It sounds kinda Marine commander level, but I think that may be floor 1 of where leadership starts.

My only point in writing this is that I suspect that, if you want to lead change, you may have togged down to the Marine commander/Buddha level of non-antiseptic human interaction.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-06T01:08:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-it-takes-to-be-a-leader.19318/post-211848>

Leaders speak a language all their own. While the words used might be in your language, or any other commonly used language, Leader speak delivers a different message than when similar thoughts and sentences are conveyed by others. Words are like nets; we hope they'll cover what we mean, but we know they can't possibly hold that much joy, or grief, or wonder. Leaders measure their words to make maximum impact to the audience they are speaking to that particular day.

### drezy — 2017-04-06T02:11:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-it-takes-to-be-a-leader.19318/post-211849>

Just sayin... A guy like the Buddha had to descend to the level of asking people to not chew with their mouths open or flick food particles on others.

Every time I've successfully lead others, way more menial hand holding was involved. Whenever I failed as a leader is when I lost touch with the menial (and often seemingly demeaning) aspects of what the task entailed.

Your policy of not wasting energy on people who don't really want to hear natural wisdom related to the sun makes total sense to me.

I am related to people that are highly valuable to me that don't necessarily want to hear this all yet. I've made peace with the possibility that they just never will. This thread is about acquiring and condensing the leadership required to start to change those most beloved but most stubborn minds of your members. Again, it may be completely futile and I'd be happy to become a Sisyphus for the people I most care about.

Am I possibly the one putting my mask on first in a malfunctioning (cultural) plane wondering if things could have been different? Highly likely. I wasn't looking at all into any of this and stumbled like a big idiot into this whole perspective. My benefits are obvious to me and to some of those around me that are somewhat observant.

Still I feel unaccomplished in leading this change for some of the people in my life who could most use this knowledge. My little inter&post generational family get it and sun & CT with me. I'd love, however, to reach down and cinch up my cahones to get my mother/mitochodrial donor to follow suit. That's really the tender/painful reason I started this thread.

Getting your own oxygen mask on is great. What next? How best to influence?

I would like to better lead those in my life.

# Thread: Complex trail of breadcrumbs... need direction
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/complex-trail-of-breadcrumbs-need-direction.19330/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Livingbliss — 2017-04-11T06:47:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/complex-trail-of-breadcrumbs-need-direction.19330/post-212133>

I found JackKruse.com today with a few simple search terms... fatigue, circadian rhythm, and maybe mitochondrial dysfunction or craniosacral....

Short story: 16 yo athlete gets braces (orthodontics), suffers fatigue, weight gain, goes low carb for a week (unrelated?), fatigue worsens or continues, braces are tightened, suddenly fatigue is debilitating (can't get out of bed), nausea and strange sleep cycles - jet lag type of effect continue..., rapid (unexplained) weight gain = 60 lbs in 4-5 months, craniosacral treatments seem to do wonders but weight loss is elusive, life is "changed" no longer participating in her sport (rowing)... goes to college, few months later is back to debilitating fatigue, upside down sleep cycles, etc.

Please help! I see there are some answers here... and direction for what to do.... but it is very difficult to find a concise set of instructions of where to begin... I would like somewhere to point my daughter (turns 20 this Saturday)... and also just a handful of instructions that are manageable for her. A magic first step to get her out of bed, awake enough to function....

Long story (wrote this but figured short story was needed): 3 years ago my then 16 yo daughter suffered a sudden, debilitating onset of some dis-ease. She was an athlete - a rower - being recruited for college and at the top of her class of athletes in our region. She got orthodontic braces in January and soon started suffering from heavy fatigue. She was working out 2 x a day, 6 days a week and trying to improve her erg score - also attending an early college program that kept her up late doing assignments and up early to work out. I attributed the fatigue to pushing herself too hard. In Feb I noticed she was gaining weight which was odd because she had cut out a lot of carbohydrate (definite carb junky) in an attempt to loose a few pounds to help with her erg score (indoor rowing machine) which is weight adjusted... she was not grossly overweight but had a muscular rower body type.

In early March she had her wires tightened with her braces and a day later her fatigue was so bad she couldn't get out of bed. She continued to gain weight (few pounds a week). Her coaches didn't understand. Most doctors didn't understand. They talked about depression and about cutting calories and cautioned me that she could be hiding food. I know my kid likes sweets and carby foods but I also know what she was eating and it didn't add up. She didn't have access to buy food and it didn't fit with her personality.

Once I made the connection with symptoms and the timing of her braces, we went to see a naturopathic MD pediatrician who had performed a variety of tests on her the year before. She helped us with supplements to detox her body from metals (nickel?) which I believed to be part of the problem. But the doctor also noted that her tests had shown some mitochondrial dysfunction.

She missed 3 months of school and we thought she was some better but summer was easier because she wasn't pushed to go anywhere. When school started again in the fall it became evident that she wasn't better.
I met a craniosacral therapist who, after hearing my story, suggested that the braces might have disrupted her craniosacral fluid flow/system... this was new to me but a quick web search for craniosacral, fatigue and weight gain yielded an article on nitric oxide production and mitochondria, which was enough for me to get her in for one treatment to judge the effects. The effects were amazing. My daughter, who's circadian rhythms were way off unable to sleep at night and super tired in the daytime, fell asleep easily and woke up like the energizer bunny. Amazing... and subsequent treatments were needed every few days for a while... but less and less often.

Still, she had gained 60 pounds in a few months and that changed her life. She was better but not the same. She still suffered from fatigue, especially during times of stress.

Recently, as a college freshman, she has returned to a deeply fatigued state that prevents her from attending class, staying awake, studying, thinking clearly etc. I think she is flunking out, doesn't know where to go for help or what to do. So here I am, looking for answers and advice to pass on to a teenager who is to tired to hear but needs help.

### WalterNL — 2017-04-11T08:33:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/complex-trail-of-breadcrumbs-need-direction.19330/post-212135>

Couple of things are low hanging fruit for college students: wear blue blockers after sunset & only drink reverse osmosis or spring water. Then she wants to spend more time outside in the sun, if possible while grounding (touching nature). Ideally she would get sunshine on her eyes first thing in the morning to set the circadian clock, but sun later in the day is good too. A large breakfast is important to further help the body learn proper circadian timing. Sleeping should be done in a room that is cold, quiet, completely dark and close to the earth (ground floor if possible). Lastly, non native EMF's like from cell phones, wifi, etc might be big problem. There shouldn't be any wireless electronics in the bedroom, and use of them should be limited to when it's absolutely necessary.

Those are some basic ideas to start with. She most likely has to tweak things further after finding out what works best, everybody is different.

### Dimitri — 2017-04-11T15:38:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/complex-trail-of-breadcrumbs-need-direction.19330/post-212141>

I messed myself up my freshman year of college and got adrenal fatigue which left me tired but wired all the time. Had problems falling asleep and was hard for me to get out of bed in the morning. I went on the paleo diet and took a bunch of supplements and that helped but didn't fix the problem. It wasn't until I started paying attention to circadian rhythms when I started feeling better.

Getting morning sun and wearing blue blockers was the key for me, and prioritizing sleep. I don't know if your daughter necessarily has adrenal fatigue but I would suggest looking into brain gut 16 [https://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/](https://www.jackkruse.com/brain-gut-16-adrenal-fatigue-rx/)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-11T16:40:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/complex-trail-of-breadcrumbs-need-direction.19330/post-212150>

Fixing the light her eyes and skin sense is the key first step........

> [Livingbliss said:](/goto/post?id=212133)
>
> (indoor rowing machine)

minimize indoor

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-25T15:04:50+0300

> [Livingbliss said:](/goto/post?id=212133)

>
> Short story: 16 yo athlete gets braces (orthodontics), suffers fatigue, weight gain, goes low carb for a week (unrelated?), fatigue worsens or continues, braces are tightened, suddenly fatigue is debilitating (can't get out of bed), nausea and strange sleep cycles - jet lag type of effect continue..., rapid (unexplained) weight gain = 60 lbs in 4-5 months, craniosacral treatments seem to do wonders but weight loss is elusive, life is "changed" no longer participating in her sport (rowing)... goes to college, few months later is back to debilitating fatigue, upside down sleep cycles, etc.
>
> Please help! I see there are some answers here... and direction for what to do.... but it is very difficult to find a concise set of instructions of where to begin... I would like somewhere to point my daughter (turns 20 this Saturday)... and also just a handful of instructions that are manageable for her. A magic first step to get her out of bed, awake enough to function....
>
> Long story (wrote this but figured short story was needed): 3 years ago my then 16 yo daughter suffered a sudden, debilitating onset of some dis-ease. She was an athlete - a rower - being recruited for college and at the top of her class of athletes in our region. She got orthodontic braces in January and soon started suffering from heavy fatigue. She was working out 2 x a day, 6 days a week and trying to improve her erg score - also attending an early college program that kept her up late doing assignments and up early to work out. I attributed the fatigue to pushing herself too hard. In Feb I noticed she was gaining weight which was odd because she had cut out a lot of carbohydrate (definite carb junky) in an attempt to loose a few pounds to help with her erg score (indoor rowing machine) which is weight adjusted... she was not grossly overweight but had a muscular rower body type.
>
> In early March she had her wires tightened with her braces and a day later her fatigue was so bad she couldn't get out of bed. She continued to gain weight (few pounds a week). Her coaches didn't understand. Most doctors didn't understand. They talked about depression and about cutting calories and cautioned me that she could be hiding food. I know my kid likes sweets and carby foods but I also know what she was eating and it didn't add up. She didn't have access to buy food and it didn't fit with her personality.
>
> Once I made the connection with symptoms and the timing of her braces, we went to see a naturopathic MD pediatrician who had performed a variety of tests on her the year before. She helped us with supplements to detox her body from metals (nickel?) which I believed to be part of the problem. But the doctor also noted that her tests had shown some mitochondrial dysfunction.
>
> She missed 3 months of school and we thought she was some better but summer was easier because she wasn't pushed to go anywhere. When school started again in the fall it became evident that she wasn't better.
> I met a craniosacral therapist who, after hearing my story, suggested that the braces might have disrupted her craniosacral fluid flow/system... this was new to me but a quick web search for craniosacral, fatigue and weight gain yielded an article on nitric oxide production and mitochondria, which was enough for me to get her in for one treatment to judge the effects. The effects were amazing. My daughter, who's circadian rhythms were way off unable to sleep at night and super tired in the daytime, fell asleep easily and woke up like the energizer bunny. Amazing... and subsequent treatments were needed every few days for a while... but less and less often.
>
> Still, she had gained 60 pounds in a few months and that changed her life. She was better but not the same. She still suffered from fatigue, especially during times of stress.
>
> Recently, as a college freshman, she has returned to a deeply fatigued state that prevents her from attending class, staying awake, studying, thinking clearly etc. I think she is flunking out, doesn't know where to go for help or what to do. So here I am, looking for answers and advice to pass on to a teenager who is to tired to hear but needs help.

The answers here were obvious.....over training with no light controls and then you put braces on her teeth which are all made of transtion metals.......drew the nnEMF toward them and french fried her PVN in her brainstem and cause massive adrenal fatigue and acute rapid leptin resistance. The answer.....remove the braces, fix the light cycles, stop over training and she needs a strong light cycle of sun to overcome and recapture her mojo. She will need cold water pelagic fish to gain that mojo with the sun and she needs very good water to help facilitate it. Replace all the crazy training with CT. She needs a TON of CT.

She now has to do more than most people to right the ship.......if not she is headed for a major HPA axis disease, AI or depression. Her D3 and BUN/creat ratio are likely horrible. I bet her melatonin cycle looks horrible and since this control mtDNA and heteroplasmy rates that would be indicative of why she gained 60 pounds so quickly

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-25T15:30:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/complex-trail-of-breadcrumbs-need-direction.19330/post-212908>

"Humans inherit mitochondria from their mothers and mtDNA through the oocyte. In a human female embryo, the first primary oocytes develop from the primordial germ cells from two to three weeks into the process of embryo development. As reported by various scientists, the number of mitochondria in the primary oocyte ranges from fewer than ten to two hundred. Robert P.S. Jansen in his 2000 article "Germline Passage of Mitochondria: Quantitative Considerations and Possible Embryological Sequelae" reports fewer than ten mitochondria per human primordial germ cell. However, by the time the female infant is born, each primary oocyte has approximately 10,000 mitochondria per cell. There is another tenfold increase in mitochondrial number during adult growth and development. For most female mammals, the mature oocyte has from 100,000 to 600,000 mitochondria. The amount of mtDNA in each mitochondria in the female germ-line is slightly more mtDNA than the number of mitochondria. Ovarian insufficiency is associated with major depletion of mtDNA in the oocyte." [https://embryo.asu.edu/pages/mitochondrial-dna-mtdna](https://embryo.asu.edu/pages/mitochondrial-dna-mtdna)

EXECUTION takes INITIATIVE and INTELLIGENCE. EXECUTION is the antidote to PROCRASTINATION. Ideation without execution leads to deletion of any idea. A poor idea executed accomplishes more than a great idea that stays locked away in a person’s head. Small changes can lead to massive non linear changes in cells because of the physics of how cells work. This idea offends the unripe mind, but it makes a lot of sense to people who use the three legged stool to reverse an issue. EXECUTION and action calls off the fence of indecision and put us in the valley of decision; it calls us off the bench and onto the field. It does matter how much talent you have as a player in your life, but that talent is useless with inaction because you can never score a goal while you’re on the bench. Ideation without execution leads to deletion of every good idea your recovering brain and body intuit.

### Sue-UK — 2017-04-12T11:00:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/meditation-what-are-your-suggestions.19332/post-212210>

> [Lindasmullin said:](/goto/post?id=212172)
>
> im aware of negative thought loops
>
> That keep me in the same behaviors
>
> I do breath meditations where I'm following my breath. But I'd like something to help me change my negative thoughts. I am noticing more when I go to Oh this is hard I'm not getting better etc
> And right there say stop and mentally say positive thoughts to myself
>
> What programs or cds can you suggest
>
> Anyone use dr Joe dispensa cds??
>
>
> Thank you. Linda

One thing I have done in the past is have 3 choices of what to do with a thought that arises during meditation. If the thought is negative and doesn't serve me I send it up into the universe for healing, just let it go and watch it go into the light. The light is healing. Then ... if it comes back send it up again ... If its something like I haven't paid the electric bill, I put it into a mentally visualised pidgeon hole, I'll deal with it later. Wait for the next one ... if the thought is something nice, like a pleasant memory, or thoughts of a loved one etc, I think it, and enjoy it, I physically smile and even swallow the smile, it becomes heartfelt .... I'm not fighting my thoughts, I'm not struggling to do it "right". It is what it is (sometimes a lot of repeating negatives), but I have a choice on what to do each time a thought comes up. I can also choose a thought, especially to finish with .... pick something that makes me smile, or fills me with gratitude, or love.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-12T06:02:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-am-beginning-the-journey.19334/post-212202>

Welcome.........

What should the ill person do at the beginning? They should be competent on what they do not know now. but the are beginning to realize is important on the journey of discovery of what is optimal for wellness. What does a competence creator do? They innovate because they know they are unique. They take what is known and they connect it in few ways other could ever conceive. They do this because they think differently and carry unique perspectives. Innovators make use of the simplest ideas. Innovation is a two edge sword; it can dull or excite but both are master teachers for the creation of competence. Commit to what excites you, and what excites you will provide the answers for your ideas to connect in new ways. Success come because of the innovative failures we will face on any chosen problem we look to solve. Wellness quotients rise when you double your failure rate in mito-hacks. This teaches us the value of opportunity and risk simultaneously. The creative mind plays with the object it loves and tries to improve it. Serial mitohacking eventually creates the genius of the mitochondriac. Be unique and be different. We have bred too many people with a monoculture of thinking here. Discovery consists of simultaneously observing the same thing as everyone else sees and thinks and thinking of something different and innovative. Facts alone, do not define science. They are but a current approximation of mankind's level of understanding in the present moment. We need to be reminded of this constantly. Science is never settled. Consensus is the business of politics. Science should always be anti-consensus. **A consensus in science is pseudoscientific.** If we've reached a consensus of what is and is not true, then we cannot call it science. That is called belief or dogma.

To become healthy you must first unlearn the things that you think are ture that just are not true. That is the first step to wellness.

MARCH 6, 2017
Under a court order, last week California public health officials released draft guidelines advising cell phone users to keep phones away from their bodies, use speaker phone and limit use. The guidelines, first reported by the San Francisco Chronicle, advise Californians that scientific studies have linked electromagnetic radiation from long-term cell phone use to an increased risk of brain cancer and other health problems...

...The California Department of Public Health had to be forced to release the guidelines by a lawsuit from a University of California, Berkeley, researcher and says the guidelines don’t constitute its official position, but this is a major development in the debate over cell phone safety. [http://www.ewg.org/enviroblog/2017/03/california-cautions-cell-phone-users-keep-your-distance](http://www.ewg.org/enviroblog/2017/03/california-cautions-cell-phone-users-keep-your-distance)

### Scompy — 2017-04-13T01:55:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hordes-meet-courage.19337/post-212239>

One has to wonder, where are the hordes of teachers speaking out about nnEMFs? Oh yeah, they are zombie hordes looking down at their cell phones and tablets...(facepalm)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-13T04:20:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hordes-meet-courage.19337/post-212249>

Teachers could care less........they are more worried about their job not anyone's health due to their job.

### Scompy — 2017-04-13T04:28:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hordes-meet-courage.19337/post-212251>

I agree... plus, tablets and devices make good babysitters for them so they don't have to teach. It's quite pathetic what the school system is turning into... any teacher with half a brain will stand up to their ilk.

### drezy — 2017-04-18T21:43:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-hordes-meet-courage.19337/post-212519>

> [Scompy said:](/goto/post?id=212251)
>
> I agree... plus, tablets and devices make good babysitters for them so they don't have to teach. It's quite pathetic what the school system is turning into... any teacher with half a brain will stand up to their ilk.

Despite the fact that I clearly remember the education majors in my college days partying at least 6x as much as any other major as well as the fact tat I was never invited, I'm going to go ahead and throw out a little defense of teachers here Scompy.

This is kinda built-in and not so much a teacher issue to me. Think about the sensationalization of every few startups that go big and the fact that nobody hears about the other thousand that fizzled or flopped in the same year. With this kind of messaging out there people, parent specifically, seem biased toward thinking that STEM (science technology engineering & math) must obviously mean someone sitting at a screen designing or analyzing something. This does have a trickle down effect. I have no doubt that a school district full of parents like anyone here on the forum would be different, but go to a school and look at what the parents themselves are focused on while waiting to pick up their kids.

I think many of the teachers really do think they are helping STEM development by involving tablets. Anecdotally, I spend a ridiculous amount of time working with my boy on his academics and it shows in his grades. I'll admit to a mito no-no of sorts. When my son had spelling quizzes I'd work with him and his progress was slow as molasses. After a couple weeks i wrote a very simple terminal program that would speak the word, wait for him to type it, and eventually weed out what my son nailed regularly. It also showed him a little graph at the end of each session. The graph was ugly and made up of a sequence of "\*" characters like "\*\*\*\*\*\*\*" where each ugly little character accounted for a 5% hit rate. I'm convinced that once my son saw this little "live" graph it was game on and his score immediately moved for the 80-90% range to 95-100% range from then forward. Judiciously applied, the technology is really helpful.

Everything that is being pushed onto teachers these days is promoting the STEM (aka 0.005% of startups) perspective. The reason that is being pushed is that it's being requested explicitly or implicitly by parents or an attempt to "parrot" some other "more successful" school district. Light, water, and magnetism for my family and the herd can run wherever it wants. It won't catch us under it's hooves.

Alexis De Tocqueville wrote that all Americans seemed to posses “a clear, free, original, and inventive power of mind.” We're still building "better mousetraps" and getting our fingers stuck in them along the way. Not to get all eugenic on you, but my advice for all of us is focus on your own.

Every time I'm feeling particularly quixotic I just reread the story of Ignaz Semmelweis. He tried to help physicians stop spreading disease and ended beat to death in an insane asylum.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### kris90 — 2017-04-13T15:18:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-amen-regimen.19339/post-212267>

I wasn't sure where to post this since I don't have access to the Ask Jack section, but wanted some opinions. The interviewer (Markus) seems to be a silly guy trying to promote and sell vegan products/herbs, but I found the interview interesting nonetheless.

It seems unlikely that this man can maintain his physique and hold a world record in deadlifting while only eating 1 meal a day that is 1000-1500 calories, but based on a lot of principles preached by JK himself, this man seems to be optimizing his environment and circadian rhythm. Some examples is that he trains right before dinner every night which allows a massive increase in protein synthesis, and the fasting increase autophagy allowing for metabolic efficiency and recycling proteins rather than replacing them.

The guy also seems to live like a tree and live in nature, does a lot of meditation, etc. He probably harnesses a massive amount of energy from nature, thus requiring very little food for energy.

Thoughts? Do you think this guy is legit?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-13T17:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-amen-regimen.19339/post-212270>

Circadian biology always trumps food regardless of your fuel choices.

### kris90 — 2017-04-13T17:24:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-amen-regimen.19339/post-212271>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=212270)

Boom

Higher quantum yield = thriving on less food

# Thread: Journal of a man with a brain on fire
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 60
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Remo — 2017-04-14T20:18:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/post-212329>

Hello my friends,

I will try to keep this short as possible

My problems in life started as a little kid. I had terrible exzema and nightsweats since I was about 3-6.
As far as I can remember my panic attacks and depressions started when I was about 10 years of age. At that time I was considered a very gifted youngster showing creative skill in writing and mathmatics, which came natural to me. My panic attacks, combined with hot flushes, increased reaching a point in which I experienced them daily.

Enter highschool: the mental problems continued and even worsened until they accumalated into an adrenal breakdown. The only thing that kept me going was eating junkfood. They were the only thing that could lower my stress and give me temporary energy to go on. Getting my Gymnasium highschool diploma.. it is quite a miracle that I was able to finish it considering in what state I was in at the time. The years and years of stress, mental depression, panic and pain eventually took its toll when I collapsed at the age of 22.

I had to quit my studies and eventually got bedridden. For some years I could barely do daily activities. The simplest of things would tire me out. By this time I had already seen and tried many types of doctors but the main concensus was that they could't find the main reason I was so ill.

When you are mocked, ridiculed and plain out laughed at you will quickly learn the game of life which is adaption. So I did. I accepted my autism stamp in the hope that it would buy me time to find out what was really wrong with me. Of course, I had and still have very severe mental issues, but to suggest that all of my physical complaints where caused by autism felt, and still does, like insanity.

So I went and started my own journey which eventually led me here

\------------------------------------------------------------
Some Keynotes regarding my health and problems:

\- I have already gained significant gains in my skinhealth. I suffered from bad skin, acne, exzema etc. for years. It seems that it was a lack of DHA and iodine from raw fish that did the trick. Wow. I used to had one of the worst skins in my family.. now I have one of the best. Raw Herring is a gift.

\- my main problems now are:
1\. Physical fatigue. Worse when I eat carbs. Worse when lack of sun. Worse inside. So I try to avoid these but its not easy.
2\. Spine/Back pain + variococele. Kind of a shameful condition but I am learning to live with it. Painful though.
3\. Bi-polarity.
4\. Sugar cravings.. especially when im in or nearing Keto state. Usually I can go without binging for weeks until bam. Definitely a mitochondrial issue ?
5\. Lymph issues. whenever my body is at the point of breaking out a sweat I get the pain of a 1000 needles and my skin becomes inflamed. It resides if I keep sweating. However it starts all over again next time.. so im reluctant to do sports anymore.
\-----------------------------------------------------------
Some of the labs I did in the past which I thought stood out, normal ranges first followed by results in **bold**. I could use some help trying to identify what these mean:

\- NO (nitrogen oxide (NO): 18-35 value **79,3
\-** Progesteron : 0,63-79,60 **1.10
\-** Candida lgG : <12,5 **37,4**
\- DHEA-sulfate : 0,94-11,70 **6,62**
\- Arabirose : 0,1-47 **29**

\- Lymfocyten : 17-43 **59
\-** Neutrophile Granulocyts: 45-75 30

\- N-Acetylcysteine : 0,02 - 0,28 **0**
\- Pyridoxic (B6) : 2 -26 **0**
\- B2 : 0-2 **0,37**
\- B7 : 0 - 12 **0,68
\-** MalonAcid : 0 -16 **13

\-**2-hydroxyb-acid : 0-2 **2,5**
2-oxo-glutateacid : 15-200 **14**
5-hydroxyindoleacetic : 0-20 **0,35**
CitricAcid : 180-560 **114**

I am not sure if these are still useful or relevant but maybe some of you see something Im not.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

My protocol so far:
\- atleast 1x raw fish a day. Herring is my go to.
\- atleast 3x a walk outside, getting as much sunlight as possible. Start just after breakfast at sunrise.
\- blueblocking glasses, blue filters etc.
\- earthed sleeping
\- taped mouth sleeping to avoid hyperventilation at night (most of us do this without knowing)
\- concious buteyko breathing (nasal breathing)
\- cold showers always
\- Infrared Sauna 1x a week (need to do this more often but I need to save up for lightbulbs)

I notice an insane difference in energy when im out in the sun. It literally charges me up for 2/3 days after which I just get ill again.

What I think I can still do to improve:

\+ Infrared and UV indoor lights
\+ more sauna usage
\+ going deeper into keto using more raw fresh fish

**Now I need to mention that I live in Holland which is an EMF hellhole. Afaik no western country has such a high limit concerning radiation.** I sadly do not have any power over the control of my router (I live with my folks) so my own place is an EMF hellhole as well :/

### nonchalant — 2017-04-14T20:53:44+0300

I used to had one of the worst skins in my family.. now I have one of the best. Raw Herring is a gift.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/post-212357>

@ Caroline:
my grandmother (mothers mother) is a very vital person still doing sports in her late 80"s. None real health issues that I know of except some dental replacements.

my mother on the other hand has had a similar journey to my own having been very ill at a young age. She was allergic to cows milk and had to stay in the hospital several times since she had such great stomach pains. She developed arthiritis in her 20's which is a bit better under control now after I pushed her to go more outside and eat more seafood.

I resemble my mother allot with her complaints with the exception that she has quite allot of energy and no mental problems.

What I did around that time was to follow a paleodiet using allot of carbs (sweet potato) and combined that with Buteyko Breathing + intense sweating exercise. Looking back my improvements were mostly due to buteyko and the sweating (which increased my leptin sensitivity and skyrocketed my Buteyko CP, which is in indicator of your cellulair oxygen content). Interestingely enough I now see just how important profound sweating was for my positive times.

I stopped using Buteyko methods mainly because they were so incredibely hard for me to do + there was controversy using it if you had a Lyme infection (which I think I had/have but which isn't easily proven).

### Inger — 2017-04-19T20:43:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/post-212600>

Well I do sometimes use reptile light and infrared bulbs. But not too much, not daily. In winter I do try to use them a little every day. Like when I dry brush and shower in the morning I put them on for about 15 minutes
Sometimes I use UVB/UVA tanning beds. I did not use them this winter tho, except maybe 2 times, because I was so broke and they are pretty expensive here

Now spring is here and I go outside when sun rises and try to spend all morning outside preferably nude if sun is out, like it was today . I am so lucky to start work later, like I have to leave at 10:40 AM, so I have plenty of time to sun in the mornings which I so love  Mornings are so beautiful, and no one is outside, except me, my cat and the birds

I do travel to the sun (French Riviera) a few times/year to fill up a bit with sunshine  The sun is so awesome there! Also very cheap flights from Hamburg, just 1,5 hour flying time.
I need to do it more. Seriously it helps a lot, if I feel sun depleted and stressed. Spring and autumn are my favorite times there, less tourists and perfect temps for me

### Inger — 2017-04-20T11:24:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/post-212632>

I use Solar Glo, got them from Amazon  I think I have the 150 watt? At least I got me the strongest one  The one with UVA and UVB and IR. I also have blacklights in my home that I have on when inside when I remember  I do not have them close to my body tho, just far away, because they radiate pretty strong magnetic fields..... try to keep them a few meters away.
The solar glo does that too... but you will have to keep them a bit closer to get the effect.... That is why they are not optimal at all.. they flicker.. and radiate nnEMF . Still I feel a nice boost when shortly in front of them when I cant get natural light

I live in a small town with only 400 people
I can tan nude on my terrace in the mornings, because my landlord that live in this house too, never go out so early
I do keep a towel or some clothes by my side in case they will come outside tho, and OI do have to watch a bit around..lol But usually they never go outside the house before 9 AM  Good for me

I also have a big forest nearby, just 5 minute walk from me.
There is a tiny lake where I do tan and swim nude in summer  Very little people there... and if someone comes I just put my bikini on, I can see from far if someone comes, you know
There is a man walking his dog around - and he has seen me 2 times tanning nude..lol He says I do not need to put my bikini on at all! Haha.. I still do tho.... when he comes with his dog  It is not a official nude beach......
We have nude beaches here, plenty of them. I work on a half island, where I pick my oysters. There is nothing but one restaurant and the sea...full of oysters... and sheep are grassing  There is a place on that beach where people swim and tan nude. It is beautiful!

The only downside to this area IMO is that here are so many windmills......
That is why I am looking for another place to live. I think windmills are not a good thing at all...

Please don't live in fear of what your neighbors may think and do Remo. If you have the will to nude sunbath, there is always a way. You can use tarps and my favorite is, I invested in a few of those plastic folding picnic tables and I can strategically place them by me as needed. I won't let anything stand in my way of nude sunbathing. When I am at our little haven on lake MI and others are on the beach, I head up into the woods and go to the sun openings and nude sunbathe there. Last year I was laying by a hemlock tree and there was some hissing and stirring around coming from underneath the tree. I though it was a deer that was bedding down for the day. I told it that there was enough room for both of us in this forest and I wasn't there to harm it. As I laid back down to sunbath, it became quiet again, but only for a minute. The animal hissed and jumped out from underneath the hemlock and it was one pissed off skunk! Thankfully, the little guy didn't spray and he scurried off in the other direction, leaving a slight scent behind. I had a good laugh and I laid back down to harvest my sunshine. I could have been fearful and left, but as I said, won't let anything or one get in the way of my nude sun time. My life depends on it!

### Remo — 2017-04-22T20:08:14+0300

interesting! I found somebody who could relate with my "stinging/burning sweating pain stings" and he told me it was common for Lyme patients because neurotoxins get stored in fat tissues if your liver cannot control the toxic load. The skin gets the same treatment in which toxins/heavy metals etc. get dumped in there causing a variety of skin issues: exzemia, acne, itch etc.

Using this explenation it makes perfect sense why my skin has cleared up so much the last months: it got detoxed from all the toxin buildup from the sulfur + DHA/Iodine in fish/seafood. After the detoxing agents in the skin the stored fat is next to be detoxed. When I started going keto these attacks became much worse.... which could be because the extreme fat burn that happens in deep ketosis causing a rapid release of build up toxins! Apparentely there has been some serious toxin buildup in my belly fat (im athletically slim build but I learned this doesn't mean anything) but im positive I can get rid of it.

Doing sports outdoors this summer will help greatly becoming leptin resistance again.

\- Interestingely: small amounts of weed really _does_ help improve chronic pain and mood.
The only downside is the extreme food cravings you get as a result. Last night I had a fantastic evening with friends where I smoked some weed and ate a ton of (bad) food... yet I woke up wonderfully and happy for the first time in weeks! Friends, laughter, relaxation, weed.. they seem to even counter a ton of bad food.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/page-3#post-212737](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/page-3#post-212737)
Deficiency Symptoms:
Pantothenate deficiency symptoms are thought to be uncommon because of widespread
distribution in all foodstuffs. However, human deficiency symptoms may include:
fatigue,
depression,
**burning feet,**
dermatitis,
**burning or pain of arms and legs,**
anorexia,
nausea,
indigestion,
irritability,
mental depression,
fainting,
hair loss,
increased heart rate, and
susceptibility to infection
\------------------------------------------------
Note large variety of names:
pantethine
pantetheine
pantothenic acid
pantothenate
Panthenol (also called pantothenol)
vitamin B5

[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panthenol](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Panthenol)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantetheine](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantetheine)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantothenic\_acid](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantothenic_acid)
[http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantethine](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pantethine)
\------------------------------------------------

If your problems are skin deep you may easily attempt to hack it using some of the creams containing
DL-Panthenol
.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/page-4#post-212746](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/optimal-journal-debra.19042/page-4#post-212746)

### Remo — 2017-05-02T19:12:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/post-213248>

this question is asked allot but.. I am looking for some advice on which IR/UV lightbulbs to get.. I understand that it depends on where you live etc. but a general suggestion would be super useful (for me and the other newcomers).

### WalterNL — 2017-05-03T10:10:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/post-213275>

> [Remo said:](/goto/post?id=213248)
>
> this question is asked allot but.. I am looking for some advice on which IR/UV lightbulbs to get.. I understand that it depends on where you live etc. but a general suggestion would be super useful (for me and the other newcomers).

Well it's difficult to find out exactly what would be right for our location. We'd have to measure sunlight spectrum throughout the day for a few years and based on that design a bulb that delivers the right spectrum each day.

But anyway there are full spectrum lamps like Exo Terra Solar Glo 160W or Osram Vitalux 300W. These will get you both IR and UV (and visible of course), irradiance depending on how close you are to it. I have not found other full spectrum lamps. There are many IR lamps out there, usually with detailed specs so you know the frequencies used. UV lamps are made by producers like Exo Terra, Komodo or Sperti, and there are also handheld UV-flashlights.

For each lamp it's important to know the irradiance and spectrum, because we need the whole spectrum of light that passes through the atmosphere and we need enough power to have effect. It changes depending on location and season, but as I said it's difficult to find out exactly what that would be for us. On top of that you need to experiment for yourself where you are lacking in terms of light, so what you may need to supplement.

### Penny — 2017-05-03T19:49:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/post-213310>

> [Remo said:](/goto/post?id=213248)
>
> this question is asked allot but.. I am looking for some advice on which IR/UV lightbulbs to get.. I understand that it depends on where you live etc. but a general suggestion would be super useful (for me and the other newcomers).

[http://www.rubyluxlights.com/](http://www.rubyluxlights.com/)
I use the rubylux - I have their UV/IR and just IR - 660nm+415nm - I also use this amazing IR light:
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0067S8IZ8/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0067S8IZ8/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)
You also need this cord with it:
[https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000VRL632/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000VRL632/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

I like it because I can stick either light on the crook of my neck and just hold it or prop it up - the cord is long and I can target any part of my body with it - back of the head and it's lights out for me

I also use a red incandescent light bulb which weirdly helps me sleep too - but I have major sleep issues - you might not need to go where I have to...
And lastly, I have a couple of these on my casablanca fan which I run with the red incandescent lamp if I read in the evening - that helps me sleep too - I must be a train wreck...
[https://www.amazon.com/Sunlite-SL20-BLB-Spiral-Blacklight/dp/B002P4TYVK/ref=sr\_1\_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1493830052&sr=8-1&keywords=uv light bulb
](https://www.amazon.com/Sunlite-SL20-BLB-Spiral-Blacklight/dp/B002P4TYVK/ref=sr_1_1?ie=UTF8&qid=1493830052&sr=8-1&keywords=uv+light+bulb)
As far as selenium goes, I have found that eating brazil nuts is more bioavailable than the ol' LEF selenium - in fact, research has confirmed that - oysters are also loaded with selenium and zinc - more sleep hints

little update:

\- ups and downs like usual but I have hit a low spot yet again. Getting all kinds of neurological symptoms lately , ranging from eye problems to face nerve issues. Nobody really takes me seriously but thats fine as it is human nature to most. I am highly suspected to have late stage Lymes disease but since I was not spotted on my GP tests some years ago it will be tough to get additional testing done.

Neurological issues//Bells Palsey//Conjuctivitis//Chronic Fatigue//Balance issues//Suicidal thoughts, all these things still "come and go" despite me following Jack's protocols.
Sometimes I have "fantastic days" where almost all of my symptoms just dissapear. Almost always due to the presence of very cold water + sun ( sea ). I am therefore trying to go swimming more and see if it helps for the long term but im not seeing a bright future. Even simple things that should be relaxing like reading/watching a tv show are hard to do.

### Remo — 2018-04-24T23:51:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-man-with-a-brain-on-fire.19343/post-239870>

been a while since I posted here. Mixed developments im afraid.

Found out I had (severe) neurological Lyme affecting my whole brain area and causing 9/10 of my symptoms. I started treating using "rife machines". It seemd to have quite some positive affect on my "Body" , reducing many of my symptoms like pain and fatigue, but my brain... the problems have gotten worse. The "brain fog" " extreme depression " "emf sensitivty" are all still there to the max. Over the last months I developed heavy pressure behind my right eye which comes and goes. Sun definitely decreases it (sun = lymph movement = sweating = feeling better). My doc thinks they are brain lesions caused by the lyme.

A year ago I said I would try and research "MMS". I can hereby say that "MMS" is the only thing, beside intensive sunlight + sweating, that can rid me of this incredible pressure behind my eyes/brain. However, both are temporary relievers and I have to do a longer more strict protocol to see the true effect of MMS on neurological late stage Lyme. The MMS has also helped tremendously with energy/pain reduction. After this personal experience I am intrigued in why it is working and if it can actually aid me in killing the bacteria for good.

Any tips on battling neurolyme are welcome btw

### ValerieBee — 2017-07-23T23:38:29+0300

This was my first Jack podcast. I loved the interaction, and Luke's description that he was exploring the science behind his intuitions. That's my journey as well. I'm rusty on my science, having left my B.S. behind about 15 years ago for other pursuits, so right now the most ah-ha moments I am having are when I see the spiritual concepts I've been exploring and experiencing come to life for me through quantum biology. This is how I'm hooking myself up to the Science again. I had become so disenchanted with Science because it was bucking my intuition (especially in health care), and because I saw so much bias and BS through this Peer Reviewed Publication validation crap. I ended up working in the law, specializing in health care rights, so I was constantly pushing against Science. The Dogma of Science is entrenched in the court system so deeply it is immeasurably disappointing and heart wrenching. So many people are failed and even hurt by the dogma that is blindly followed by "justice." So this is yet another reason I am truly rejoicing to find Dr. Jack's work, and this community of people! This is true science, as it should be. It is true exploration and truth-seeking and wellness. All of that spirit was in that interaction with Luke Story.

New weekend blog. Nature is always fundamental in health but no one wants to understand it is at is smallest levels to see why we are so off track. This is why I tell people to still read my blog even if the do not understand quantum physics. What I write is important for all living things. The goal of the blog has always been to convince you not to turn away your attention because you do not understand the material. It is to draw your consciousness in to peer into how nature really works and light your curiosity on fire. QED has always be relevant to me but its concepts are not self evident , nor parsimonious until you see the world through nature's laws first. The problem for mankind is that they don’t look at biology through a quantum lens.........but they must. I am just putting some windex on their glass eyes. This links says the same thing with alternative words......but the intent is the same.
Become addicted to the things that nature does. It is the only way we can make it through the things we choose to do and remain healthy. Nature is the only ‘straight edge’ when you are the architect of your blueprint for life………We only command nature when we obey her ruleshttps://[www.jackkruse.com/reality-10-als-neuro-degeneration-soft-tissue-sports-injury/](http://www.jackkruse.com/reality-10-als-neuro-degeneration-soft-tissue-sports-injury/)

# Thread: Why are women dying of opiate addiction?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-women-dying-of-opiate-addiction.19347/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-15T17:29:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-women-dying-of-opiate-addiction.19347/post-212354>

They are designed to be more sensitive to EMF's to program the germ line and set the mitochondrial programming in materal mtDNA. When you consider the modern world is now blue lit and microwaved 24/7 you can see why their is a deep environmental link.

The number of women dying from overdoses of opioid painkillers increased 5-fold between 1999 and 2010, according to new data released on July 2, 2013 by the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).
CDC director Tom Frieden, MD, MPH, said the following during a media briefing:
The problem of prescription opioid drug overdoses in women is "getting worse and getting worse quickly."
Deaths due to opioid drugs have "skyrocketed in women."
"Mothers, wives, sisters, and daughters are dying from overdoses at rates we have never seen before."
"The increase in opioid overdoses and opioid overdose deaths is directly proportional to the increase in prescribing of painkillers."
Opioid prescriptions are "increasing to an extent that we would not have anticipated and that could not possibly be clinically indicated."
The CDC now says that since 1999, the percentage increase in painkiller-related deaths was actually greater among women than men (400% in women vs 265% in men). Prescription painkiller overdoses killed nearly 48,000 women between 1999 and 2010. More than 6600 women, or 18 women every day, died from a prescription painkiller overdose in 2010
There were 4 times more deaths among women from prescription painkiller overdose than for cocaine and heroin deaths combined in 2010. In 2010, there were more than 200,000 emergency department visits for opioid misuse or abuse among women — about 1 every 3 minutes.
Previous research has shown that women are more likely to have chronic pain, be prescribed prescription painkillers, be given higher doses, and use them for longer periods than men. The higher doses in women are "something we don't really understand," Dr. Frieden said, "given that, on average, women weigh less than men, and at the same or higher dose, they are more likely to have adverse events than men."

### Joe Gavin — 2017-04-17T14:32:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-women-dying-of-opiate-addiction.19347/post-212440>

Opiates provide that "soft, warm hug" that many humans are missing. We could extrapolate that women may be more attracted to this type of "hug". Doesn't leave a smell on your breath, rather small so can be hidden easily, and in a weird way more socially acceptable (ala Mother's Little Helper). Of course repeated use will lead to single-pointedness and delay discounting, to the exclusion of alternative goals. One bad day, the extra pill is taken, perhaps a bit more booze too, and the mito shut down. Brutal how our species is so caught up in the illusion of mind, aided and abetted by blue-light driven programming.

The opiates aren't used every day, just for the 'bad days'. I sure wish I could get them to eat more seafood. Or in the case of the woman, any seafood at all. Ice or cold sets off a flare of reaction, so she won't contemplate CT. I think alpha MSH and also DHA would sure help with their generalized pain. She did take some D-ribose I brought her to help with sleep, and told me it helped her leg pain quite a bit. Hello all,

Name is Jonathan and I am glad to have found this site. I found it on accident when I was researching NAD+ boosters because for the past 18 months I have been dealing with chronic brain fog and I have spent tons of money getting tested and have tried everything from supplements to diets to lifestyle changes but nothing has helped. I have been told it was a side effect of TMJ, it's CFS, it's leaky guy, it's adrenal fatigue, etc. and I have tried to address those issues but nothing is providing any relief. I am still just a newbie to this site and all the information on it so I am still not entirely sure whats wrong with me but I am starting to realize that I think this site might finally have the answer I have been looking for. I just purchased Jack's book and am making my way through the podcast section while also trying to digest the Adrenal Fatigue RX...not sure if that is where I should start or if I should be trying to make my way through the Leptin information. I look forward to fixing myself with the help of this site so I can finally get my brain back.

### JanSz — 2017-04-16T06:05:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-az.19348/post-212377>

TMJ, it's CFS, it's leaky guy, it's adrenal fatigue, etc.
\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
It looks like it may be worthwhile to figure out if your problem is TMJ
or something else.

'''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''''
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temporomandibular\_joint\_dysfunction](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temporomandibular_joint_dysfunction)
**Temporomandibular joint dysfunction** (**TMD**, **TMJD**) is an umbrella term covering [pain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pain) and dysfunction of the [muscles of mastication](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Muscles_of_mastication) (the muscles that move the jaw) and the [temporomandibular joints](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temporomandibular_joint) (the joints which connect the [mandible](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human_mandible) to the [skull](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Human_skull)). The most important feature is pain, followed by restricted mandibular movement,[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temporomandibular_joint_dysfunction#cite_note-Mujakperuo_2010-2) and noises from the temporomandibular joints (TMJ) during jaw movement. Although TMD is not life-threatening, it can be detrimental to [quality of life](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quality_of_life),[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Temporomandibular_joint_dysfunction#cite_note-Shi_2003-3) because the symptoms can become chronic and difficult to manage.

\=

### JohnnyAZ — 2017-04-16T10:53:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-az.19348/post-212386>

I can definitely start a journal.

My family has always had a history of autoimmune diseases. I don't know much about my grandmothers health other than she was a smoker and died of an aortic anuerism at 77. My mother was an autoimmune mess with Hashimoto's and she was always struggling with weird conditions no doctor was ever able to diagnose (vertigo, tremors, tinnitus). She ended up dying of adenoma carcinoma of the lung. My health was great until I turned 13 and we moved to a new location. At that point I developed many digestive issues that no doctor could make sense of. When I was 22 I started to get symptoms of fibromyalgia and CFS. I struggled with this for years but through diet and exercise I really only have the CFS symptoms now. I quit smoking about 2 years ago and ironically it was shortly after I quit that the brain fog began (often have wondered if there was some connection in this but can't find anything to validate or deny this suspicion). You didn't ask...but all my aunts on my mothers side have various issues as well and my one aunt died of hemachromatosis while the other has serious anxiety issues.

I have been reading all sorts of things on this site and started with the Adrenal Fatigue RX on the home page but wondered if I should have started with the Leptin one. I can move over to that one starting tomorrow.

I have not read Ott's book as I thought I should start with Kruse's book first. If you feel I should start with Ott I can purchase it now.

I started going outside in the morning this week and looking to the east but my backyard is all concrete and I can't seem to find if my feet need to be planted on natural ground or if standing on concrete will have the same effect.

I currently live in Arizona and I am a district manager so I spend two days in front of a computer and the other 3 days working out of my car and driving from location to location. I am curious to find out nnEMF of cars and if this is a good or bad thing that I am out and about.

My diet has ranged over the past years as I try to eat natural but I have also experimented with my diet. Diet seems to have no effect on the brain fog as far as I can tell.

My libido exists but it is odd. I want to be intimate but quite often lack the energy. I read today in the adrenal reset that this could be due to the oxytocin release which is why I desire it but my energy feels sapped by evening.

I have not yet removed the electronics. Since I was a child I have slept with the TV on and I now realize how detrimental this is. I am going to remedy this immediately but I fear insomnia (I have been an insomniac since I was young and having the TV on was the only thing that helped as I was able to ignore my random thoughts and listen to the TV while falling asleep). I will remove the electronics this week though and will just have to adjust to a new routine.

I don't know what a smart meter is.

Can't get well in the same place I got sick? Does that mean I have to move? That will be an issue for a while.

I do feel I have come to the right place as well.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-17T01:40:55+0300

### JohnnyAZ — 2017-04-17T03:38:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-az.19348/post-212422>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=212417)
>
> Welcome......it is clear the mitochondria you got from your mom are likely a real issue. Tells us more about your move when it occured and where it was from and where did you go and get sick.

I have moved several times in my life and now that I think about each new home brought new problems. When I was 13 I moved from the Northwest suburbs of Chicago to a more rural setting. The location, from what I am reading, sounds like it might actually be an improvement as we were a little more off the grid and lived on a 5 acre lot. However the house was brand new and our finances were improving so we had things like big screen tv's and even bought our first computer. At 21 I started commuting to UIC in downtown chicago and I would ride the metra train daily for over an hour each way. During my time at this school many odd health things started to creep up like serious joint pain in my neck. At 26 I moved to Arizona to escape the winters of IL as I have always hated being cold. To this day I am overly sensitive to cold in any facet. Our first year in AZ was filled with constant head colds and respiratory/sinus issues. At 27 we moved to a new house in AZ and things took a serious dive. The people who lived in the house before had major health issues and one needed oxygen but I don't know why...I always attributed it to mold as shortly after moving into the house we found a massive site of mold under a slow drip in the water heater that had rotted the surrounding wood. I have learned from a podcast that mold thrives in a low magnetic field so not sure if this house falls in that category. Its a newer home built in 2001 and in a nice neighborhood but I do know my neighbor has had major heart issues and I include that as it may be important. I hope that provides the details you were looking for Jack.

As for the car I drive its a 2016. Because my work requires I put on about 40k miles a year they provide me with a vehicle. It is a 2016 Equinox.

Everyone,

I forgot to mention my biggest drop off into this world of brain fog came after a schedule change. Looking back I have had symptoms of this issue creeping up for years but in August of 2015 my twins started school. Our schedules were drastically rearranged and we all started needing to get up at 5:30 since I needed to help my wife get our highschooler to school. Since I was up and could not start work yet I started working out in the morning from 6:30-7:30 instead of going from 4-5 like previously had been for several years. I did a cortisol test a month ago and after my morning trip to the gym my cortisol plummeted below what is should be at even 8 PM. Not sure if this is relevant or not.

This morning I stood in the dirt barefoot and looked to the east. I also slept without TV. Starting Jack's book today.

### JohnnyAZ — 2017-04-18T06:49:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-az.19348/post-212474>

Honestly the sleep was rough. I have an extremely active mind and I struggle to control it. I have been sleeping with the TV on for 23 years as it helps me to focus on that instead of thinking about random things from a decade ago (etc.). Not giving up...its just going to take some time I assume.

No gym huh? I am a bit of a gym junkie. I have degenerative discs in my neck and fibro like symptoms that flare up whenever I take time off from the gym. Maybe it placebo or something unrelated but the gym has been a staple in my life for a solid 5 years regular. I live in the very desert region of AZ. There are desert trails in our neighborhood but again our neighborhood could be part of the issue. We get out several evenings for softball practice for my daughter and usually we go to a local park and I try to ground as much as I can while there.

Today I worked from home and took several breaks to go out in the sun. I wore my blue blockers every time I was on the computer but took them off when I was not. I felt good for most of the day until I got in my car to run an errand. After about 5 minutes in the car I started to feel off. Luckily I was only in the car for awhile and ended up back at home working with my glasses on. Later I was coaching at my daughters softball game and suddenly I noticed the fog was creeping up. It was also at this point I noticed the lights for the field were on. I assume there is a connection there but not sure if it is the lights or something in the field. Both of these issues beg the question...can a response to a stimulus be as sudden as the two I described seem to be or is there another issue I am not understanding?

This last question is probably a direct result of being called a hypochondriac for many years over the exact symptoms this website (and Dr. Kruse) are confirming.

### JanSz — 2017-04-20T15:12:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-az.19348/post-212637>

> [JohnnyAZ said:](/goto/post?id=212623)
>
> @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)
>
> I would like to add on an important note. I used to own large breed snakes and in the habitats I used the second bulb you posted. I used to love sitting in the room in the glow of these bulbs and never knew why. Perhaps intuitively I knew it was good for me.

This light, the Exo Terra Solar Glow 160W, is designed to provide all daily needs of reptile, including UV frequencies, IR-A and heat (in terrarium environment).
In terrarium that light is relatively close to the animal.

When human use that light, the light must be farther away, 2-3 feet or more.
Light power diminishes with square power of distance,
so do not count much on the UV part.

This light is convenient specially at wakeup time, when you want to tell your pineal gland that the day started and is time to shut down production of melatonin.
That allows adrenal glands to restart cortisol production.

You have plenty of sunlight available in AZ, but if you cant use it, then the artificial choice is red Sperti.
Not white, but Red.
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/pg-e2-ratio.6427/page-4#post-201738)

### JohnnyAZ — 2017-04-21T04:57:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-az.19348/post-212682>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) I was curious about the sun in AZ. I heard @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) talk about vitamin D winter and I was not exactly sure what that meant. It sounded as if he was saying that even in sunny areas the sun becomes less dense in the materials we need from it. My elevation is low so I don't get the benefit of being above 5000 feet and always having a closer relationship to the sun. I did however by some of the red bulbs today and have one in my family room going right now. Its funny, my 15 year old daughter walked out and was completely against the idea but an hour later I found her sitting by it as if she was attracted to it and she started asking me to explain why it was there (intuition I assume).

@[drezy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19879/) I think I could learn to live with that. Going to peruse that link you posted now.

Update to all involved thus far (@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) and others already tagged) I purchased red lights like explained and I visited my optometrist today and ordered lenses that filter the 400-500 range of blue light. I also have begun driving with my window down and making sure to look out the open window as much as possible. I stopped going to the gym and sleep in complete darkness with no electrical devices on in the room and the wifi off ( I do have a ceiling fan on and my ceilings are 10 ft high but I assume with even that distance it may still be an issue). I have taken to eating all my meals outside when possible and spend 10-15 minutes shirtless and in shorts standing on the gound facing the east. I also have a filter on my iphone and only use my computer with glasses on. My brain fog used to hit me within minutes of walking up and it would be a roller coaster of ups and down all day with mostly downs. This week my fog has taken later and later to set in and today didn't hit me until almost 1 pm and that was after a long bout inside my office building without my blue light glasses on.

I have so many questions and so much to learn but the improvement I have gotten from this site in one week is immense!!! I appreciate you all more than you may realize and I am truly indebted to you. My plan is to get the grounding rod installed soon, I am looking at the quantlet (not sure if it is for healing or optimizing and right now my focus is on healing) and I will become a gold member within the next week as I am awaiting my next check. Once I am a member I will post the entry I have been drafting into a journal entry as several people have suggested I do.

The podcast I listened to today was about using an MRI to diagnose issues. Have you guys done this? It seems Jack is the only one who can read them in the facet we need them read...or maybe I am incorrect and there is a Dr. in AZ I can see. If not does Jack take patients if they make the trip out to see him? I am intrigued at getting an MRI analyzed. Problem is getting it ordered by a doctor who does not subscribe to this belief as well as getting it read correctly.

Funny side note: While typing this, my daughter just came up to me and informed me of something she learned now about light's effect on the body and she taught me something new...so perhaps it is catching on with her as well!

I tagged Jack in this and realize that was slightly arrogant of me as the man is incredibly busy...perhaps that is a no no as well. Not sure. If it is I apologize. I just wanted him aware of the changes already taking place. Some might argue placebo but I swear something is different.

### Dennis Clark — 2017-04-28T21:15:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-az.19348/post-213053>

Hi, Caroline: Thanks for the quick reply. If I am correct, you took time out on your way to Mexico? Indeed, the galvanized stock tank seems preferable to the plastic one. I contacted Janz for some pointers, and he has been very helpful, as always. One interesting CT tub idea that I came across is an actual freezer (with recommendation to unplug it when submerging!) As for my new location, I am only 7 miles from my previous one, with a city change from Tempe to Phoenix. (No need to bike to work anymore.) I do still send out my newsletter, although mostly directly from or linked to articles at HerbScientist.com. (Recent post on sunshine vs breast cancer should be an eye-opener for many.) The subscription incentive there (aka, my free report: "How to Increase Longevity with Optimal Health and Fitness in Spite of Modern Medicine") is a peek into Krusology. Doing my part to spread the word. Back to CT ... I am going to experiment with blocks of ice made in my garage freezer. Can hardly wait to get cold again soon.

Your comment about CT and a sauna is valuable. I just recently came to realize the power of saunas for IR light. Now I am going to have to plot how to get a sauna! I used to belong to an athletic club that had jacuzzi, cold plunge, sauna, and steam room. That combo doesn't seem to exist anymore, at least for the public.

One more thing (actually, there are many) that I am figuring out is how to get optimal exercise outdoors. So far I have just been playing in the dirt ... er, putting in a new lawn in my back yard. Bien viaje.

1) A meter that measures Electro Magnetic Fields (areas of your car, all electric appliances, powerlines, etc.). E.G. Trifield meter.

New blood tests as of last week show that LDL went up to 180. HDL went down to 36 and cholesterol went up to 240. Test also show I am low in vit d 16.6.

So now I'm wondering with these results where do I stand? 7 weeks on Leptin RX and I have dropped 30lbs but blood panels are not great....thinking I should cut back on fat and red meat and stick to fish and leaner meats.

P 86-100g
F 150-180g
C less than 25g daily

# Thread: Introduction and my health question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-and-my-health-question.19356/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Katrina — 2017-04-18T00:18:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-and-my-health-question.19356/post-212463>

Hello....

I am a single woman, 52 years old. I reside in So California. I work as a fitness professional teaching land and water aerobics. I work primarily with Seniors. I have largely been healthy in my life, although I struggle with weight loss and gain rollercoaster. I have an addiction to sweets which comes from emotionally issues.

My mother had ruebella (German Measles) when she carried me. I am pretty normal except I have hearing loss which was recognized when I was about 5 years old. Over the years it has progressively gotten worse but I function as a hearing person. I have also been diagnosed and treated as such for glaucoma. This has been ongoing for the last 15 years.

My question is this...I have been told that both the hearing loss and the glaucoma are neurologically related...I have lost vision, about 80% in one eye and a small amount in the other....is this something that can be corrected by stopping the pressure and healing - as I understand that the body can heal itself- the damaged tissue/nerves? I have been told that the only way that anything that can be done to, possibly as it's not guaranteed, to stop the pressure is to have laser ( most likely) surgery done on them. I am looking for alternatives as I don't really want to undergo surgery and I would like to be able to communicate in one fashion or another. Since I have had the hearing loss for so long I am accustomed to it and have learned to live with it. They eyesight loss, however, has been in the past six years and it is a real challenge as I am on my own, no spouse to help and children are almost grown and are moving out to start their own lives.

So any advice, suggestions are welcome. I have found your work intriguing, I have read your book and it had made me question things. Thank you for your time.

### JanSz — 2017-04-18T03:04:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-and-my-health-question.19356/post-212471>

> [Katrina said:](/goto/post?id=212463)
>
> My question is this...I have been told that both the hearing loss and the **glaucoma** are neurologically related...I have lost vision, about 80% in one eye and a small amount in the other....**is this something that can be corrected by stopping the pressure** and healing - as I understand that the body can heal itself- the damaged tissue/nerves? I have been told that the only way that anything that can be done to, possibly as it's not guaranteed, **to stop the pressure is to have laser** ( most likely) **surgery** done on them. I am looking for alternatives as I don't really want to undergo surgery and I would like to be able to communicate in one fashion or another

I had Laser Iridotomy for Narrow Angle glaucoma.
Easy surgery.
Works fine.
If you have Narrow Angle, Laser Iridotomy may be your quick and easy way out.
After surgery I was driving home myself.
Make sure to have dark glasses with you. (If you forget dark glasses, ask for spares)

[http://www.glaucoma.org/treatment/laser-iridotomy-and-narrow-angles.php](http://www.glaucoma.org/treatment/laser-iridotomy-and-narrow-angles.php)

This video provides a brief introduction to laser iridotomy for treating narrow-angle glaucoma.
[http://www.glaucoma.org/treatment/video-laser-iridotomy.php](http://www.glaucoma.org/treatment/video-laser-iridotomy.php)
