### Tyler Chism — 2017-08-30T15:21:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-222569>

> [Emma Sabin said:](/goto/post?id=222562)
>
> Thanks Tyler - I've joined so will ask for some help.
> I think I may be doing my eyes some damage at present with the lack of light at nighttime.
>
> I also take off my glasses a lot to get natural light on my eyes and wondered if this is also not good because at -12 and -13 there is a huge amount of differential blur. I wondered what you thought?

You sound like me, I definitely want to get as much natural into my eyes as possible. Here's what I did: In the morning I would stand facing the sun and not wear any glasses for 15 to 30 minutes. Then I would go ahead and wear my glasses the rest of the day. this assured I at least got what might be considered the minimum amount of morning sun that I need while still wearing the lenses most of the day so as not to have any blur. And I wouldn't worry too much about wearing the lenses all day, because the area around your glasses is still allowing natural light into your eyes, it's just not as much as would be optimal. My logic is that I want to get back to 20/20 as soon as possible so that I can start getting the optimal amount every day, and this requires that I wear the lenses for now to make this happen.

I will tell you the biggest thing from Jake that helped me was the change in habit/ change in lifestyle and behavior. This is difficult but it's crucial, I had to actively keep myself from falling to my old pattern of looking down when I walk or looking at things close to me. You have to break this habit! start looking in the distance. when driving look out to the farthest sign ahead and try to bring the words into focus (this is a crude version of the active focus, but it works.) when you walk look out ahead of you in the distance, don't look down! keep your head up and good posture (this will also increase your confidence). and very importantly make sure to not use your full prescription when looking at things up close, if its something small like your phone you can pull it up close to read it. keep it at just enough distance to where you have to actively focus to bring the words into clarity, without squinting! this is the sweet spot distance that you basically want everything at.

For me these habits were difficult, your body operates off of neurological patterns, this makes you more efficient in your movement and makes it to wear you don't even have to think about it when performing the tasks that you do often. And the way you look at the world around you is something that you do very often! But this also makes it quicker to build the new habit, you just have to work hard for about two weeks at catching yourself starring at the closer object as opposed to looking out in the distance.

I was scarred that it wasn't going to work when I first started, after listening to the podcast I went to the eye doctor (one that was willing to work with me) and he dropped my prescription down from -4.00 to -3.50 in both eyes. Turns out the -4.00 was overprescribed ( most people are overprescribed) and I had been wearing these for years ( I actually remember eye doctors for the past few years being surprised that my myopia wasn't progressing as most peoples do, because your eye will continue to elongate as long as you wear overprescribed lenses and practice poor vision techniques!) Anyways when I dropped to -3.50 I was skeptical but within about two months I could actually tell my vision was pretty good so I decided to drop down to -3.25. I didn't go to the eye doctor cause I could buy the lenses online for 15 to 20 bucks as opposed to 300 to 400! the -3.25's were pretty rough and I them for about 5 or 6 months, again I was scarred that I would stop making progress, but then things started getting clearer and clearer. I dropped again to -3.00 then in only a month I dropped again to -2.75. Hopefully I can keep progressing quickly, but from what I've read there will be some long stretches of seemingly no change and some breakthrough periods.

Anyways I hope this helps, I hear that high myopes like yourself actually progess pretty quickly in the beginning because of the way diopters are measured so hopefully you can jump down pretty quickly.

This is so helpful - thank you for taking the time to message me the details. I will try and start reading Jake's stuff today. I need to order new glasses too so will get them a half diopter less I think. The tricky one is my right eye which has a cataract in. I can't see much out of that one at all so focussing will be irrelevant. Its hard reading all this stuff online too as I now its damaging my cataract eye even more. ### Tyler Chism — 2017-08-31T21:24:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-222669>

> [Emma Sabin said:](/goto/post?id=222642)
>
> This is so helpful - thank you for taking the time to message me the details. I will try and start reading Jake's stuff today. I need to order new glasses too so will get them a half diopter less I think. The tricky one is my right eye which has a cataract in. I can't see much out of that one at all so focussing will be irrelevant. Its hard reading all this stuff online too as I now its damaging my cataract eye even more. The idea of pulling my phone up to my eye close to read it worries me because of the blue light - what do you think about this?

I can't speak to having a cataract as I haven't really looked into it. I do know that Jake writes about it and here is an article where one person seems to have great improvements with their high myopia and a cataract in their right eye [http://endmyopia.org/cataract-alternative-treatment-debbie-progress/](http://endmyopia.org/cataract-alternative-treatment-debbie-progress/) So it looks like there is some promising alternatives to surgery, such as eye drops.

As far as holding the phone close to your eyes I think the same advice would apply to everyone: the time spent looking at your phone or a screen should be reduced as much as possible. However, with long term viewing I would say you have two options. One is to use a blue blocker app on your phone and computer and just get your face close. the other is to have a pair of glasses that are reduced from your normal prescription that is specifically for up close things. Actually having two pairs of glasses, one for distance one for up close, is what Jake recommends. You don't want to have more than two or you will confuse your focal plane. I myself just decided to have one pair of glasses for distance. I just avoid up close work and when I do read a book or my phone, I just get my face close. And on my phone I make sure to have my blue blocker app turned on

zennioptical.com (where I buy my glasses) will make whatever lenses you get into blue blockers for a few extra bucks. This may be a good option if you decide to go with reduced prescription differentials.

### Tyler Chism — 2017-09-05T15:12:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-222900>

> [ScottishEmma said:](/goto/post?id=222715)
>
> Did you try the + lenses hack (reading glasses).
>
> I had a look in the shop - my prescription is -4.75 - and they had +1, +2 and +3. Did you try that to relax the muscle Jake is talking about?

If you are referring to wearing reading glasses for up close work then that will only be good if you have good vision already or if you wear contacts. For people like us we still need minus lenses even for up close work, unless you're like me and just get your face at just the right distance away from the close thing. But if you are higher than -4 then I would probably recommend a pair of reduced prescription lenses for close work like -3 or whatever is best. I actually do wear contacts sometimes (although I don't recommend this either as this will block almost all UV light) and when I wear contacts THEN I do have some reading glasses that I use to read a book or look at a computer or phone screen.

Basically the Habit and lifestyle change I mentioned earlier is what I did to relax the muscle. If you get outside and practice the active focus then the muscle will start to relax. So get outside and just look at something, back away from the thing to where the thing JUST goes blurry then just look at it like Jake describes. try to pull it into focus to where it is not blurry anymore, don't squint, if you have to squint then you're a little too far. I just practice this on random things as I go throughout my day. This habit change is what will get you results.

I just listened to the podcast again yesterday, I think the way Jake says to "only wear the lenses you need" is a good way to put it. So make sure to not wear your distance lenses for up close work, and the lenses you wear now are probably a little too strong so you can start by Immediately backing off your current prescription by .25 to .5 diopter (this might be tricky to determine but you can print out the centimeter chart and determine exactly what you need). I myself just went to the eye doctor that helped determine what the lowest prescription I could go with, which was nice because of the nice equipment they had to determine this, especially if you have astigmatism and need a cylindrical correction on top of your spherical correction. The downside to the eye doctor is that you get measure indoors under artificial light, so the diopter amount that you get in there will be different than what you get if they conducted the measurement outside because of the lighting. Now that I have a good idea of the whole process I haven't been back to the eye doctor, nor will I go back.

### Tyler Chism — 2017-09-05T22:35:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-222932>

> [Emma Sabin said:](/goto/post?id=222916)
>
> Sorry I meant ages to deliver.
>
> I did actually get a reply from Jake but he said eh can't do one to one as too busy. He was nice and said to stay in touch.
>
> I'm slightly confused Tyler, apologies, would you mind clarifying for me. So, with -11.75 and -13.50 (and a stigmatism), should I be keeping my glasses on for all long vision and taking them off to read up close? And not keep taking them off to get light in my eye in the day? I also need reading glasses but can only wear those with lenses and I no longer wear those as they're too dangerous for my eyes being so myopic. I guess I'm asking what would you do if you were me? What prescription would you take my glasses too and not sure how I can use reading glasses without my contact lenses? I will definitely subscribe to Jakes 7day/week thing now! I've not had a chance before and then less questions for you! Thanks again.

No worries, I don't mind to help at all. So you would want two pairs of glasses. One pair for distance which will be close to what you have now but reduced enough to challenge you to focus on things to bring them into clarity and not be overprescribed. The other pair will be around -9 or -10 diopters. this is a really rough estimation, with higher myopia like yours a jump of 1 to 2 diopters is actually less of a jump than 1 to 2 diopters for someone at -4. At -4 diopters a decrease of 2 diopters is a difference of 25 centimeters, for someone at -12 diopters a decrease of 2 diopters is a difference of 1.7 centimeters. So you can get away with a bigger jump, but how big I'm not sure. You'll really want to thoroughly look through the blog and on facebook before getting your 2 pairs of glasses.... But if you find that you made a poor decision after you buy some you can just buy the right ones as its not terribly expensive, and you might be able to keep the other pair (if they're lower prescription) for when you can jump down to them. Also you'll want to come to a good understanding of how to correct for astigmatism, this is on the blog also, but you'll need to know when you punch in your prescription online (if that's the route you decide to go). I would definitely get a copy of your prescription from your most recent visit and go from there, make sure you also get your pupillary distance (PD) its the distance in millimeters between your pupils, its necessary for buying glasses and it wasn't included on my prescription from my doctor visits so I had to email the doctors office and ask them... or you can measure it, but its a pain.

If you read one blog post I posted earlier its about a high myopia person who quit correcting for astigmatism and she said her eyes did much better... I myself used to have astigmatism but it corrected itself long ago.. I do wonder if some astigmatism prescriptions are unnecessary, but you might want to do some more reading to determine for yourself.

So 2 pairs of glasses, one for most of the time when your outdoors/looking not up close, the other a reduced prescription for looking up close. I would recommend always wearing either pair and only wearing nothing for up to 20 to 30 minutes in the morning to get light in the eyes, otherwise leave them on. And if you decide to wear contacts (first of all you'll have to correct the prescription accordingly because the contacts are closer to your eyes the diopters will be slightly different than your glasses prescription) then you can wear reading glasses with your contacts when your looking at things up close... I know its a lot to think about but I want to really assure you and encourage you that its really not too bad to figure out, It will only take a few days of researching for you to understand how to figure out your prescription. And actually you can get on zennioptical.com and start to order some glasses and when you go to put in your prescription it will help you entering in the info and explain what each thing means, so you might want to look at that first then you'll know what numbers you need to go get.

I was going to get a 3rd pair made up with my prescription and the amber so I don't need the Bono ones. Now I'm not sure. What do you do?
>
> Then no more questions

I don't have any blue blockers, I just don't watch tv or anything at night, and I go to bed at dark, and my phone has a blue blocker app. But it is a good idea to have some. I would say get blue blocker lenses on your second pair of glasses (the ones you'll use for close up) because I would imagine those will be the ones you'll look at screens with or be indoors, but it really depends on your lifestyle and what you do. If you don't mind spending the money then you could get both a regular pair and a blue blocker pair with each of your glasses. They are cheap but at about 20 bucks a piece 4 pairs of glasses can add up. So I would say this one is up to you.

### Jake Tassell — 2017-09-21T16:34:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-224086>

Years and years ago, I picked up this weird-looking old book in a junk shop in Hastings, 'Instantaneous Personal Magnetism', by Edmund Shaftesbury. Originally published in 1935 and put out by something called 'The International Magnetism Club'....[https://books.google.co.uk/books/ab...al\_Magnetism.html?id=TNmMIED3zwYC&redir\_esc=y](https://books.google.co.uk/books/about/Instantaneous_Personal_Magnetism.html?id=TNmMIED3zwYC&redir_esc=y)

It had these eye exercises in it. They went something like this....

Look straight ahead.
Look 12 o' clock.
Look straight ahead.
Look 2 o' clock
Look straight ahead.
Look 3' o' clock.
Look straight ahead.
Look 4 o' clock, etc, and round the clock until you've completed a sort of 'Union Jack' pattern.

### KalosKaiAgathos — 2018-05-19T15:48:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-241553>

Better rhythm = better eye health...

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29721962](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/29721962)

**The Retinal Circadian Clock and Photoreceptor Viability.**
Circadian rhythms are present in most living organisms, and these rhythms are not just a consequence of the day/night fluctuation, but rather they are generated by endogenous biological clocks with a periodicity of about 24 h. In mammals, the master pacemaker of circadian rhythms is localized in the suprachiasmatic nuclei (SCN) of the hypothalamus. The SCN controls circadian rhythms in peripheral organs. The retina also contains circadian clocks which regulate many aspects of retinal physiology, independently of the SCN. Emerging experimental evidence indicates that the retinal circadian clocks also affect ocular health, and a few studies have now demonstrated that disruption of retinal clocks may contribute to the development of retinal diseases. Our study indicates that in mice lacking the clock gene Bmal1, photoreceptor viability during aging is significantly reduced. Bmal1 knockout mice at 8-9 months of age have 20-30% less nuclei in the outer nuclear layer. No differences were observed in the other retinal layers. Our study suggests that the retinal circadian clock is an important modulator of photoreceptor health.

Credit: JA (to keep the individual partially anonymous)

### ToddC — 2018-07-02T00:55:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-244721>

I just joined the forum, and my first search was to see if anyone had a recommended company to purchase prescription eyeglasses (with NO UV protection) from. I assume this would be best for the glasses I wear for distance outdoor vision. I have used Zenni in the past, but all their lenses come with UVA/UVB protection. I can get glasses from them to block out blue light, but I can't get glasses that don't block UV. Thus, I go without glasses quite a bit when I'm outdoors (and I'm outdoors a lot, and indoors on the computer very little). I'm also in the EndMyopia Back to 20/20 program. So, I know I'm not helping reduce my diopters by seeing in a blur for a good portion of each day. I've been working on reducing my prescription glasses for the past 14 years. I've reduced each eye by 1.25 diopter. I wish I could figure out how to reduce even further. Right now, my 20/20 prescription is R -3.00/L -2.50.

Anyone know where to buy glasses with no UV protection?

Hi ToddC

Again I know this makes my vision trickier at night, I'm sure this is not the EM protocol, but I can't do it all and protecting from blue light is my Number 1.

I am unable to take the UV out of my glasses as I am -11.5 so no chance apparently at that strength. Good luck in finding some though. I'll let you know if I find any.

### ToddC — 2018-07-10T18:17:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-245542>

> [Penny said:](/goto/post?id=244789)
>
> What does that mean "I know I'm not helping my diopters by seeing in a blur for a good portion of each day?" I just take them off most of the day unless I'm reading on a computer - I have fantastic close vision though -

This is the way I understand it:
If all the eyes see is blur, they adapt to the blur eventually, and make it the new normal. This is called blur adaptation. If eyesight is overcorrected with 20/15 correction, or 20/20 correction in extended close-up situations (like computer work), then hyperopic defocus occurs, which means the eyeballs elongate to meet the new normal, and diopters increase. When we undercorrect our prescription so that distance vision is both a bit clear and a bit blurry (like 20/30 acuity), then this type of stimulus gives the brain a chance to make the image clearer. If we actively try to clear a slightly blurry image, we can (with practice) make it clear. When the new clarity starts to stick, and becomes the new normal, then we can reduce our diopters a little bit. The idea is to reduce .25 diopter at a time, mostly keeping our distance vision at the edge of clarity. Ourselves and our brain will get the stimulus it needs to always move towards clarity, instead of blur.
I have a pair of glasses for distance vision, and a pair of glasses for close-up vision. The close-up prescription is reduced to where the text is at the edge of blur and clarity when I'm seated at an ergonomically comfortable position from the computer screen.
I also spend time going without glasses at all, especially the first hour of the day. I awake at dawn and go outside to greet the rising sun. I don't want my glasses to interfere with the nourishing morning light, and it's also good for our brain to know how much blur remains. The rest of the day I just go with the flow. Sometimes I'm going without glasses, and sometimes I am. It depends on how I feel. It's a balancing act between getting the clarity we need to improve eyesight and getting the uninterrupted sunlight we need to nourish our bodies. Right now, I'm trying to get at least 2 hours of natural light exposure during the day without glasses.
Hope this helps, Penny. If not, try going over to endmyopia.org. That should help for sure!

### ToddC — 2018-07-10T18:52:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/myopia-partially-reversed.19397/post-245549>

> [Emma Sabin said:](/goto/post?id=244970)
>
> Well done you on reducing your prescription. What do you think that is from? I am focussing on the natural light on my eyes and blue light protection at night. Again I know this makes my vision trickier at night, I'm sure this is not the EM protocol, but I can't do it all and protecting from blue light is my Number 1.

Protecting from blue light is also my Number 1. In order to both improve eyesight and protect from blue light, I had to change my lifestyle a bit. I realized that I was doing lots of close-up after sunset. I would catch up on emails and read books mostly after dinner time. With protecting from blue light, we only use amber incandescant light bulbs or candle light at night. This dim lighting is not good for reading (especially when trying to improve eyesight), and computers are not good for protecting against blue light, flux or no flux. So, I don't read or do any computer work after sunset anymore. Dim lighting like candlelight is better for distance and peripheral vision. Daytime is for focusing on the fine details. My favorite evening activity is telling stories and singing songs around a campfire. My goal is to do more of that. As well as, star-gazing!
The first eyeglass prescription we tend to have is from ciliary spasm, which is about -1.25 diopter prescription. After that, any increase in prescription is usually from hyperopic defocus. I think the improvements I have made are from correcting the initial ciliary spasm. Correcting the hyperopic defocus is where, I think, following Jake's advice at endmyopia.org is most helpful.

I was doing the Bates method and he said the more dim light you read by, the better... ? But JK doesn't like reading by a red light and says if you do it chronically you can get a cataract - I think this is due to the flicker effect which speeds up ubiquitination in your eye - I think I have to kiss reading at night goodbye... every fake light flickers - I suppose even the UV lights flicker - even though they seem to make \*tons\* of serotonin -

Such a mine field we walk...

# Thread: Why are some people who live indoors 24/7 not overweight or obviously sick?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-some-people-who-live-indoors-24-7-not-overweight-or-obviously-sick.19403/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2017-04-30T22:02:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-some-people-who-live-indoors-24-7-not-overweight-or-obviously-sick.19403/post-213120>

A friend brought up a point and I actually was unable to refute it.

Why are so many people who live indoors all the time not overweight? Like tech CEO's; Jeff Bezos is the example he mentioned.

My inclination is to think that because obesity did not manifest, something else will in the future. The outside might look fine, but the inside is not.

Separately, there are times when it's cloudy in Philadelphia and I feel depressed if I'm alone. **But, if I'm with someone having an interesting conversation with someone, it seems to "trump" the negatives of the environment.**

**Could the dopamine boost from conversation/doing something you love override the effects of a bad environment to some extent?** If so, how, and to what extent? Is the dopamine like a credit card, bound to run out eventually?

Thanks!

Where the defect is in the body limits the coherence of water in that particular region of the body.

Here is an example of a group of scientists not knowing the published research already completed in their own field. We've known for 20 years now that water below 1.4 nm does things we just do not expect. This is why water is confined in small spaces in cells and around organelles like mitochondria. All living things, plant and animal are liquid crystalling because of water confined at small scale. Electric polarities manifest at these scales in water and determine the alignment of the body plan to give rise to the major body axes seen in morphogenesis. Light is an electromagnetic wave that is trapped in water. The sun's light give water the ability to provide electrodynamical forces acting on the coherent liquid crystalline nature of cell water. This light plays a major role in pattern formation and morphogenesis. Water is the supreme stage on which life becomes possible because of how it stores light energy and will not thermalize it as heat. Water gives cells flexibility to proteins, reduces the energy barrier between reactants and products, and increases the probability of quantum tunneling of electrons and protons by transient compression of the energy barrier. This is especially true in between the respiratory proteins of mitochondria. Sunlight become quantum coherent in cell water at small scales to make coherent domains or what Polllack calls the exclusion zone (EZ). Coherent molecular motions are musch slower than visible light spectrum vibrations of the sun, so that ordered alignment of the molecules in a cell will still be registered by the light transmitted across the cell. The most active parts of the cell will always appear brightest, and this indicates that the molecular motions are the most coherent. It is clear to me that the universe is powered by water electricity. Star provide the wireless charge. Organisms are powered by water electricity too via chloroplasts and mitochondria. Sunlight hits water and creates billions of electrons in a sea that is used as a redox pile to power all the metabolic reactions within a cell. Giuliano Preparta and Emilio Del Guidice extended QED field theory to condensed phases of liquids. This was done close to 20 years ago. Water is the means of life and the message of life because sunlight makes water the universal electron donor. # Thread: Emma's First journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 50
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### emmao — 2017-05-03T23:12:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-213323>

hello everyone,
I'm Emma, I'm 17 years old and I'm from Nebraska. I wanted to become a member here to post about how open minded I've become because of my encounter with Jack.

My family has followed Jack Kruse for about 5 years now.
I started transitioning into a healthy lifestyle when my mom peaked interest into organic food. I was always embarrassed by what others thought so I tried to hide the food my mom packed in my lunch and would try to avoid having people over so they wouldn't lurk in my pantry. Given this was about 11 years ago, when organic food wasn't looked upon highly. I slowly started to accept the fact that I was helping my body out, despite my friends opinions.

Then when I was about 12, my mom and dad latched onto the biohacks and everything Jack preached. Every night I would arrive home to my mom and dad listening to podcasts in the dark. I despised coming home to have my mom say "Emma come listen to this I think you'd like it!"

I wanted none of it.

In middle school I struggled with acne. I look back at how stupid I probably looked covering my face in makeup to hide the volcanos. I never understood something was wrong with my body internally, so I just wanted to go to the dermatologist to get rid of it. My mom refused to take me, and I despised her for making me struggle with it. It wasn't until I was a sophomore that my parents started to pay me to eat oysters. Slowly but surely I started to see a difference in my skin. My acne was nearly gone and in the summer my skin would be bronzed. To this day I notice when I don't eat oysters, I break out horrendously. After I learned about DHA and how it helped my skin, I started to ease my way into the lifestyle of my parents.

My parents have worn orange goggles for what seems like forever now. I've never let them wear them when my friends were over and I'd always make them go upstairs to be out of sight. About a year ago, my curiousity got the best of me and I wanted to see if they actually worked. I would wear them for awhile then one night I wouldn't and I noticed I couldnt fall asleep without them. I worked my way into wearing them every night and started being open about the goggles. I send out snapchats to my friends and stopped caring that they say they're "weird".

In December, my parents and I took a vacation to Mexico and Met with Dr. Kruse a few days. Honestly, we were waiting outside the Royal to meet him, and I was so embarrassed because I felt like a fan girl. All I could think is how I'm 17 waiting outside for some fancy doctor to come tell my mom "hi" and leave. Dr. Jack and Jeremy treated us like normal people and really emphasized why all these things were important. Hearing this all in person changed my entire aspect on what's in store for my generation!

So here's the summary.

Ive convinced a few friends to try the glasses and even one of my friends parents. I worry about all the things my generation is going to struggle with because of blue light!
I am ready to graduate high school In a few weeks and I'm starting to think about my future. Jacks advice to me was "make money fast then go what you want". I've still kept this close to heart, but I'm very interested in some sort of natropathic dermatology. I want to learn to heal skin naturally with red light and doing what's right for your body. Hardly anybody in my generation understands the things I do, so I hope it gives me an advantage in whatever I do.

Hi Caroline! Totally didn't mean for what I said to come off that way.

Like Jack says, I know what I say but I don't know what others take from it.

I absolutely agree that we can all learn from everyone. I never said that we can't or shouldn't.

But, it is quite natural for people to spend time and learn with other people at similar stages in life.

The entire forum is composed of adults who have graduated high school, college, worked, and raised families. I have done nothing but take advice from you guys because I truly believe you have tremendous wisdom to share.

But, it really helps to have people in similar age groups and situations in life so we can learn from each other at our respective places.

When I first got into this, no one on this forum had been in high school and navigated that whole social environment with the quantum eye. I still asked everyone for advice because you guys have dealth with difficult things in high school as well. From experience, I know that it really helps to have people in similar situations to share feelings with; that's why I shared these names with Emma.

Excuse me for not clarifying: this group I shared is in addition to the hundreds of extremely wise and experienced adults on here who have taught me more than most others in my life combined, seriously! Not to the exclusion of them.

Thanks Caroline for pointing out something that could be very easily misunderstood. Your wisdom has guided me very far. In addition, it helps to be able to discuss with people at a similar young age who is navigating similar challenges going through high school, into college, and considering beginning a career in this field.

### caroline — 2017-05-07T04:55:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-213560>

I do get it Matty! I thought I would just give you a little nudge! LOL

Of course you need friends who are experiencing life at the same stages.

I hope you are having lots and lots of fun and not taking life too seriously all the time!

### Matty_M — 2017-05-07T05:01:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-213563>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=213560)
>
> I do get it Matty! I thought I would just give you a little nudge!
>
> Of course you need friends who are experiencing life at the same stages.
>
> I hope you are having lots and lots of fun and not taking life too seriously all the time!

I just spent four days in Vero Beach with Nick from this forum and his girlfriend, then a week in SF and Berkeley. There was not one cloud in the sky, I did yoga every day, swam in the bay, and walked around Berkeley shirtless in the sun. To my surprise, I was the only one barefoot and shirtless there in the streets... today I ran some dogs around a park and swam in the 50 degree creek. It's pretty magnificent. I took 8 total flights in all that traveling. Seeing the world from above 10,000 feet makes it difficult to take us too seriously . Hope you are well too!

### caroline — 2017-05-07T05:13:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-213566>

Hi Matty ....did you read Jack's new post on airflight and an airline pilot?

I love that you are connecting with so many people and doing your own thing!

You see - you have pointed out above and very clearly I might add .....we all live our own life and do our own thing.

Even tho I didn't experience going to high school with all our new knowledge that JK has shared with us ......you/we are on a singular journey.

No one else was walking around shirtless and shoeless, probably very few swimming ....... good for you! did you care? I think you are having the time of your life! You are getting healthy and strong with clear eyes and an amazing vision. I am positive your future will unfold in surprising and multi faceted ways ....stay tuned!

now that we know better ..... we are doing so much better.

It is all up to each one of us ..... what we take away, what we implement, what we focus on, what we allow in our life....

You are doing great Matty!

### Penny — 2017-05-10T23:47:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-213832>

> [emmao said:](/goto/post?id=213323)
>
> hello everyone,
> I'm Emma, I'm 17 years old and I'm from Nebraska. I wanted to become a member here to post about how open minded I've become because of my encounter with Jack.
>
> My family has followed Jack Kruse for about 5 years now.
> I started transitioning into a healthy lifestyle when my mom peaked interest into organic food. I was always embarrassed by what others thought so I tried to hide the food my mom packed in my lunch and would try to avoid having people over so they wouldn't lurk in my pantry. Given this was about 11 years ago, when organic food wasn't looked upon highly. I slowly started to accept the fact that I was helping my body out, despite my friends opinions.
>
> Then when I was about 12, my mom and dad latched onto the biohacks and everything Jack preached. Every night I would arrive home to my mom and dad listening to podcasts in the dark. I despised coming home to have my mom say "Emma come listen to this I think you'd like it!"
>
> I wanted none of it.
>
> In middle school I struggled with acne. I look back at how stupid I probably looked covering my face in makeup to hide the volcanos. I never understood something was wrong with my body internally, so I just wanted to go to the dermatologist to get rid of it. My mom refused to take me, and I despised her for making me struggle with it. It wasn't until I was a sophomore that my parents started to pay me to eat oysters. Slowly but surely I started to see a difference in my skin. My acne was nearly gone and in the summer my skin would be bronzed. To this day I notice when I don't eat oysters, I break out horrendously. After I learned about DHA and how it helped my skin, I started to ease my way into the lifestyle of my parents.
>
> My parents have worn orange goggles for what seems like forever now. I've never let them wear them when my friends were over and I'd always make them go upstairs to be out of sight. About a year ago, my curiousity got the best of me and I wanted to see if they actually worked. I would wear them for awhile then one night I wouldn't and I noticed I couldnt fall asleep without them. I worked my way into wearing them every night and started being open about the goggles. I send out snapchats to my friends and stopped caring that they say they're "weird".
>
> In December, my parents and I took a vacation to Mexico and Met with Dr. Kruse a few days. Honestly, we were waiting outside the Royal to meet him, and I was so embarrassed because I felt like a fan girl. All I could think is how I'm 17 waiting outside for some fancy doctor to come tell my mom "hi" and leave. Dr. Jack and Jeremy treated us like normal people and really emphasized why all these things were important. Hearing this all in person changed my entire aspect on what's in store for my generation!
>
> So here's the summary.
>
> Ive convinced a few friends to try the glasses and even one of my friends parents. I worry about all the things my generation is going to struggle with because of blue light!
> I am ready to graduate high school In a few weeks and I'm starting to think about my future. Jacks advice to me was "make money fast then go what you want". I've still kept this close to heart, but I'm very interested in some sort of natropathic dermatology. I want to learn to heal skin naturally with red light and doing what's right for your body. Hardly anybody in my generation understands the things I do, so I hope it gives me an advantage in whatever I do.

Your story cracked me up... they paid you to eat oysters? That's funny...

### emmao — 2017-05-25T08:21:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-214629>

When I met jack in Mexico I had been putting my phone in my back pocket (shame on me, but I know better now) and he had told me to remove it. So I did. He explained how my body is being hit with emfs and radiation right by my ovaries. Sometimes I subconsciously put it there, but I've gotten better at getting it away from my body.
Now that I try to avoid it, I can't help but notice when other girls put it in their back pocket or In their bras. Regularly I have to tell my friends to take it out.
Today I had been scrolling through the news portion of Snapchat. Here news articles are posted from buzzfeed, cosmo, MTV, etc. One article title struck me today from buzzfeed that said "secret benefits of boobies" with an animated picture of a girl with a phone between her breasts. It then stated how boobs were a built in phone holder.
The media these leads teens and young adult women into thinking this is totally okay. Millions of girls are seeing this influence and they don't have someone to tell them to stop! Nothing gets me more stirred up. So I took the time to email buzzfeed to let them know how I feel.
The media has a crazy influence on young women. I fear what's in store for my generation

### caroline — 2017-05-25T09:38:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-214631>

way to go "starfish Emma"!!

### PaulG — 2017-05-25T10:12:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-214632>

Welcome Emma,

I have an 18 year old son who is interested in Science but can't turn him onto this stuff. I am always sending him interesting articles on insomnia, blue light, emf etc, though he may have blacklisted me! and we do discuss topics like effects of blue light on melatonin and sleep.

I also have two other kids, a daughter (13) and son (16)..it is hard to get the message across. If anyone knows of any video content that is easily digestible that let me know.

Generally speaking, people are not that pro-active with health and wellness and usually something has to go 'wrong' before they start looking for causes/solutions. I guess it's natural when you are young to believe you are somewhat immune to disease (true to a large extent) and its only an issue when you get to middle age or older. This is known as the invincibility fallacy. See [https://psychological-musings.blogspot.com.au/2011/02/middle-childhood-and-adolescence.html](https://psychological-musings.blogspot.com.au/2011/02/middle-childhood-and-adolescence.html)

The [EMF warriors library](https://www.emfwarriors.com/library/) is a very good resource of articles and videos.

Now way I am googling "secret benefits of boobies" would get me into trouble..

This is known as the invincibility fallacy. See [https://psychological-musings.blogspot.com.au/2011/02/middle-childhood-and-adolescence.html](https://psychological-musings.blogspot.com.au/2011/02/middle-childhood-and-adolescence.html)

My mom sent me links and ALWAYS talked about things with me. Everytime we got in the car it was like a broken record. I hated it; I bet he does too. If he doesn't want to be helped, you can't do anything about it, frankly. It takes one thing to trigger the curiousity of a teen before they're hooked. For me it was my skin, since I was so embarrassed and just wanted it gone. Once I realized that, I simply kept following. Find ONE thing that would interest them. For your daughter, it will most likely be acne or problems with her period like her cycle is off or her cramps are "unbearable". Don't overflow their uneducated brains yet, its overwhelming.
If you can take your family to Mexico for the December trip, do it. Everything started to seem real to me after this. Talking with other members and Jack influenced me more than any article or anything my mom had said to me.

### Matty_M — 2017-05-25T16:34:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-214651>

> [emmao said:](/goto/post?id=214629)
>
> When I met jack in Mexico I had been putting my phone in my back pocket (shame on me, but I know better now) and he had told me to remove it. So I did. He explained how my body is being hit with emfs and radiation right by my ovaries. Sometimes I subconsciously put it there, but I've gotten better at getting it away from my body.
> Now that I try to avoid it, I can't help but notice when other girls put it in their back pocket or In their bras. Regularly I have to tell my friends to take it out.
> Today I had been scrolling through the news portion of Snapchat. Here news articles are posted from buzzfeed, cosmo, MTV, etc. One article title struck me today from buzzfeed that said "secret benefits of boobies" with an animated picture of a girl with a phone between her breasts. It then stated how boobs were a built in phone holder.
> The media these leads teens and young adult women into thinking this is totally okay. Millions of girls are seeing this influence and they don't have someone to tell them to stop! Nothing gets me more stirred up. So I took the time to email buzzfeed to let them know how I feel.
> The media has a crazy influence on young women. I fear what's in store for my generation

Natural selection at play . You will be much freer once you realize that you can't save everyone. In fact, you can hardly do anything well if you don't save yourself first. Otherwise, we will be slaves to the banks for education, marriages, and mortgages for most of our productive life. We would have to trade our time here on earth for a paycheck of numbers in a computer to get our daily bread (seafood).

We can only do what we can do.

People will hurt themselves because the programming system (education is a euphemism) in our country has taught us to trust authority and that scientists are doctors would tell us of any risk.

If we want to solve the problem, we have to think about how to teach people to think for themselves.

We aren't all that different from Germany in the 1930's....

[http://schoolsucksproject.com/new-start-here/](http://schoolsucksproject.com/new-start-here/)

^^^Jack, I think you should share this podcast. I suggest everyone here checks it out. It explains magnificently how our system has programmed people to be "obedient idiots".

The other day I was walking along a beach here in Maine with a friend while on my senior project. He was flying a $1600 drone to get footage of the seaweed harvest that our friends were out for. I imagine looking at myself through the lens of the drone, walking along the beach. No thoughts, no preconceptions; just things the way they "are". Anything imposed onto the situation is really a product of our neocortex and doesn't necessarily represent the truth.

Larch Hanson of [theseaweedman.com](http://schoolsucksproject.com/new-start-here/), who we are working for, is extremely connected and wiser than just about anyone I've met. Jack, you've got competition . He says that thoughts are ephemeral; temporary and fleeting, like a flower.

I realized that I can do literally just about anything with the time I'm given to visit earth. My real home is out in the cosmos. The only way I will ever achieve what I want is:

(Take a guess)

By deciding WHAT I WANT!

I have all of these vague images in my mind of things I can do or outcomes in the real world that would would be fantastic, but they are hardly concrete. If I don't know what they really are, then I will never achieve them. I could just work down whichever path "feels right", but I can't expect to feel significant progress towards any outcome if I haven't defined the outcome I want.

"If one does not know to which port one is sailing, **no wind is favorable**."
\- Lucius Annaeus Seneca

Larch rarely answers questions directly, rather he tells stories. I think it's rubbing off.

For me, the most attractive port is getting myself stabilized. Then, working on what I can do to change the global programming system so that people are indoctrinated with distrust of authority and love of logic, instead of the other way around, **which is where we are now**.

By the way! It's 9:15 am and I've been up since 2:30 am. I got up for a seaweed harvest at low tide at first light. The colors lighting up the Maine coast this morning were beyond godly. There were seals all around the rocks where we harvested, one was sleeping and we walked right up to it. Another swam right up to our little rowboat. It was godly. I was connected to the 40 degree water doing labor with my hands. I am charged up like never before. Maybe a little sleep deprived. But, the 71-year-old who runs this place consistently gets by on under 4 hours of sleep during harvest season... like his Norwegian ancestors' mitochondial haplotype...

Again,

"If one does not know to which port one is sailing, **no wind is favorable**."

I guess it's natural when you are young to believe you are somewhat immune to disease (true to a large extent) and its only an issue when you get to middle age or older. This is known as the invincibility fallacy. See [https://psychological-musings.blogspot.com.au/2011/02/middle-childhood-and-adolescence.html](https://psychological-musings.blogspot.com.au/2011/02/middle-childhood-and-adolescence.html)
>
> The [EMF warriors library](https://www.emfwarriors.com/library/) is a very good resource of articles and videos.
>
> Now way I am googling "secret benefits of boobies" would get me into trouble..

STOP WITH THE ANTI -INSOMNIA "SHIT".  USE THE INCREASED TESTOSTERONE/BETTER ERECTION/MORE MUSCLE APPROACH.  In his mind , his problem isn't sleeping more, it's more about" with whom he can sleep." This is not rocket science.

or use cold.......
And for my new members reading this page they might be wondering how Cold Thermogenesis works here to raise dopamine: UV-A and IR-A light and COLD are a medical solution to a medical problem.
Cold water immersion at 14°C increased metabolic rate by 350%, norepinephrine by 530% & dopamine by 250%. Wow, even cold can raise dopamine. So this is why if you are away from the tropics (inside the 20's) you must CT to risk becoming an obedient idiot in a blue lit RF/microwaved world. [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/10751106/)

It is why sunlight in PDC and cenote use is big for things like this.

### Billybats — 2017-09-27T20:48:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-224389>

> [emmao said:](/goto/post?id=213323)
>
> hello everyone,
> I'm Emma, I'm 17 years old and I'm from Nebraska. I wanted to become a member here to post about how open minded I've become because of my encounter with Jack.
>
> My family has followed Jack Kruse for about 5 years now.
> I started transitioning into a healthy lifestyle when my mom peaked interest into organic food. I was always embarrassed by what others thought so I tried to hide the food my mom packed in my lunch and would try to avoid having people over so they wouldn't lurk in my pantry. Given this was about 11 years ago, when organic food wasn't looked upon highly. I slowly started to accept the fact that I was helping my body out, despite my friends opinions.
>
> Then when I was about 12, my mom and dad latched onto the biohacks and everything Jack preached. Every night I would arrive home to my mom and dad listening to podcasts in the dark. I despised coming home to have my mom say "Emma come listen to this I think you'd like it!"
>
> I wanted none of it.
>
> In middle school I struggled with acne. I look back at how stupid I probably looked covering my face in makeup to hide the volcanos. I never understood something was wrong with my body internally, so I just wanted to go to the dermatologist to get rid of it. My mom refused to take me, and I despised her for making me struggle with it. It wasn't until I was a sophomore that my parents started to pay me to eat oysters. Slowly but surely I started to see a difference in my skin. My acne was nearly gone and in the summer my skin would be bronzed. To this day I notice when I don't eat oysters, I break out horrendously. After I learned about DHA and how it helped my skin, I started to ease my way into the lifestyle of my parents.
>
> My parents have worn orange goggles for what seems like forever now. I've never let them wear them when my friends were over and I'd always make them go upstairs to be out of sight. About a year ago, my curiousity got the best of me and I wanted to see if they actually worked. I would wear them for awhile then one night I wouldn't and I noticed I couldnt fall asleep without them. I worked my way into wearing them every night and started being open about the goggles. I send out snapchats to my friends and stopped caring that they say they're "weird".
>
> In December, my parents and I took a vacation to Mexico and Met with Dr. Kruse a few days. Honestly, we were waiting outside the Royal to meet him, and I was so embarrassed because I felt like a fan girl. All I could think is how I'm 17 waiting outside for some fancy doctor to come tell my mom "hi" and leave. Dr. Jack and Jeremy treated us like normal people and really emphasized why all these things were important. Hearing this all in person changed my entire aspect on what's in store for my generation!
>
> So here's the summary.
>
> Ive convinced a few friends to try the glasses and even one of my friends parents. I worry about all the things my generation is going to struggle with because of blue light!
> I am ready to graduate high school In a few weeks and I'm starting to think about my future. Jacks advice to me was "make money fast then go what you want". I've still kept this close to heart, but I'm very interested in some sort of natropathic dermatology. I want to learn to heal skin naturally with red light and doing what's right for your body. Hardly anybody in my generation understands the things I do, so I hope it gives me an advantage in whatever I do.

Hi Emmao,

Awesome story and a beautiful women. You have made me start focusing more on making an effort to take bigger steps. To rethink my strategy. Thank you for your story!

### caroline — 2018-02-17T02:45:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emmas-first-journal.19416/post-234953>

> [aguzman said:](/goto/post?id=234936)

\[/quote\]
Please remember that everyone here is very busy with their own lives and issues. We are all trying to limit our computer/blue lite exposure time as best we can. Lots of people here put in a lot of their limited free time trying to help others.

You have only been here for one week???????

Dr. Kruse is a crazy busy guy ......please remember he is in full time neurosurgery practise. How he does all of this is a mystery to all of us. He has dedicated the last number of years to helping all of us here with all the blogs and webinars and podcasts ...and also answering the same questions - over and over and over for people who don't necessarily want to help themselves. If you come to jackkruse.com for a quick fix ...I am afraid - it ain't gonna happen.

Most folks here do pitch in as best they can with their own personal experiences - but we are not Doctors ....we are just regular folks like you.

Jack is incredibly busy with a huge new project that will take health care to a new level.....please cut him and us some slack.

Are you a paid member? Jack needs us all to step up and have skin in the game now. Of course you don't have to be - there is years of stuff here to get thru and process.

Have you listened to all his podcasts? The 2 Vermont talks? Are you following the webinars? the blogs?

There are people here with very serious and life threatening illnesses that are doing their due diligence - day in and day out and working really hard to help themselves.

Dr. K. is the guy with the flashlight. He answers questions when he can and always posts new information for us to check out.....and keep us on track.

There have been new people here that simply amaze me ....they read and follow the easy start guides that Jack has provided - and they just dive in....and they are making a huge difference in their lives.

There are a lot of us here who have gone to great expense to go to meet Jack at one event or another .....skin in the game. I flew from Australia to Orlando.

But it is more than that - a lot more than that ....we have to dive deep to make a difference in our own life - no one else can do it for us.

We really are all willing to help......

# Thread: ALL THINGS SUNSCREEN
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-04T02:56:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213329>

Sunscreen with SPF of 8 absorbs between 92- 95% of UVB photons and reduces vitamin D3 synthesis by the same degree. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18290718](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/18290718)

### QuantumRadical — 2017-05-05T00:36:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213396>

I haven't used sunscreen for the past seven years. Before that, always used it--highest spf available. Now, I sunbathe just short of burning as often as I can. My skin health and health in general has greatly improved. My dermotologist just shakes her head.
Q'Rad Randy

### QuantumRadical — 2017-05-05T00:37:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213398>

I am very fair skinned and burned easily. Not so much now.

### caroline — 2017-05-05T07:25:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213430>

me too - haven't used sunscreen the past five years or so. I don't burn at all and I feel great. Everyone tells me I am so lucky because I have an olive skin tone. I have never had an olive skin tone - I have always been very fair and burned easily.

### Inger — 2017-05-05T13:43:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213436>

I hardly burn either anymore, and I am a fair skinned scandinavian....
I feel SO blessed to have got to know how horrible sun-blockers are for health!

After staring the Iodine protocol again I have the feeling my skin get even tanner faster ... like olive-tan colored..... need to follow how this evolve  I wonder why...

### Huck — 2017-05-05T22:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213462>

Since I got my vitamin d level up, I haven't burned. I was at the beach this week and spent 7 hours in the sun the 1st day. I turned red for a couple of days, but it just seems to be darkening my tan now. No blistering or peeling.

The wind was blowing pretty hard which is why I didn't realize how hot my skin was getting.

### drezy — 2017-05-06T01:18:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-213468>

As a super-white Polish/Swiss(mitochondrially) boy, as I've got my D up I've experienced the same sun "callous" that you hint at.

Also, even at 15 yo I had a 138/89 BP. Now I'm coming in at 120/82. It's kind of alarming at first until I realize "oh, wait! this actually feels good." 2 or so months still haven't caught up with decades quite yet.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-19T01:21:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/all-things-sunscreen.19417/post-214276>

The world is changing and the clinicians in clinics and hospitals do not realize it. Patients are becoming more educated on the cutting edge of science because they are engaged with the reading new things out of curiosity and net research. Most scientists and clinicians like to deride the internet because they believe it is populated with quacks. There is no doubt that that is true in some spots, but when you have a blog and website deidicated to real science with cites to published research the truth become hard to deny. Believe it not some of them still do. The dominant belief today is that UV light causes melanoma. For ten years all the data I personally reviewed led me to the exact opposite stand point. Now we have this GEM of published research performed by a group or respectable scientists who performed a global worldwide analysis of cutaneous malignant melanoma (CMM) over UV-Radiation dose by sex, age group (0–14, 15–29, 30–49, 50–69, 70–85C) and Fitzpatrick skin types I-VI. Surprisingly to them and most readers, but no tto me, increasing UVR doses, which represent erythemally- weighted doses comprised primarily of UVB (290–315 nm) radiation, DID NOT significantly correlate with increasing CMM incidence for people with any skin type ANYWHERE in the world. The world is changing..........when will the skin and eye docs get this message? Might the real of melanoma can be found in the picture below? [http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/19381980.2016.1267077?needAccess=true](http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/pdf/10.1080/19381980.2016.1267077?needAccess=true)

### Jerry Fulford — 2017-05-04T19:47:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/healthy-eating-buying-bulk-and-camping.19418/post-213378>

Hi folks,

Am planning on doing long term camping this summer and am feeding a family of 4. Am working on creating meal plans and want to buy in bulk to reduce wastes and costs. Was going to go for quinoa, but see it's not highly recommended.

What are your suggestions for nutritious bulk foods?

Also, funny dream last night. Was on a cruise with Kruse eating raw oysters. Sorry Jack, but I'm not there with the raw ones yet

Thank you,

Assuming that your father lives in
Srinagar, Uttarakhand, India
The latitude for **Srinagar, Uttarakhand, India** is: 30.2246751
Elevation: 548.56 Meters (1799.74 Feet)

He should have plenty of sun.
He should be outside, with minimal clothes most of the day.
At night, 2-4 hrs before bed time, do not watch TV, cell phone,
sleep in totally dark room

Ask doctor for (all) this tests:
scan and post results (do not re-type)

1------- Pregnenolone,
2 ------ Progesterone
3 ------- Cortisol, AM & PM
4 ------- Dehydroepiandrosterone (DHEA) Sulfate
5 ------- Testosterone, Total, Serum
6 ------- Testosterone, Free and Weakly Bound
7 ------- SHBG
8 ------- Dihydrotestosterone (DHT)
9-------- Estrogens, Total
10 ------- Estradiol, Sensitive
11 ------- Estrone, Serum
12 ------- Metabolic Panel (14), Comprehensive
13 ------- Complete Blood Count (CBC) With Differential
14 ------- NMR LipoProfile® With Insulin Resistance Markers (With Graph)
15 ------- Thyroid-stimulating Hormone (TSH)
16 ------- Thyroxine (T4)
17 ------- Thyroxine (T4), Free
18 ------- Triiodothyronine (T3)
19 ------- **Triiodothyronine (T3), Free**
20 ------- **Reverse T3**
21 ------- Prolactin
22 ------- **Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum**
23 ------- IGF-1
24 ------- IGFBP3
25 ------- **Insulin, Free and Total, Serum**
26 ------- Hemoglobin (Hb) A1c
27 ------- **Homocysteine, Plasma**
28 ------- **Vitamin D, 25-Hydroxy
**
**Make sure to get**
**22 ------- Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum

that will tell us about eating chicken or not.**

But he should eat lots of butter or ghee (2-4 tablespoons)/day or more and drink vinegar (diluted in some water) 2-4 tablespoons, after each meal.

\===============================
low fat/low salt diet does not sound good to me

low fat bad advice overall
human body is made of fat and water

low salt is usually due to high blood pressure
but
low sun exposure causes low vit d and that in turn may cause high blood pressure

# Thread: How does blue light expand the respiratory proteins?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-blue-light-expand-the-respiratory-proteins.19422/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Matty_M — 2017-05-04T23:56:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-blue-light-expand-the-respiratory-proteins.19422/post-213393>

what is the mechanism here?

Can someone refer me to the papers or blogs on this topic?

Thanks

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-05T02:42:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-does-blue-light-expand-the-respiratory-proteins.19422/post-213407>

Do you understand how coupling works? The coupler of the two is called an ' interactive controller' . When a system loses negative feedback control you uncouple cycles from one another. Let me give you a clear example of a LOSS of negative feedback control: in this type of coupling mechanism two components of the system have opposite effects on one another. Consider prey and predator as a couple. Consumption of prey by predator has a positive effect on the eater, and a negative on on the eaten animal. The negative effect on predators on prey prevents uncontrolled growth of the predator's population. This stabilizes the population sizes of both groups when they are coupled.

Blue light from artificial bulbs and LED screens uncouple electron spin from ECT flow speeds to release massive amounts of light. At the same time the respiratory proteins stretch out because the size of the mitochondria changes and gets larger and this engages the allometric scaling laws of metabolism. This is a power law in mathematics. In Nick Lane's latest book he mentions it in there. Every one Angstrom increase in the distance between the respiratory proteins slows electron and proton tunneling by a FACTOR of ten. Anytime you slow ECT you are closer to death. Cynanide stops ECT hence we die. When ECT is slowed you get mitochondrial diseases. Health is simply the slowest form of death we create on the inner mitochondrial membrane. When these respiratory proteins stretch out in dimension and lenght we call this increasing heteroplasmy rate.

What is the linkage to these coupling events? Loss of redox power because of an increase of heteroplasmy of mitochondria in tissues. The redox potential is the critical linkage of the first two biologic cycle of energy conservation, namely ubiquitin and the cell cycle. These two processes are control by the central retinal pathways to couple to growth and metabolism cycles. The coupling of the mitochondria to the nucleus how light controls biology at all levels. This is precisely how light from our surfaces couples cell growth via ubiquitin rates via our surfaces. Blue and Red light in the sun are like predator and prey........they each perfectly balance one another. But modern artificial light has 4 times the amount of blue and no red. This destroys the coupled cycle. Why is this series so critical to understand? Because ubiquitin is the major stabilizer of negative feedback control on cell growth and it works via melanopsin which is the key opsin of the central retinal pathways. The actor of ubiquitin is LIGHT!!!! This explains why light is the most powerful drug we have. When you uncouple blue light from all the other frequencies you create an asymmetry that can be used to help or hurt you based upon the use of that blue light.
[http://www.nature.com/articles/srep27754](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.nature.com%2Farticles%2Fsrep27754&h=ATMuod73FifQC12BOPUE4YFyNpJfetuiiHOU0kVdNtBnAhjR2s4PcLB_KF8eTQot_fJxGXQH44SkNsTWQ7_CjxWblYDYiPxsb-V3LVOzho6zZZ7Qsz-hZklEy4S7ZH8pG-p4lL0DTg)

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/haarp-and-circadian-disruption.19426/post-213421>

So you think man cannot try to control the natural heartbeat of the Earth that your alpha wave is yoked to by frequency matching? You think man made light cannot effect your brain waves or your heart beat? You'd be dead wrong. The Schumann resonance and alpha wave are one and the same at 7.83 Hz. On an electroencephalogram (EEG) we get wave forms that depict the electrical activity occurring at the surface of the brain on the neocortex. That neocortex oscillates at 40 Hz in humans when they are healthy. The most familiar classification uses EEG waveform frequency in the alpha, beta, theta, and delta waveforms. Did you know that all of these different waves are linked by the Fibronacci sequence which is related to the golden ratio called PHI and this is related to the type of hydrogen isotopes you have in yoru brain in the neocortex and thalamus??? The Fibonacci sequence are all based on the longest Phi wave; also called the carrier wave that couples these waves and allows the waves to share their energy by molecular resonance. This golden ratio is required to connect the waves and allow energy transfer in both human Electrocardiogram (ECG) and the brainwaves in electroencephalogram (EEG) of humans. This means that the geomagnetic pulsations of the Earth are phase matched to the human brain and the energy contained in them can be shared with the human brain in wireless fashion via the actions of proton motions in the cells of the brain. Amazing what nature can do, isn't it? The alpha wave in the human brain controls sleep and much of crcadian coupling in the SCN. Did you know this? Are you beginning to see how pulsatile RF nnEMF can ruin circadian cycles yet because of the link to protons???? "We report results from the experiment aimed at the artificial excitation of extremely-low-frequency (ELF) electromagnetic waves with frequencies corresponding to the frequency of Schumann resonance (typically, 7.5 - 8.0 Hz frequency range). Electromagnetic waves with these frequencies can form a standing pattern inside the spherical cavity formed by the surface of the earth and the ionosphere. In the experiment the ELF waves were excited by heating the ionosphere with X-mode HF electromagnetic waves generated by the High Frequency Active Auroral Research Program (HAARP) facility in Alaska. The experiment demonstrates that heating of the ionosphere can excite relatively large-amplitude electromagnetic waves with frequencies in the range of the Schumann resonance, when the ionosphere has a strong F-layer and an electric field greater than 5 mV/m is present in the E-region."
[http://adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2014AGUFMSA11A3918S](http://adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2014AGUFMSA11A3918S)

Health is resilience to adversity built by mitochondrial strength powered by circadian cycles. The GTP gene set is controlled by proton motions in the type A rhodopsin system.

"The human brain is a biological organ. On one hand it is soft, flexible and adaptive, but on the other hand is relatively stable and coherent with well developed intelligence. In order to retain intelligent thinking in a soft and adaptive organ there needs to be a constant, globally available, synchronization system that continuously stabilizes the brain. The Schumann Resonance signal provides a brain frequency range matching electromagnetic signal, providing the synchronization needed for intelligence."

# Thread: Quantlet Success
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantlet-success.19432/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### emmao — 2017-05-06T03:24:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantlet-success.19432/post-213484>

A couple of months ago my family received a quantlet (just received a second and waiting on our third). I've played softball since I was about 5 years old and I'm continuing to play into college. I spend about 2 or 3 days a week playing softball and average about 7 games on the weekends during season. After high school season in the fall, I took a three month break because everytime I pitched I would get an overwhelming pain in my shoulder. When it was time to start in February, I had no energy to get through practice and dreaded pitching in fear that my arm would start hurting.

I started using the quantlet on performance and wellness for 60 minutes. Instantly I felt the effects of the quantlet on how I performed. I wouldn't be sore the next day and I could easily make it through a practice going 100%. I started throwing harder, more accurate, and confidently after using the quantlet. There's a huge difference in my performance depending on if I do the quantlet or not.

Downside, if I did the quantlet before practice at 6, for a full hour I could not sleep! I spent hours wide awake in bed even after wearing my goggles and turning all electronics on airplane mode. I have modified my time to about 30 minutes and I've noticed this reduction has let me sleep.

Is there such thing as too much light?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-06T05:20:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/quantlet-success.19432/post-213489>

nope........unless your dehydrated. Put this in your journal. It is good.

method is need to raise aldosterone and readjust electrolytes to get back to proper hydration level

# Thread: Hi from Scotland!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-scotland.19442/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### ScottishEmma — 2017-05-07T14:03:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-scotland.19442/post-213584>

Hi everyone,

I first listened to a podcast with Jack on Not Just Paleo about two years ago and whilst I enjoyed it I didn't truly implement the information.

As chance would have it I listened to another NJP podcast with Dr Mercola which led me back to Jack and now I've listened to what must be my sixteenth podcast interview thanks to this site and the wonders of YouTube.

So yeah, thanks to much of what I've been listening to (and bombarding my husband with - seriously, he must be fed up of the words Jack Kruse!), we're moving from the 56 latitude in Glasgow to the 36 latitude in Malaga this September and it's all thanks to this work! I've had enough of the constant cloud cover and lack of light here and I'm so excited to move to a place where I can (happily) be outdoors and on the ground most of the day!

I got into Daniel Vitalis a while back so spring water has been in my radar for a number of years. Even though we don't flouridate our water in Scotland, tap water is still pretty nasty and I can smell the chlorine from the water in our house. I get my water from a company who have a bore hole - not technically a spring - and it's pretty good. Occasionally I'll collect from a local spring but I'm not 100% convinced on the parasite front. When we move to Malaga I've found a spring about 20 minutes from where we are.

I have blue blocking lenses for me and my kids and I'm switching off circuit breakers at night. I also have just tried my first oyster, mussel and prawn! I've been upping my game fish-wise and I'm so frickin' relived to not be so bogged down with the food paradigm anymore. (I started my whole journey with raw veganism in 2007 and have been entrenched in all the usual, paleo, keto, bulletproof etc.)

I am totally ADDICTED to getting up at 5am to watch the sky for a half hour and getting outside as much as I can. The light in the morning is waking me up in new ways and I can't wait to see how this journey unfolds.

THANK YOU, JACK! I look forward to learning more...

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-07T17:25:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-scotland.19442/post-213593>

welcome. It sounds like you are becoming a mitochondriac........and that is a good thing. [http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/19381980.2016.1248325](http://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/19381980.2016.1248325)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-10T04:42:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patent-on-blue-light.19451/post-213788>

**The Mind Control Delivery System: Project: HAARP**
Many are predicting that biometrics will be required to access the Internet soon. The fact is that some what your biometric information because this is the first step in gaining control over your mind. The main threat to humanity lies in the fact that the technology exists to control all of human behavior at one time. A reasonable person would certainly ask “how is that possible?” To answer that question, I refer the readers to Nick Begich’s **[website](http://www.earthpulse.com/)** in which he details how mass electrical signals can beamed up from an array of antenna and bounced off the ionosphere in either a narrow beam covering a specific and defined geographic area, or it can be reflected back to earth in a broad beam application in which millions could be impacted by one electrical signal designed to change human behavior. We know this technology as Project HAARP.

United States Patent **_5,159,703,_** Lowery, October 27, 1992, _Silent Subliminal Presentation System_, Inventors: Lowery, Oliver M. Appl. No. 458339 Filed December 28, 1989, discusses the microwave technology necessary for mind control. In this patent, we witness the move from the mind control of certain individuals who might have been programmed, which is frightening enough, to witnessing entire populations being controlled. The patent discusses the possibility of impacting millions at a time. The net effect of the technology is enhanced when there is a transducer in the body such as an _**embedded microchip**_. I think the technology no longer needs the chip embedded........I think if it is close to your body I think it is now good enough when the network is upgraded to 5 G. This means tech gear can act nefariously in certain cases.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-10T04:48:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/patent-on-blue-light.19451/post-213791>

**A Very Ominous Warning From the Inventor of Electromagnetic Mind Control**
[](http://www.thecommonsenseshow.com/siteupload/2015/02/jose-delgado.jpg)
Jose Delgado, the reluctant pioneer of mind control technologies.

In his later years, and after working for the CIA for over 40 years in perfecting this technology, Dr. Delgado was very critical about the distinct lack of Bioethics and consumer protection with regard to these technologies. Until his death a few years ago, Delgado remained particularly critical of technologies which can change human nature. Delgado, stated the following:

_**“This technology has two sides, for good and for bad, and we should do what we can do to avoid the adverse consequences. We should try to prevent potentially destructive technologies from being abused by authoritarian governments to gain more power or by terrorists to wreak destruction. However, Delgado is a realist as he postulates “can you avoid knowledge? You cannot! Can you avoid technology? You cannot! Things are going to go ahead in spite of ethics, in spite of your personal ethics.”**_

The above quote from Delgado is a very ominous warning about how serious the subject of mind control is about to to become for every person. After researching Delgado’s past, it is clear that he felt the same about mind control as Einstein felt about the unveiling of nuclear weapons.

### drezy — 2017-07-15T00:30:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/technology-and-kids-are-a-bad-mix.19452/post-218862>

Maybe, it depends on many factors. I'm raising a 12 year old boy. As soon as I saw him fixing his hair I knew I had him pinned down.

I asked him two simple questions:

1.  Do you want to carry baby fat up until and through your 20's or do your want to get lean?
2.  Do you want girls to see you as a nice teddy bear or do you want them to almost irrationally seek your attention?

My boy is mart, so you can guess his answer easily enough. You are in a position to anticipate your children's priorities and seem like a damned genius for doing so. Over the last month and a half my boy grew near 0.50" and lost 13 lbs. His own momma is almost complaining to me how her doughy boy is getting more ripped and it disturbs her. Tough, because I've got a boy to raise and this house is a man's world:
