### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-13T00:32:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-food-this-question-is-driving-me-crazy.19461/post-216009>

Tonight's lesson: How does inflammation affect mitochondrial redox? When inflammation is raised mitochondrial energy production drops from cytochrome 1-3. This is where food electrons enter. This is why superoxide signaling goe awry i cytochrome 1. Sunlight bypasses cytochrome 1-3 provided UV-A is present. Why is that a key point most everyone misses? UV-A light makes nitric oxide (NO) and it shuts down cytochrome c oxidase and it makes melatonin. Look at the picture below. Red light in sunlight is by far the most abundant frequency making up 42% of sunlight. IR-A light makes massive amounts of ATP with no need for food. So how does inflammtion change things? Keap 1 has four discrete protein domains in which cysteine residues are critically important in stress, sensing of oxidation. This cysteine binding element also is where the NRF2 pathway binds directly to Keap 1. This shows you why cysteine sits in the most critical spot for oxidative sensing in all human cells.

The higher oxidation potential of inflammationmanifests as an extracellular decrease in the ratio of cysteine/cystine and a reduced maount of water made by mitochondria in the cytosol. These things result in an intracellular decrease in the ratio of GSH/GSSG and NAD(P)H/NAD(P). This implies that a metabolic shift in the redox state of the cell's mitochondria by inflammation is more important than ROS made in the mitochondria or on macromolecules. This is why the antioxidant theory of aging is flawed. So lower levels of cysteine and cystine are always seen when water is not being made and both are seen in aging and disease as the first signal from mitochondrion of where disease begins and accelerates the aging process.

Here is some of the complexity I am trying to leave out for the non scientific reading this. The Keap1-Nrf2 pathway plays a crucial role in determining the sensitivity of cells of the ABC transporters. ABC transporters are ATP-binding cassette transporters are members of a protein superfamily that is one of the largest and most ancient families with representatives in all extant phyla from prokaryotes to humans. ABC transporters are transmembrane proteins that utilize the electrons of adenosine triphosphate (ATP) and GTP to fully unfold biological proteins involved in translocation of various substrates across membranes and non-transport-related processes such as translation of RNA and DNA repair. Although most eukaryotic ABC transporters are effluxers, some are not directly involved in transporting substrates.

For example, both the cystic fibrosis transmembrane regulator (CFTR) and the sulfonylurea receptor (SUR) are directly affected by ATP's ability to cause a full open confirmation of its proteins for proper action. This is associated with the regulation of opening and closing of "ion channels demon proteins" using GTP. These proteins are affected by ATP and GTP levels to directly affect the protein conformational folding of the human ABC protein. Here again, we see ATP/GTP being used as an electron withdrawing cardinal absorbent to open the protein conformations of these proteins in membranes. This implies the more ATP we make, the more accurate these transporters will function in cellular signaling. This is why a high fat diet is beneficial for longevity and health. What food guru's forget is 2/3 of ATP in a human is made when that human is out in the sunlight that contains UV-A and IR-A light. Why? UV-A light makes nitric oxide which shuts down tunneling of electrons from cytochrome 1, 2, 3. Food electrons enter mitochondria there. Sunlight makes ATP at cytochrome c oxidase by turning off proximal ECT and using the 42% of IR-A light in sunlight. Sunlight provides double the amount of ATP production that food can provide. We seem to forget this. The more ATP made , the accuracy of cellular signaling, the less chance you age or get ill. This is why sunlight trumps food. Considering quantum cell theory, it really is that simple. Get sun and build your redox first and foremost.

We seem to forget this. The more ATP made , the accuracy of cellular signaling, the less chance you age or get ill. This is why sunlight trumps food. Considering quantum cell theory, it really is that simple. Get sun and build your redox first and foremost.\[/quote\]

This picture fascinates me. I am looking at the differences and similarities of plant and animal cells. I'm thinking of the human gut as being the portable equivalent of the root system. Just like a root system it forms symbiotic relationships with bacteria etc, and its in the dark. When the light conditions and the environment are right, a plant can take what it needs from the soil and it's root symbionts, and is essentially vegetarian. And some - dare I say it  - have even got humans to grow them in large numbers and look after them ... So in some high quantum yield environments human vegetarians could do well, but I think its about the light and how humans photosynthesise, much less about the chlorophyll given to the human root, because of:

Plants can go dormant for months, so humans couldn't have survived relying on chlorophyll in latitudes that have definite seasons. In plants with not so good (for them) environments, to survive some have adapted to become parasitic, insectivore and even carnivores, and I think there's a human parallel.

It comes from sunlight!

UV-A light make NO......and NO shuts down proximal ECT 1-3. Then Red light drives the ATPase to make ATP using the 42% of IR-A (paper from Nature above shows you red light turns water into a quantum torque machine to turn the ATPase and allow protons to move across the cell membrane from the matirx to the outer mitochondrial space. IR-A thermodynamically is not powerful enough to change the structure of things with mass but it capable of moving things with mass. Remember the pictures I showed in Vermont about the retina and where their is apuacity of blood vessels to photoreceptors? What moved the energy from the photoreceptor to the circulation with no problem? Water. IR-A light has a absorbance spectrum of 600 nm - 3000 nm to move things. It is the ultimate small powerful molecular quantum engine that operates at 100% efficiency to spins the ATPase and this spinning alone can make ATP while also pumping protons that have been excluded from EZ water by what? Sunlight! All there needs to be to work is an electric field present in the inner mitochondrial membrane which is present.

Now back to food..........When do carbs grow naturally? When UV light is present in the environment. This is why carbohydrates only make 36 ATP from one mole of glucose and fats make 147 ATP from one mole of palmitate. What makes up the deficit in carbohydrates? IR-A light from sunlight to spin the ATPase. Quantum Surprise! IR-A and UV-A light make ATP without any help required from carbohydrate electrons. Quantum biology 101. Food is just a different package of sunlight eukaryotes can use when sunlight is not powerful. But the sunlight via your eyes and skin have to match the food electrons you eat. They are excited by the incident EMF light of the sun.
PHOTOSYNTHESIS FORMS THE ENTIRE FOOD WEB. We forget that liquid crystalline water inside of leaves and planets is adjacent to lipid and protein interfaces provides excitation energy that enables incident light to split water into hydrogen and oxygen. It uses CO2 and water as substrate and the incident solar light as the energy source in photosynthesis to make sugar. This carbohydrate creation simultaneously generates food with electrons programmed by the solar ligth that created it. Those excited electrons contains the electric and magnetic fingerprint of solar light frequencies in cells that out mitochondria pay attention by making a specific free radical signal. That signal is key to how biochemistry can or cannot work.

I've been playing with acetyl l-cysteine lately and milk thistle - I read in Jack's book if glutathione is high, no pathogen can kill you - then there's Martha Herbert saying "It's glutathione, stupid"... I must say, even with a crappy 500 mg dose, it will stop an allergy dead in its tracks - I do get a lot of sunshine though - but sometimes I screw up and eat something that I can't digest and it is nice to know it is there in the ol' bag of tricks... I have also been having insane luck with magnesium oil and baking soda and salt - in fact I feel quite stupid - actually I feel that way most of the time - but if you walk the beach with your feet in the salt water (which is what magnesium oil actually is) and if you read the bipedalism blog where - it was unholy cool - he talks about how we are designed to absorb salt through the feet - and then I was reading how seawater actually has some astronomical amount of magnesium - we are designed to absorb salt and magnesium through our feet - which is why Jack says all you have to do is sit at the edge of the ocean and stick your feet in the water - I think if you popped some baking soda before you did that, it would be even better because you would create an exclusion zone which would transport the magnesium plus the salt would give your charge separation of water even more of a "kick" - add some epsom salts for the sulfate and hydrate yourself with ice water - and sleep is a beautiful thing I did this whole thing in the shower last night for all you people who don't have access to the ocean - rub epsom salts all over your body after you bathe - add lavender essential oil to it for fun - then rub magnesium oil on top of that and then coat the whole thing with baking soda - then do some yoga headstands against the wall or rub some accupressure points and you will start to sizzle - at that point, you will actually \*want\* your shower to be cold - which is what you do next - blast yourself with cold water - you can also just mix the magnesium oil in with the epsom salts - if you do this before bed, chase the whole experience with an infrared light and it feels so good

>

Conditions in the fields WWI made wounded soldiers prone to infection but physicians noticed that sunlight had a healing effect, so they encouraged the injured to sit in sunlight, with their wounds exposed.

World War I was a catalyst for innovation in medicine. The sheer number of wounded encouraged physicians to look at alternative treatments for diseases and injuries.

Before the war, light, both artificial and natural, was already being used to heal wounds, cure some forms of tuberculosis, and treat rickets, a Vitamin D deficiency stemming from a poor diet and lack of sunshine.

Richard Hobday, in his book “**The Healing Sun**,” notes that during the war, surgeons were confronted with gruesome wounds, some more difficult to treat than anything previously experienced, and that they had to be inventive to cope with the massive numbers of casualties.

“For the first time, surgeons had to deal with injuries caused by high-velocity weapons fired at close range,” Mr. Hobday writes. Conditions in the field made wounded soldiers prone to infection. With no antibiotics and a limited supply of disinfectants, physicians noticed that the sun’s rays had a healing effect, so they encouraged injured soldiers to sit with their wounds exposed to the light.

The war had also led to a spread in poverty and malnutrition, and a corresponding rise in cases of rickets, which causes bones to soften. An avoidable and curable disease, rickets mostly affects children. The experiences of battlefield physicians prompted more doctors to treat rickets with sunlamps.

Around the end of the war, Kurt Huldschinsky, a physician in Berlin, also found that ultraviolet radiation from a vapor lamp, a form of sunlamp, cured rickets. Sunlamps have since been used to treat skin disorders, burns, sleep disorders, neonatal jaundice and even some psychiatric disorders.

In the aftermath of World War I, physicians also took a closer look at the dietary causes of rickets. The surge in the number of children affected gave rise to two popular theories—that rickets was caused by a poor diet, or that it was caused by lack of fresh air and sunlight. The answer was both.

“It was the terrible level of child hunger/starvation in the city in the aftermath of the war that made this research possible,” says Dr. Roberta Bivins, director of the Centre for the History of Medicine at the University of Warwick, in England. A Viennese study to see whether vitamin supplements and dietary changes could successfully treat deficiencies in humans proved that rickets was mostly a disease of nutrition, entirely curable with a cheap vitamin D supplement, she notes.

Pretty interesting info on sunlight & medicine hidden deep in the medical library - found this gem in a book from 1917. A bit dusty but full of great info.

.

Yeah - well that's really cool - how sunlight shuts down cyto 1-3 - but then if that's true, why do we have to eat breakfast at all? I mean, if you go into the sun after breakfast, aren't you saying the electrons from the food won't be transported?

"It stops ECT from 1-3 using melatonin. What keeps us alive at night? Light.......what do cells release at night Matty? ELF-UV light right? This keeps the ATPase functioning well enough to make ATP when men are working on their heteroplasmy rates. Look at pic below now also from Vermont:"

And that's why in spite of drinking too much wine last night, I actually slept great because I popped a massive dose of acetyl l-cysteine for the hell of it - very cool thread - and talk about the sea within...

[http://www.phaelosopher.com/2006/12/01/magnesium-medical-miracle-from-the-sea/](http://www.phaelosopher.com/2006/12/01/magnesium-medical-miracle-from-the-sea/)

"Rene Quinton “was the first person to discern and to investigate the similarity between sea water and mammalian lymph and blood plasma systems. He endeavored to solve the mysteries of sea water and its compatibility with living organisms by proceeding to carry out several scientific experiments in hospitals. The results he obtained were dramatically unexpected, since several people who were dying regained their vigor. Rene Quinton worked in collaboration with Drs. Potocki, Mace and Jarricot, and for four years he labored at perfecting his techniques and adjusting dosages. In 1904, he released the results of his hospital experiments in a book entitled, ‘L’eau de mer, milieu organique’ (Sea Water: Organic Medium). The book was re-issued in 1995 because of the timeliness of its well- researched contents.”
[\[ii\]](http://00000814/%23_ednref2) In one experiment, René Quinton and his medical team drained a dog of all of its blood and replaced it with isotonic (diluted) seawater. The dog should have died immediately, one would think, but the dog lived. On day two after the transfusion, 50% of the blood components had reappeared. By day four, almost 100% of the missing blood components were restored in what appeared to be proof of biological transmutation (a change from one element to another). Not only did the blood completely regenerate, but soon after the procedure the dog bounced around like a puppy with greater vitality than before, and it lived for many years afterwards."

# Thread: DO I REALLY NEED ALL THESE SUPPLEMENTS?

### Dennis Durost — 2017-05-13T01:25:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-really-need-all-these-supplements.19468/post-213940>

I am 75 and have been a big supplement taker for years. I currently take 22 different supplements. About six months ago I got into Intermittent fasting and fast for 16 hours a day. When I do eat it is high fat and low carb. About three months ago I started a rather rigorous exercise program developed by Doug McGuff, MD that involves one 15 minute workout a week doing 5 machine exercises to failure. I do the workout during the last hour of a fast. Several weeks ago I found Dr Kruse and have jumped in with both feet. I now rise at dawn to get some sunlight, spend most of my day grounded, wear my blue blocker glasses when called for and turn off the circuit breaker to our bedroom at night to reduce the dirty electricity effect. I pan on getting a magnetic mattress as well as the new devise to be released later this month. I also plan to start today with a CT session. Just had a nice fatty grass fed burger and am staring at that glass of ice water. With all that said, is there a core group of supplements I should be taking? Hopefully I can get rid of much of what I am now taking and save a few bucks in the process.

### drezy — 2017-05-13T03:42:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-really-need-all-these-supplements.19468/post-213947>

I'm a baby at 40 compared to your 75 years young.

-   Until recently was a big supplement user (up to 10 different ones)
-   For the last 10 years I've been paleo into keto-paleo half way in. I could always hush a room of paleo acolytes with the statement "I've been on it for over a decade."
-   Prior to Doc Kruse I was effectively able to get to a fast like yours easily with some supplements
-   With many factors like no sun, bad environment, not enough DHA, and [neoglucogenesis](http://medical-dictionary.thefreedictionary.com/Neoglucogenesis) I slowly managed to pack on pounds.
-   As a taller gent I only put it on around my kidneys (proto saddlebags). Old friends never noticed but I knew and it bugged me.
-   What changed ? sunlight and CT and DHA See ( [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/i-think-im-turning-into-a-mitochondriac.19191/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/i-think-im-turning-into-a-mitochondriac.19191/) )
-   250lbs & 17-ish % body fat to now 227lbs and 13ish %. Zero workouts (minus 2 in 3.5 months that my wife insisted on me joining) zero supplements(not suggesting your path, just detailing mine).
-   Why zero workouts and zero supplements? What Jack was saying seemed so over the top I decided I would just jump in with both feet like you. I'm happy I tried it. The 40 yo me could kick the 30yo me's ass at pretty much anything now like endurance, memory, strength, the boudoir performance.

You're well on your way and I'm cheering for you adding CT. In the beginning, and if I spent any time away from CT, I just imagined that my son's life was in danger if I didn't just go all into the CT tub. It sounds really silly and melodramatic but it got my butt into the cold quicker.

Tips I've learned from Jack and other members that might apply to you:

-   Get the dminder app if you have a device. Simply seeing your abysmal D stats (yeah it's not all about D, but functionally this is great) for a week will shame you into getting more sun with less clothing on. Having a UV plan is great. Feeling ashamed of you current calculated D level is way better. I even have a calculated D level competition going with my wife. Let me tell you as a husband any competition with your wife where she happily removes her top just to beat you is a fine competition with me! Did I maybe bump up her fitzpatrick skin type up one level in the app so she needs to remain nude near me longer, you ask? No comment.
-   Never forget your morning (or anytime) "wood-o-meter" (thanks @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) for helping with this most critical of all tests). That is a good indicator of progress. It sounds crude but never lies about your hormones, stress levels, blood pressure and many more stats. If you find yourself thinking "Hey, I remember this wood from a decade and a half ago" then you are on the right path.

### Dennis Durost — 2017-05-13T07:11:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-really-need-all-these-supplements.19468/post-213948>

Here is my summary response. I appreciate all the insight and recommendations. Let me respond:
1\. Don't like fish in general and am allergic to shell fish. Instead I've started on Jack's recommended Nordic Naturals.
2\. I don't do structured water but do consume reverse osmosis. I believe Jack said that was ok in one of his webnars.
3\. The sups kind of accumulated over time. Each one was added based on a perceived need at the time. Selenium, Vit C , Iodine and Olive Leaf Extract cured my thyroid problem and Ultra Prostragen got my PSA back under 1. I plan on cutting back slowly per the recommendation.
4\. JanSz: Glad you are using the HCL with pepsin. Everyone over 50 should be taking at least on capsule with each meal to assist in digestion. I cured my acid reflux by HCL supplementation. Initially took six capsules with each meal since my stomach acid was so low. Within a week after starting HCL all reflux symptoms vanished. I then straightened out my gut biome and was able to titrate down to now only needing one with a meal.
5\. Got into paleo about 5 years ago since a tennis partner was writing a book on the subject (Art DeVany, New Evolution Diet). Art also steered me to a supplement I will never quit and that is Glutathione. Art claimed he had not had a cold in 15 years. I, on the other hand, was plagued with chronic bronchitis every two or three years. Started Setria Glutathione 4 years ago and have not had a single viral issue.
6\. Did first CT today. I am muscular and lean all over except for stomach and love handles. Am doing a spot treatment using a waist trim belt and ice packs. Went for an hour but almost caved after just three minutes. Fortunately some numbing set in and I was good to go. Plan to do daily for a month and see what transpires. My wife thinks the CT is crazy. Her recommendation is I simply dump the beer and all will be good. Sure.

Again, thanks for the input

1.  Makes me a potential Manchurian Jack candidate
2.  Helps me grasp the framework faster

This is something contextual that you may help you be able to optimize your fish oil (put to ~1:34:0 5ish):

Also a personal anecdote and if you are cooking oriented, my father hates fish more than anyone I know. If you say "fish for dinner" near him he'll make a face like he just stepped in a fresh dog pile. He just hates the stuff. I'm a foodie kinda guy so this was always the challenge that, if met, would be the crowning achievement of my lifetime. Frankly, James Beard could keep his award if this was attainable.

I wasn't screwing around and knew I only had one shot to trick the old man. After trials and errors on my own, I finally settled on an ahi tuna steak that was as about as robust and meaty as a beef as far as I could tell. My dad dropped off my son after my son's only-grandchild-gets-to-be-prince for hours visit. I asked him "does this taste about right?". He took the bait and said, "Yeah, beefy."

I'd love to stick around longer on this planet, but If I get bit by a rattler snake tomorrow, there is some peace and sense of accomplishment at my core now.

The process involved brining and hot smoking. If you or your wife is cooking oriented hit me with a pm/convo if you'd like or I could slap up the basics in the quieter "recipes" section of the forums.

Here is a quick lesson in how circadean biology and blue light cause your stomach acid to go to crap - blue light at night will kill melatonin production - without melatonin, your beta cells continue to pump insulin - insulin uses zinc - you need zinc to make stomach acid... are you seeing a trend yet? If you get cold, your respiratory proteins get closer together to make energy - if they are damaged (high heteroplasmy), this will not go well... you need to generate a small burst of superoxide to generate a repair signal to your mitochondria... many people on this forum use nicotine for that - the sunrise will do it for you in your eyes... which are also a clock which needs to run quicker than all the other organs in your body also sporting a clock... seafood is the best and quickest way to get your cells to work - you need electrons from the earth, cells that can hold a charge and sunshine to power it all -

I use supplements for particular hacks - usually damage control from crappy choices - you can get all the zinc you need from oysters, selenium from brazil nuts - really, if I want a heat wave, I just pop 4 of those babies and I can feel my body heating up - gouda cheese will give you k2 also - the whole thing is driven by the sun...

The easiest way IMHO to structure water is to put it out in the sun for 4 hours or stick a mint leaf in it and leave it outside - or a venturi or ice it and swirl it -

I played 3 hours of tennis yesterday in the heat with nicotine gum and my son said I was on fire... it was amazing - it would have been even more amazing if I had some ice water there too -

I also drink lots of coconut water for the potassium -

### JanSz — 2018-04-14T16:55:46+0300

At one time everything was perfect.

At this time we think that that place was near current Lake Victoria.
So, we mad an error.
We started mowing away from that perfect place.
That was 100's millions years ago.
Earth crust moves a lot, up-down or sideways.
Earth moves thru different space and time, where it has newer been.
Planetary configurations changes, orbits change, new planets appear in our system, we are constantly going thru different Birkeland currents.
Also, time change, so likely the place was not perfect any more.
Since that perfect day, (or period of very long time from pov individual life span), we are loosing brain weight, (on average), (generation by generation).
We are still making technological progress, but that is only killing us faster (on average).
Single geniuses do not change average trends.
Well, maybe some do. Lets not loose a hope.
Paradigm shift, please come.
Assume that it will be good for future of humanity.
How good that would be from individual's pov?
.

.................

### Sue-UK — 2018-04-15T11:59:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/do-i-really-need-all-these-supplements.19468/post-239425>

With the shit off a shingle concept, I think it could also depend on light frequencies - an example nicotine conversion to vitamin B3 in the presence of UV and oxygen. [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/another-nad-question.13775/page-3](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/another-nad-question.13775/page-3). Animals who don't obtain a nutrient from their diet naturally, or if food is scarce, may be making it by transmuting one (readily available) element to another, using their natural environment. It could also be down to tissue hydration, (particularly for the water soluble nutrients), and the ability to recycle, for example during periods of hibernation. Then there's storage ... it may be normal to have high levels at a certain time of year, and for it to drop in line with the light cycle or cold, which could trigger behavioural changes, like the urge to seek out specific whole foods when they become available, or migrate to get it. In humans, trading cooperation to get high iodine food sources may have enabled living in low iodine areas. Cooperation has huge survival value, and environmental pressure may have helped drive this aspect of brain development.

I might crave a particular food for several days, then not want it for weeks. As my vitamin d storage drops I crave the sun even more and make more effort to get it. Fluctuating levels of nutrients affects my behaviour, whether it's craving liver for a few days, or booking a week in the sun. If food is information, in the right light cycle it provides the context of the whole food, I avoid supplements because I don't want them creating "noise" that interferes with the environmental information I sense as a whole, including food, which could affect my decision making. Supplements are OK in an emergency as a short term treatment, but as I'm constantly hacking my environment, and with the nutrient profile of seafood, as a lifestyle choice it's not for me.

When I do eat it is high fat and low carb. I do the workout during the last hour of a fast. Several weeks ago I found Dr Kruse and have jumped in with both feet. I now rise at dawn to get some sunlight, spend most of my day grounded, wear my blue blocker glasses when called for and turn off the circuit breaker to our bedroom at night to reduce the dirty electricity effect. I pan on getting a magnetic mattress as well as the new devise to be released later this month. I also plan to start today with a CT session. Just had a nice fatty grass fed burger and am staring at that glass of ice water. With all that said, is there a core group of supplements I should be taking? Hopefully I can get rid of much of what I am now taking and save a few bucks in the process.

Should we be taking supplements?

**Definitely NOT**!

But caveat is only if:

-   We had health conscious parents and our mom during our gestation in womb ate very healthy, did not drink or smoke or used prescription drugs, avoided nnEMF, she chose a natural vaginal birth, and she breast fed us to complete weaning.
-   You are in top health and relatively young either chronologically or biologically.
-   You live and work in a wonderfully healthy location with no undue stress, and are exposed to no air, water, or nnEMF pollution.
-   You are blessed with wonderful health promoting genetic endowments with few genetic abnormalities or SNPs.
-   You have access to bountiful, nutrient dense natural organic foods grown in rich, fertile, biodynamic soils free of pesticides, glyphosate, and other contaminants.
-   You drink the purest, most life-giving water on planet free of contaminants.
-   You follow Dr Kruse's recommendations about sun exposure, light, etc.

Otherwise-- I think you **absolutely need** natural-sourced full spectrum dietary supplements according to your individual needs, health status, toxicity, and age-- all taken appropriately.

There are three common eucaryotes that eat scientifically recommended food (mostly).
They can be put into two categories.

1\. people and pets
2\. industrial animals, fed by the farming industry

Examine how people and pets are fed.
Examine how animals **that must be very healthy when slaughtered** are fed.
\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
When one would count our cells, we are mostly gut bacteria.
When we are sick, we go to the doctor. Doctor gets paid when we are sick.
Think of a doctor's incentives.
When the farmer takes care of his animals, **the sick animal gets slaughtered first and discarded.**
Vet gets paid to keep most animals healthy, growing fast, **(excreting lots of good gut bacteria)**
The vet that can't arrange for that gets fired.
**I am not talking about vets that care for pet animals.**
Animal food cost $$.
Farmer wants a healthy animal that cost less to feed.
**A major part of the Solution:**
**Fatty acids content of the feed.**

And not only get:
Fatty Acid Profile, Comprehensive (C8-C26), Serum
but
**employ your neurons in finding better ways of keeping fatty acids in their sweet ranges.**

### Katie Durham — 2019-10-07T18:11:10+0300

Well, my girlfriend works in a daycare/kindergarten and they make a point of trying to be outside as much as possible whenever the weather allows it. Meaning when it's not heavy rain they usually spend a couple of hours outside each day. The youngest kids also have their daily nap in strollers outside. Only when it gets really cold the napping is done inside. There are a lot of immigrant children where my girlfriend works and their parents are not used to the outside napping and are generally more skeptical of their kids being outside and getting dirty compared to Norwegian parents.
I suspect we eat more fish than Americans,salmon in particular. Cod liver oil (tran) is also something that can be found in several households. Lamb is also popular in the fall and at Easter time.

On the other hand, the general wealth level is quite high in this country and that means tablets, phones and other tech are something that can be found in almost every home. Children often have their own units from an early age. Life expectancy and general health level are something I think will change very soon. Went to the local swimming pool for a sauna session yesterday and most of the kids there was either overweight or just didn't look healthy. Whenever I go to the local supermarket I leave with the same impression - there is a lot of people that look unhealthy and no one bats an eye

### Inger — 2018-11-23T12:32:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-and-timing.19472/post-256311>

Yes, Andreas. I second what you say.
I guess it will change soon.... more tech means less outside too, in addition to the increased nnEMF.

As a Norwegian, my mom also always put us outside for daytime naps, even in the coldest winter! And windows were always open in the bedroom in the night.. I recall it snowing on my face as kid..lol
That is why I am addicted to sleep by an open window I guess.. It is just in my blood.

Traditionally, Norwegians spend lots of time outside, at least my family did. We made picnics a lot.. went to the lake and sea swimming with my parents, went up in the mountains walking etc. My grandparents did the same. Children were sent outside to play. So we played a ton outside as kids. As up to 3-4 years old often completely naked in the sun..lol

My new neighbors wife is from the Philippines, and my landlord invited us all for dinner on Monday. It was interesting to hear how life is on the Philippines. They are inside in air condition all the time as it is too hot to be outside. What a bummer! So they live in such strong sun but they all avoid it... Except when they go to the beach for swimming as they can cool off in the water, she said.

So weirdly, in Scandinavia, some people might get way more sun on their skin as most people do at the equator, because up north people really love being out in the sun when it is there. You go out into the sun to warm up, the less clothes the better the warm up effect
Well... at least it used to be so..

# Thread: Big news - Hydrogen bonds directly detected for the first time
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-news-hydrogen-bonds-directly-detected-for-the-first-time.19473/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lahelada — 2017-05-14T02:54:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-news-hydrogen-bonds-directly-detected-for-the-first-time.19473/post-214015>

[https://m.phys.org/news/2017-05-hydrogen-bonds.html](https://m.phys.org/news/2017-05-hydrogen-bonds.html)
[https://m.phys.org/news/2017-05-hydrogen-bonds.html](https://m.phys.org/news/2017-05-hydrogen-bonds.html)
"
Hydrogen is the most common element in the universe and is an integral part of almost all organic compounds. Molecules and sections of macromolecules are connected to one another via [hydrogen atoms](https://phys.org/tags/hydrogen+atoms/), an interaction known as hydrogen bonding. These interactions play an important role in nature, because they are responsible for specific properties of proteins or nucleic acids and, for example, also ensure that water has a high boiling temperature."

" To date, it has not been possible to conduct a spectroscopic or electron microscopic analysis of hydrogen and the [hydrogen bonds](https://phys.org/tags/hydrogen+bonds/) in single molecules, and investigations using [atomic force microscopy](https://phys.org/tags/atomic+force+microscopy/) have also not yielded any clear results.

Dr. Shigeki Kawai, from Professor Ernst Meyer's team at the Swiss Nanoscience Institute and the Department of Physics at the University of Basel, has now succeeded in using a high-resolution atomic force microscope to study hydrogen atoms in individual cyclic hydrocarbon compounds."

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-16T02:34:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/big-news-hydrogen-bonds-directly-detected-for-the-first-time.19473/post-214097>

Lots of truth bombs here tied to the April 2016 webinar and all the work on CD in EZ water and hydrogen bonding on water becoming opaque under compression with temperature as it is adjacent to mitochondria at small scales. H302 = perfect black box radiator that is a perfect toplogic insulator called the MINOS around a mitochondria, made by a mitochondria because it reverses the reaction of photosynthesis. I cannot stress how much this video is the key to understanding water.

# Thread: Hello From the Florida Space Coast

### NADme — 2017-05-14T12:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-florida-space-coast.19474/post-214023>

Greetings from the Florida Space Coast. Former rocket man; mission critical desk in support of Space Shuttle operations to include without limitation Hubble, Galileo, Magellan and other STS payloads. Ground Operations and Solid Rocket Boosters. Other DoD/NASA engagements to include many years in Washington, DC.

Presenting with Hemochromatosis (I am Iron Man), Atrial Fibrillation (Move over Tin Man), Secondary Hypogonadism (iron enriched that drag on the ground), Hyperurecemia (Gout without a doubt), Hypo-pituitary with a T2\* MRI confirming for depositive iron on my pituitary and liver using both turbo and side echo modalities. Moderate stroke in 2004 with 1 cm infarct to basal ganglia.

My life ever since has been dedicated to the science of it all as a guy with a background in contract administration, negotiations and music. As a result of my own personal health plight I have adopted a mindset that if I need something, build out to it, generate revenue from it and enable myself access and at least enough revenue to self pay without cost impact.

I am now a Co-Owner of a substance abuse detox and rehab facility in Cocoa Village, Florida. My business partner is a Georgetown MD with a Yale degree in chemical engineering. We are infusing with NAD+ and detoxing a variety of addictions to include heroin and alcohol. I have trained at the longest standing NAD+ clinic in America. I will start to infuse myself on a regular basis near term, which will also include nutritional IV's that I will cocktail as a result of various labs.

Associated research has resulted in patent pending work that includes; evolving a means to deliver a molecule that acts as an adpatogen and levels the effects of testosterone therapy in men, to include LH, FSH, cortisol, aramotase/E2 leveling and other unique features that appear to have a positive affect on breast and prostate cancer (to be determined through trials). It also eliminates testicular atrophy caused by testosterone therapy thereby eliminating the need for HCG. This adaptogen has been lost in the world of sauce do to the ignorance of man, the mighty dollar of advertising and the ills of professional athletic abuse that has kept those in need of therapeutic relief access from something classed schedule 3 without warrant. I am also working on, and am very close, to deliver a protocol/formulation that should convert many in atrial fibrillation back into normal sinus rhythm and have the ability to sustain NSR.

My current focus is on calcium homeostasis and NAD utilization to achieve such a state. Personally I want off a calcium channel blocker for rate control. I have been intrigued by certain entries on this forum specific to NAD, EMF and Ca related. There was a particular entry that cited use of calcium channel to mitigate the effects of EMF. I believe citing the work of Dr. Ying, that Ca homeostasis, a profound element of mitochondria core and optimization, can be achieved by NAD therapy, although certain adjacent supplementation is required, ie. DHA, etc. The ribose expressions are key.

I was made aware of Dr. Kruse and company when an associate on a different men's health board, Janzs, made references to the work of Dr. Kruse. I sure hope he is still around, I miss corresponding with him. I live as a man of faith and believe in Yeshua/Jesus as Lord and Savior. Hopefully my time here will be a benefit and service to others.

Glory to God.

If we can define basal, temp at point of waking, before any physical movement, then my basal is very low. My thyroid levels all come back ranged and leveled. I have not checked cortisol for a while but from what I remembered was alright.

I came to a conclusion about 6 months ago that i need to avoid oral ingesting of any supplement that is forced to do a liver pass and GI tract, etc. Infusions, injections, nasal, SL, suppository, transdermal patches are how i operate now.

By example, I was taking Life Extension B complex, a solid formula. I never really had a wellness presence from them. I use a lipo spray under the tongue and my daily temp sustains 99.5-99.9. Also, selenium and iodine has an effect, even though i do not supplement with them right now.

As for fatty, at least 20 lbs of medically induced water weight by the calcium channel blocker diatalizem and who knows how much more with allopurinol.

How do I know? Well you know me Janz and you know I keep it real, so I tapered diatalizem, 12lbs water weight off in 3 days with no change in diet or exercise. in a few weeks 20 lbs down, tachycardia ensues, back to ER to get a cardizem push and IV with heart rate sustained at 180 BPM+

Round and round we go, but it is going to be very interesting to see how me loading NAD is going to affect Ca homeostasis and how it relates to the SA VA nodes and calcium channels. As a side note. I have one RF electric meter 10 feet from my head on the other side of a layer of concrete block and the house next door in close with another RF electric meter pointing my way.

I provided this Dr. Kruse citation to a metnor and colleage engrossed in NAD research:

And Jane non native EMF causes calcium efflux in every cell in the body. Ions in any static and uniform magnetic field will typically move in a circle with a cyclotron frequency determined by its charge q, its mass m, and the strength of the magnetic field called B. The operation of a cyclotron is based on the fact that the period of the motion of a charged particle in a uniform magnetic field is independent of the velocity of the particle, as can be seen in the following mathematical derivation:

F = ma

qvB = mv2/r

Solving for r:

r = mv/qB

Now find the period, T:

T = 1/f = 2π/ω = 2πr/v = 2πmv/qBv

Since the velocities’s cancel:

T = 2πm/qB

The “Cyclotron Frequency” f (c) immediately follows and is:

F© = qB/2πm

These ion cyclotron resonance effects were extensively investigated in Blackman’s lab but elsewhere in the 1980s up until today. All laboratories who have studied these effects found that calcium cyclotron frequency directly affects calmodulin regulation of the calcium ion concentration in solution and a host of biologic functions. These are all tied to marked alterations of voltage dependent calcium channels when the field strength is above 30 Hz. To date, 23 studies have been done and repeated this finding since 1980. Anything that affects calmodulin by definition affects magnesium ion concentrations as well in a cell. All of these things directly affect the cytochromes in mitochondria. These proteins are designed to act to sense charged particles like electrons and protons and their spins and energies just like an MRI machine does. We understand the inputs to mitochondria and the outputs but biology has no clue about how mitochondria really work. The reason? It is a QED story not a biologic one. This is why an altered field is primordial to calcium and magnesium regulation. Taking more calcium or magnesium do nothing for the intracellular voltage channels. Using calcium channel blockers however can help marginally. Altering your native environmental EMF is the smartest move.

Why is Calcium ion frequency important for you to understand? Ion cyclotron resonance is a phenomenon related to the movement of ions in a magnetic field. today the Earth's magnetic field is no longer in its native form. It affects the NAD+ ratio's found in mitochondria directly. This is no longer a food story, it is an electrostatic charge story. Gilbert Ling said that in 1952 to deaf ears. This is why it is in biology's blind spot. Moreover, it links the photoelectric effect to the magnetic field that ions are in. Whatever the frequency of the electromagnetic field strength is dictates how biochemistry reactions will be altered for the better or the worse.

### drezy — 2017-05-15T18:36:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-florida-space-coast.19474/post-214080>

> [NADme said:](/goto/post?id=214046)
>
> In theory if I can quantify the wave form, then could not I tone generate an inverted signal to negate and secure my sleeping environment?

Let me lay out why you should simplify your approach to "change you environment" and then share my major aha moment with Jack's material. Forgive any technical showboating I promise theres a big point at the end.

If we are talking wifi routers and cell towers sadly even if you knew the exact data content of the packets (that shapes the signal) and the technical specifications of every device around you even ignoring the frequency hopping that wifi devices do ([https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frequency-hopping\_spread\_spectrum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Frequency-hopping_spread_spectrum)), you really have a laws of physics problem on your hands.

The basics:
The velocity of EMwaves through air is ~ 299,702,534 m/s that's .98 ft/ns if you are 15 feet away you only have ~15ns to act
Worse, for such a system the velocity of any electron in your "detector" and system wires is ~ .64\*c or .62 ft/s
Even worse, that was just detection you'd need processing and then to send the inverted signal through a perfectly aimed antenna (meaning more .64\*c slow speed).
Just to add a little more worse to all the rest, for every one inch your detector is away from your processor the signal and the processor from the counter-signal antenna, as the signal trudges through metal (and slows to .64c) the originating signal gains a relative 4 inches per ns position advantage.

So why would I be such a technical little Debbie Downer here with you?

First, when you think about "changing your environment" I'd like you to take simpler more practical measures that are more effective. Many over-engineered attempts will actually suffer from the law of unintended consequences and make for far worse environments if one is not very careful.

Second, all of this was part of my aha moment about a quarter of the way of catching up with what Jack has laid out there. In a nutshell I spent over 12 years designing high speed systems. One massive battle I fought was exactly this battle with material physics. Wires are not as wirelike as you think once you send high speed signals (and high frequencies through them).

-   A wire actually slows down and transduces high frequencies
-   A wire transduces this frequency into magnetic and (effectively) chemical energy fields
-   As a rule of thumb higher frequencies are absorbed through less distance of wire, lower frequencies penetrate deeper

I looked and finally \*saw\* the below picture from the forum and about messed my pants. \*mind blown\* In the bulletpoints above you can exchange "wire" with "body" and "frequencies" with "sunlight". My decade plus "problem" is 1:1 analogous with what is keeping me healthy.

Above we looked at hypothetically designing a complex RF system. We already **ARE** a complex EMF system.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-05T17:40:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-cloudy-manchester-uk.19476/post-215428>

From Kris90, a member here who just posted this, "Just got back from Vermont this weekend, all I can say it WOW. Not only did I meet @[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/), but I spent a great deal of time with him. I had breakfast with him twice, and we lived like the sphinx yesterday finally getting some AM sun in Vermont. For those who have not yet met Jack, all I can say is anything you think you know about him in NO WAY compares to the person he actually is. He is the most genuine, caring, and coolest dude I've ever met! The thing about Jack that blows me away is how he makes time for everyone. He probably talked to 100s of people over the course of 48 hours, and he would NEVER dismiss anyone even though he was bombarded with curious minds. I would say I probably spent about 12 hours with Jack (or atleast in his presence) and of that I probably got 2 hours of his time, which is incredible when you think of it. Those few hours I got directly speaking to Jack, listening to him and having him listen to me were among the most valuable hours of my life.

To sum up this experience in a sentence, I got the largest dopamine boost of my life. I also got to spent a good deal of time with @[Matty\_M](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/11546/) and his friend Brian, the Quantum Thieves. These kids blew me away. At 17 years old, just finishing high school, they are so wise and so well informed. Both of them appear to be at a crossroad where I see a map of endless pathways for them to take, and there is no doubt in my mind, whatever they decide will lead to success. That is because these boys have higher dopamine than anyone of their age, and they have the ability to listen to their instincts, and can build time out of the sunlight they allow in their life. No one I know at that ripe age is able to do that. Not only does @Matty\_Mhave high dopamine, but he also strives for even higher levels. He convinced me to take a dip in the lake on Saturday evening. The water must have been 40 F, and we both stood in the lake, submerged waist-down for probably 30 minutes, chilling (literally) in the moonlight, raising each other's dopamine as we had a very special, meaningful discussion about life. He was brave enough to fully submerge after about 30 minutes, as I chickened out, haha!

From the amount of Jack's time I received, I felt the reassurance that the environment I have put myself in is about as optimal as it can be living at the 45th latitude. Jack confirmed that my optimal health is going to depend on an environment with lots of CT, epi-paleo, ketosis, fasting and getting as much damn sun as I can yield year-round whether it's 30 C and sunny, and I can be out naked, or -30 C out in the snow. As much as I would like to relocate closer to the equator, the best thing I can for myself is continue to live in the ancient pathway mentioned in CT 6 (my favorite blog): [https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-6-the-ancient-pathway](https://www.jackkruse.com/cold-thermogenesis-6-the-ancient-pathway). Some biohacking ideas would be to build an infrared sauna, or fire-burning sauna and possible invest in a quantlet, as well as get down south every year. I am hoping to make a trip to Mexico for some more quantum fun this year! We shall see.

Jack's presentation was absolutely DYNAMITE! It was awesome seeing him blow away the food gurus and really helped open everyone's eyes. Be sure to look for it on YouTube, as I know it was recorded. I saw it front row, first hand, but I can't wait to watch it again.

My own personal takeaway is this: its easy to thrive when you hear Jack's message loud and clear. Here it is for myself in a nutshell at the 45th latitude:

1) Get sun
2) Less sun/lower quantum yield = more DHA and more CT
3) Fasting and/or ketosis = stimulate autophagy, be a fat burner to feed electrons through Complex II of ETC, maximizing energy efficiency with minimal ROS

It's literally that simple. Now I'll have more time because I don't have to worry about things that are more insignificant like calories, macros, vitamins, supplements, exercise, etc. I will exercise to move, and I will eat for the social aspect. Everything else I do will be about maximizing my energy, and recycling it, so I can hold it within my body and not lose it to my environment."

### Jack Kruse — 2017-06-12T22:11:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-cloudy-manchester-uk.19476/post-215990>

Change comes fastest to those people who are capable of unlearning and relearning. People who do not listen to feedback, will not be able to pivot based on changing circumstances, because they refuse to acknowledge reality. This is tied to a low dopamine state. Moreover, because they think they already have all the answers, they are not open to receiving feedback or contemplating advice designed to help them.

I look for and seek people who are honest about their limitations, and who are willing to put in the hard work needed to find out the answers in their environment to what they don't know, rather than someone who is happy remaining confidently ignorant.

The buck does stop with you, but you do need to be humble and curious , so that you can roll with the punches the environment throws at you.

Until you ALTER your thinking, you will always recycle your experiences...............I don't waste my energy or knowledge on people who “cry” for help but don't really want to hear the truth. Your intelligence is a powerful force that will change people's way of thinking, but only when they're ready. Paul is not. Most out there in the world are conditioned “pharmaceutical or food guru sheep” that have been led for years that masking the disease is the way forward not disease prevention or the promise of a disease reversal. I am not emotional about the truth's nature has revealed.

Before you make an opinion, look at all the facts and not just the one's you agree with, A truly wise person is willing to be wrong so they can be illuminated by the truth. . You cannot help people......only they can help themselves. I try to be a rainbow to those who seek help but I cannot clear the climate in their world, only they can. Few will even try to help themselves, so we must move quickly through the walking dead to find the living among us in this world. Again good luck Paul. This thread is now closed.

### Evgeni — 2017-05-15T12:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-214073>

I will redecorate my room this summer and have to choose a color. Any ideas what color to choose for the walls in the place I spent most time when I am indoors ?

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-16T02:32:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-214096>

Red. Google Finsen and red rooms.......you'll learn why it is wise.

### Lahelada — 2017-05-16T14:26:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-214114>

I just moved into a house with a burgundy bedroom. Too early to say anything definite as other factors are involved but I dream a lot more.

### JanSz — 2017-05-16T16:45:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-214128>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=214096)
>
> Red. Google Finsen and red rooms.......you'll learn why it is wise.

[http://photobiology.info/HistFinsen/HistFinsen.html](http://photobiology.info/HistFinsen/HistFinsen.html)

It remained for two doctors in Bergen, Norway to sequester newly diagnosed smallpox patients for two weeks in **"red rooms"** to validate Finsen's hypothesis. Every patient emerged with no fever, free of blood poisoning and scarless! A similar report of success from Gothenburg, Sweden gained Finsen an international reputation and launched his career and the field of phototherapy.

\--------------

How could light which could kill smallpox patients, cure lupus patients, they asked? Finsen persisted and arranged with the chief engineer of the electric light works for the use of a 25 amp D.C. carbon arc lamp. There, in the electric light plant, Finsen treated the lupus vulgaris of a Danish engineer named Mogensen who had been given up by countless doctors as untreatable. **For two hours every day over the course of 4 months** the treatment persisted. But at the end of that time, Mogensen emerged a cured man, causing an uproar in the medical community.

......................

### JanSz — 2017-05-16T17:02:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-214132>

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
Comments pls.
Is this about povidone or iodine?
Should we stay clear of Betadine??

Finsen had constrictive pericarditis.
I found this:

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/313149](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/313149)

An apparent **postpericardiotomy** syndrome preceded evidence of right heart failure in the other two patients. Etiologic considerations include the possibility that pericardial irrigation with povidone-iodine (Betadine) solution may have contributed to subsequent fibrosis.
\--------
povidone \[po´vĭ-dōn\]
a synthetic polymer used as a dispersing and suspending agent.

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-25T13:05:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-214640>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=214114)
>
> I just moved into a house with a burgundy bedroom. Too early to say anything definite as other factors are involved but I dream a lot more.

I was intrigued enough to buy some burgundy bedding .... I think of it as a more sedating red than bright red, so was more intuitively drawn to it .... Haven't noticed much effect myself on dreaming, so I was interested in DH's response because he reckons he doesn't dream .... It is definitely very very rare he remembers anything. I'm outside earlier in the morning but have taken to taking him in a drink later as he is waking up (opening the curtains does it if he's still dozing) and asking him about his dreams...... Night one, he woke up remembering his dream (He dreamed I was being annoying ...). Night 2, didn't remember dreaming. Night 3, remembered he had been dreaming but couldn't remember the details. Night 4 (last night), remembered his dream.

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-27T15:32:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-214845>

Night 5 and dream remembered by DH. Last night, night 6, we had a massive thunderstorm at 1 a.m. It kept DS2 awake, DH woke up and thought oh its a storm and went back to sleep, remembered his dream this morning. Me I slept right through it, and I'm usually pretty sensitive and it would wake me up, and keep me awake until the thunder had passed into the distance, even if the curtains blocked out the lightening flashes. Intrigued enough to have ordered a long, long sleeved 100% cotton kaftan in a deep red, description wine colour. Will see what my new pre-bedtime attire does.

### caroline — 2017-05-28T03:22:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-214896>

we bought some purple/eggplant coloured sheets a few months ago when we came across an organic bedding place on our travels.
I just put them on the bed 3 days ago after not using them for awhile - I am dreaming like crazy. I never connected it....... now I know better!

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-29T09:44:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-214972>

Nights 7 and 8, DH remembered his dreams. I took him a cuppa at the usual time, but he was already awake and had opened the curtains. I was outside at 5.30 cloudbathing, so I'm now waiting to see if he starts waking up more in line with the dawn light cycle. New curtains now on my list.

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-02T13:51:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-215302>

DH still remembering his dreams. Seems to be waking a bit earlier.  I've been separately trying to make a link between the colour yellow as a solar signal to the liver and had already found this picture.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The text with the picture _"Colour and brightness as indicators of time of day (A) Spectral irradiance of 'average' daylight measured when the sun is 6 deg above or below the horizon. Note that, in addition to the pronounced difference in the amount of light, there is also a substantial change in spectral composition, with reduced middle-wavelength ('green-yellow') light at negative solar angles. ..."_ (onto B etc which refer to mice). Possibly why the saturated burgundy bedding is better than the original much lighter plain green?

I remember from one of the Alexander Wunsch videos that he said Finson's red room treatment was about _subtraction_ of frequencies, which is why I was originally trying to find whether there were any subtractive differences were between sunrise and sunset, that might affect fat burning and signalling to the regenerative phase of sleep.

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-05T17:07:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-215426>

Yesterday I’d had my first meal at lunchtime and was sat in the conservatory looking out at the pouring rain. I’d been trying to understand a paper on the mammalian circadian visual network at dawn and dusk but was tying my brain up in knots because it was mostly about mice, and I was trying to think in reverse for how it would be different in a diurnal mammal, brain waves, yellow on and off switches … then I was thinking about subtractive frequencies behind Finsen’s red room therapy. I got to a point where I had to stop, I was going round and round in circles … but going outside in the pouring rain wasn’t very appealing. I don’t sleep in the day unless I am sick. If I go to bed for a nap in the afternoon it doesn’t happen, I just relax or meditate for a bit then get up. Even after hours in the sun I don’t experience the early afternoon small spike in circadian sleep pressure. But having seen DH’s response just to the burgundy bedding (dreaming every night now), I had ordered some blackout burgundy curtains, which arrived on Friday, so decided to give a red room treatment a try. Yesterday afternoon not only did I sleep, but I dreamed. I also had no interest in eating later, the one meal was enough. Almost 24 hours before eating again. I’m hoping my mitochondria are thanking me.

### Theka — 2017-06-22T17:59:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-216838>

I am interested in the responses to the various shades and tones of red. Blue-violet in burgundy versus the orange-red sunburnt coral colors. What about terracotta?

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-23T10:39:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/post-216872>

> [Theka said:](/goto/post?id=216838)
>
> I am interested in the responses to the various shades and tones of red. Blue-violet in burgundy versus the orange-red sunburnt coral colors. What about terracotta?

I wouldn't use terracotta in a bedroom. I've got very pale cream walls, and with the burgundy curtains, which hang down in front of the window frame, there's a little "bleed" of natural light underneath from the hang, but as there's no artificial light pollution I don't worry about it. In the morning the "bleed" of natural light hits the cream wall and is yellow - orange, showing the a.m. crescendo of that end of the spectrum. The colours hitting the wall get brighter as time progresses. In the evening as the yellow-orange decrescendos, the bleed heads more to the blue-violet end of the spectrum, and it gets softer and dimmer as evening progresses, until eventually it is in darkness mode. So the burgundy gives me a different morning and evening signal, which I think helps keep my circadian cycle set, which I don't think I would get from terracotta.

### Big@nt — 2017-07-11T16:24:27+0300

# Thread: Paul's journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pauls-journal.19480/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Paul Keenan — 2017-05-15T21:08:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pauls-journal.19480/post-214083>

**Family background**; no genetic illnesses we know of, parents and grand parents from Manchester, UK. Going back few centuries my fathers side is from Ireland.

I was extremely healthy as a kid and teenager up to the point of having antibiotics for acne age 16. I was never sick more than a day ever. I started with digestive issues whilst on antibiotics, but was pretty fit otherwise.

From 16-18 I had a lot of trauma;
A couple of days after my 16th B day I pulled a muscle in back, then after a few months got diagnosed with Scheuermann's disease, which is considered to be a form of juvenile osteochondrosis of the spine. It is found mostly in teenagers and presents a significantly worse deformity than postural kyphosis.

Nearly a year later whilst at college I tore my quadricep in right leg.

A few months later I has surgery for ingrowing toenail which went wrong. I developed a major infection which spread up the leg.

I had to drop out of college and was immobile. I was on lots of antibiotics and have never been same since.

The infection took months to heal and left me with some venous pooling and discoloration of foot. Nearly one year after this the toenails started growing back and causing me issues, thus I had to get same surgery done again. After this I now had problems with both feet. The surgery involved 3 injections of local anesthetic into each big toe, then a chemical agent on nail edge, plus some cutting.

The first few years after this I was lost. I had no NHS help and was eating very poorly, lots of sugar to the point my digestion was decimated. I was totally bed ridden, did not walk for about 2 1/2 years, lost all my muscle and had crippling fatigue.

The last 7 years I have been on a quest to become well again. My father bought me a laptop and I started researching. I have seen dozens of doctors, had dozens of tests and been up and down with my health and mobility. I started studying nutrition again, I put in place mitochondrial support, I had lymphatic drainage massage and started eating Organic food. In this time I have been quite mobile, and pretty bad as well. The last few months I have had constant adrenaline. My heart has been racing, my muscles are fatigue, and my breathing has been impaired.

I have been diagnosed with Venous incompetence of the superficial veins, POTS and chronic fatigue. The later two are more likely than not related to a low cardiac output state made worse when I stand causing an adrenaline surge to prevent supply demand mismatching and hypoperfusion, which in turn is linked to a low mitochondrial function state. I was doing brilliantly for a year on Idebenone, chemical analog of Co Q10, but it stopped working. Likely due to problems with NADPH meaning I can not reduce it to Idebenol.

My bedroom has been set up to be a home theater room. Very dark. I sit about 10 feet from TV. In the last 10 months I have probably got a about a dozen hours of sunlight. I normally wake up about 10. 30am. My sleep was very good using Magnesium citrate until the last few months. During that time I have needed melatonin which causes vivid dreams, causing me to awake with a racing heart 2-3 times a night. I can not tolerate the heat. I barely sweat. Heat increases my heart rate. I have had periods over the last few years where I have sweated normally and been able to tolerate the summer heat, now I am dreading going on holiday. My vitamin D3 levels are super low, as are my Iodine levels. I had both tested a few weeks back.

My diet consists of lots of nuts, some seeds, tuna, cod, broccoli, green beans, eggs and rotating meats each day including pro, chicken and beef. Organic and free range when possible. I have introduced Organic Ghee and beef fat recently for cooking. I drink mostly Highland Springs water, but recently had it tested and the levels of Barium are not acceptable. This will be coming from chemtrail spraying. I do not put on weight, and I rapidly lose muscle. My proprioception is not great and my muscles fatigue very easily.

I have been watching Dr Jacks videos for the last week now. My blue light blocking glasses came today, I have them on now.

I am determined to get well. I am 31 years old now

I know I have mitochondrial issues, I have had the testing done. I know my environment is not great. I am looking to improve things significantly.

cheers

### Sue-UK — 2017-05-16T11:21:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pauls-journal.19480/post-214111>

Hi Paul and welcome to journal land.

If you are not waking up until 10.30 you are missing valuable morning light, which as well as its circadian rhythm benefits, comes with IR. In the cool morning air it comes without the heat, so my body doesn't need to produce sweat, the surfaces stay cool anyway. In my location in the UK, at the moment sunrise to 10.30 would give me 5 hours possible light harvesting. [http://www.ukweathercams.co.uk/uk\_sunrise\_and\_sunset\_times.php?region=England](http://www.ukweathercams.co.uk/uk_sunrise_and_sunset_times.php?region=England). If my circadian rhythm sucked and I was sleeping in to 10.30, I'd set a battery alarm clock until I started waking up in line with the light cycle. If the weather is good I'm aiming for at least 2 hours outside after dawn, grounded with as much skin surface exposed as possible, but I also rain bathe, or CT in the rain, (challenge, not misery), but its that important for me to get enough early morning light. (Winter is a different protocol). I think the early morning light helps mitochondrial function and also signals the skin to prepare for any later heat and UV, so I cope with it better.

To use sunlight skin must be hardened.
For skin that is not accustomed to sunlight it takes 4 weeks of incremental exposure to sufficiently harden skin.
It is newer good to burn skin.
So, when skin sun resistance is developed, do not loose it.

Use MAXIMUM available sunlight, everyday you see sun.
Expose maximum skin surface, preferably completely naked, expose everything, genitals and anus.

At your latitude you do not have much sun.
Use Red Sperti.
Again, skin must be hardened, if it is not, increase timing slowly.
Timer on Red Sperti lasts 16 minutes.
At first get to 16 minutes once a day.
Eventually increase to two a day or even 3/day (specially in winter time).

Do not use vit D supplementation, but eat garlic, onions, leeks, carrots, peppers.
Sunlight and Red Sperti should help you raise vit D and A.
A-carotenes
D-sulfur

After 6-9 months of above get vit A & D tested.
goal
Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
more is ok too.

That will tell you how much time you have to spend with Red Sperti.
\----------
There is number of other items, but start with this
plus
tend to (rigid) Circadian Rhythm.

### JanSz — 2017-05-16T16:49:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pauls-journal.19480/post-214129>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/#post-214128](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/redecorating-my-room.19478/#post-214128)

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=214096)
>
> Red. Google Finsen and red rooms.......you'll learn why it is wise.

[http://photobiology.info/HistFinsen/HistFinsen.html](http://photobiology.info/HistFinsen/HistFinsen.html)

It remained for two doctors in Bergen, Norway to sequester newly diagnosed smallpox patients for two weeks in "red rooms" to validate Finsen's hypothesis. Every patient emerged with no fever, free of blood poisoning and scarless! A similar report of success from Gothenburg, Sweden gained Finsen an international reputation and launched his career and the field of phototherapy.

\--------------

How could light which could kill smallpox patients, cure lupus patients, they asked? Finsen persisted and arranged with the chief engineer of the electric light works for the use of a 25 amp D.C. carbon arc lamp. There, in the electric light plant, Finsen treated the lupus vulgaris of a Danish engineer named Mogensen who had been given up by countless doctors as untreatable. **For two hours every day over the course of 4 months the treatment persisted.** But at the end of that time, Mogensen emerged a cured man, causing an uproar in the medical community.

......................

# Thread: Lainee's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lainees-journal.19481/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Lainee88 — 2017-05-15T22:42:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lainees-journal.19481/post-214085>

Maternal Grandmother (English)

Born in NW Washington (1920s), raised on farm (raised cows for beef, garden). Lived in rural community on a farm entire life. I believe she was diagnosed with Parkinson’s disease in the 1990s. I don’t know of any other significant health issues.

Mother (English/German)

Born in NW Washington (1950s) raised on farm (raised cows for beef, garden). Unknown if she has health issues as she has not gone to doctor in years, depression started in 1980s after marriage separation, poor dental hygiene, balding, consumes a lot of sugar (soda/candy), hoarding. She has worked for a major retail store for 25+ years.

Father

Born in NW Washington, 1949. Recently passed away after complications from a stroke. For years he has had uncontrollable high blood pressure. He also had diabetes and early stages of dementia. He was an electrical engineer and his hobby was electronics. After learning a little about nnemf, I wonder if it played a factor in his health.

My History

Haplotype H1a
Female-Born 1970s in Albuquerque, New Mexico (Latitude 35, Elevation 5,000 ft) zip 87108

Lived in NM until 2000 (then I moved to Arizona). Played outside, swam (recreational, swimteam, or waterpolo) everyday during the summer, walked everywhere. I don’t remember getting sick or going to the doctor for anything. We ate a lot of casseroles, frozen veggies and mostly hamburger. From what I remember, when my parents separated we started eating a lot of fast food or highly processed food (i.e. Cook for yourself using the microwave-ramen noodles, canned soup, chef-boyardee, etc). I preferred carbs over meat (still do today). I also played basketball and ran track during this time frame. \[Never overweight during this timeframe, unknown blood panel results as we never went to the dr.\]

Currently live Phoenix, 85041 (Latitude 33, Elevation 1,200 ft)

From 2000-2011 worked in high stress office environment in the PHX metro area, florescent lighting, computer tower with two 15 “monitors. I ate very poorly during a majority of this time: fast food, soda, sunflower seeds and was very overweight and puffy.

2007-2010 heavy laptop and WiFi usage during a college Masters program.

In 2011 I started working in a rural community. I drive 45 minutes eachway into the sunrise and sunset. Its a Small office, low stress, have a nice big single pane window in my office, I do use one overhead fluorescent light fixture (I am working on getting different lamps/lights). Most cell phone providers don’t work at my office only Verizon. I do perform field work as a part of my job so when I’m outside I try to maximize my sun exposure in a professional manner.

In 2005 I had a kidney stone and in 2007 I was diagnosed with High Blood Pressure. I lost weight and my blood pressure normalized. However, I don’t think it ever really went away. In 2012 HBP returned, I take 25 mg metoporol now. I have asked the doctor what is causing high BP, he said most of the time it is unknown. In 2015, I was referred to a kidney specialist due to blood panel results. I did the 24 urine test, ultrasound, and Doppler radar test which did not identify the cause of HPB or reasons for the blood work results. Results are below. Every time I go to the Dr my blood pressure skyrockets and takes awhile to normalize. My latest vitamin d levels are low and Dr told me to take 1000iu a day. My renal function is low and I feel like my glucose is creeping up. I currently weight 192lbs (I'm 5'8) I probably need to lose about 20 lbs of fat. Body Composition info below.

Around 2012 I also found out that I had (or have) PCOS and some uterine fibroids. To date I have no cavities. I wore contact lenses for many years (15+ years), but stopped in 2011 and have just been wearing glasses (-4.0). I’ve never really worn sunglasses as they give me a headache. I have tried wearing the orange UVEX glasses at night, but I find they are stimulating and I can’t fall asleep after wearing them, maybe the red version would be better for me. I primarily drink RO water with trace minerals added back in, on occasion I will drink spring water. I do have coffee in the am during my drive to work, however since using the quantlet I only only drink a portion of it. I got a smart phone 2011 (currently have Samsung Galaxy S8, had s4 for 4 years), used an old school 32” tv until 2011, now have a small 20” digital TV that is 6 years old, I have used hard wired internet in my home since 2013. I do use the internet (3G/4G) on my phone quite a bit. I try to acclimate myself to the seasons (in the winter I try to not use the heater or jacket, summer I minimize the AC as much as possible).

I do strength training 3 days (mon/wed at 730 p, sat at 1030am) a week and i am going to incorporate swimming 3 times a week starting in june. The swimming would be at 515-630am at an outdoor pool. Sunrise is appx. 515am in june. I sleep very well from typically from 9 or 10p to 5-6am. I normally do not wake up during the night.

I recently purchase a red-infared light (620-830 nm) and am still learning ways to use it. I also purchased a Quantlet and use it almost everyday during my drive to work on wellness or P30. I think i have listened to most of Jack's podcasts, follow him on FB, ive read epi-paleo rx, and am currently reading the compilation of his blogs.

I'm trying to reverse low vitamin d, better cholesterol, increase kidney function and lower blood pressure. I believe my weight plays a major factor. I will go back for new blood work in 3 months.

\~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

fat% 33

### Jack Kruse — 2017-05-15T23:55:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lainees-journal.19481/post-214086>

Welcome: You said, "Around 2012 I also found out that I had (or have) PCOS and some uterine fibroids."

Answer: Pain during menses, PCOS and fibroids implies circadian mismatch to light is present in your life. Disruption of circadian rhythms is associated with disturbances in menstrual function. Female shiftworkers compared to non-shiftworkers are more likely to report menstrual irregularity and longer menstrual cycles. There also is accumulating evidence that circadian disruption increases the risk of breast cancer in women, possibly due to altered light exposure and reduced melatonin secretion. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/17383933](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/17383933)
